{{神社| 名称=神柱宮| 所在地=宮崎県都城市前田町1417-1| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|31|43|56|N|131|04|19|E|scale:30000}}| 祭神=天照皇大神
豊受姫大神
| 社格=県社・別表神社| 創建=[[万寿]]3年（[[1026年]]）| 例祭=[[10月29日]]| 神事=六月灯（夏祭）}} '''神柱宮'''（かんはしらぐう）は、[[宮崎県]][[都城市]]にある[[神社]]である。旧[[社格]]は[[県社]]で、現在は[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]。'''神柱神社'''（かんばしらじんじゃ）ともいう。通称'''お神柱'''。 [[天照大神|天照皇大神]]と[[トヨウケビメ|豊受姫大神]]を主祭神とし、五柱の神を相殿に祀る。 ==歴史== 島津荘の開拓のために[[大宰府]]より移住した[[平季基]]が、[[万寿]]3年（[[1026年]]）、天照大神の[[神託]]を受けて[[伊勢神宮]]より勧請したのに始まると伝えられる。以来、島津荘総鎮守として崇敬された。[[明治]]6年（[[1873年]]）に現在地に遷座し、県社に列した。周辺は[[神柱公園]]として整備された。旧鎮座地は市内梅北町で、その跡には黒尾神社が遷座している。 [[Category:宮崎県の神社|かんはしらくう]] [[Category:府県社|かんはしらくう]] [[Category:別表神社|かんはしらくう]] {{shinto-stub}}

{{神社| 名称=神柱宮| 所在地=宮崎県都城市前田町1417-1| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|31|43|56|N|131|04|19|E|scale:30000}}| 祭神=天照皇大神

| 社格=県社・別表神社| 創建=[[万寿]]3年（[[1026年]]）| 例祭=[[10月29日]]| 神事=六月灯（夏祭）}} '''神柱宮'''（かんばしらぐう）は、[[宮崎県]][[都城市]]にある[[神社]]である。旧[[社格]]は[[県社]]で、現在は[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]。'''神柱神社'''（かんばしらじんじゃ）ともいう。通称'''お神柱'''。 [[天照大神|天照皇大神]]と[[トヨウケビメ|豊受姫大神]]を主祭神とし、五柱の神を相殿に祀る。 == 歴史 == [[島津荘]]の開拓のために[[大宰府]]より移住した[[平季基]]が、[[万寿]]3年（[[1026年]]）、天照大神の[[神託]]を受けて[[伊勢神宮]]より勧請したのに始まると伝えられる。以来、島津荘総鎮守として崇敬された。[[明治]]6年（[[1873年]]）に現在地に遷座し、県社に列した。周辺は[[神柱公園]]として整備された。旧鎮座地は市内梅北町で、その跡には黒尾神社が遷座している。 {{DEFAULTSORT:かんはしらくう}} [[Category:宮崎県の神社]] [[Category:府県社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:都城市]] {{shinto-stub}} [[fr:Kanhashira-gū]]

{{神社 |名称 = 神柱宮 |画像 = [[ファイル:Kanbashira guu Miyakonojo Shaden 01.JPG|250px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = 宮崎県都城市前田町1417-1 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|31|43|56.6|N|131|04|19.3|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = 天照皇大神
豊受姫大神 |社格 = 旧県社・別表神社 |創建 = 伝万寿3年（1026年） |本殿 = [[流造]] |別名 = 神柱神社 |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月29日 |神事 = 六月灯（夏祭） }} '''神柱宮'''（かんばしらぐう）は、[[宮崎県]][[都城市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[旧社格]]は[[県社]]で、現在は[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]。'''神柱神社'''（かんばしらじんじゃ）ともいう。通称'''お神柱'''。 == 祭神 == [[天照大神|天照皇大神]]と[[トヨウケビメ|豊受姫大神]]を主祭神とし、左座（向かって右側）の[[天津彦火瓊瓊杵命|天津彦火瓊々杵命]]と[[天手力雄命]]、右座（同左側）の[[天児屋根命|天津児屋根命（あまつこやねのみこと）]]、[[万幡豊秋津姫命|万旗秋津姫命（よろづはたあきつひめのみこと）]]、[[天太玉命]]と、5柱の神を[[相殿]]に祀る。 == 歴史 == [[島津荘]]の開拓のために[[大宰府]]より移住した[[平季基]]が、[[万寿]]3年（[[1026年]]）、天照皇大神の[[神託]]を受けて[[伊勢神宮]]より[[勧請]]したのに始まると伝えられる。以来、島津荘総鎮守として崇敬された。 [[明治]]6年（[[1873年]]）5月に[[県社]]に列し、同年10月28日現在地に遷座した。周辺は[[神柱公園]]として整備された。旧鎮座地は市内梅北町で、その跡には[[黒尾神社 (都城市)|黒尾神社]]が遷座している。 [[戦後]]は神社本庁に参加し、[[昭和]]51年（[[1976年]]）7月1日にはその別表神社とされた。 {{DEFAULTSORT:かんはしらくう}} [[Category:宮崎県の神社]] [[Category:神明神社]] [[Category:宮崎県の旧県社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:都城市]] {{shinto-stub}} [[fr:Kanhashira-gū]]

revision.comment: ボット: 言語間リンク 1 件をウィキデータ上の ([[d:Q3192663]] に転記)
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 神柱宮 |画像 = [[ファイル:Kanbashira guu Miyakonojo Shaden 01.JPG|250px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = 宮崎県都城市前田町1417-1 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|31|43|56.6|N|131|04|19.3|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = 天照皇大神
豊受姫大神 |社格 = 旧県社・別表神社 |創建 = 伝万寿3年（1026年） |本殿 = [[流造]] |別名 = 神柱神社 |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月29日 |神事 = 六月灯（夏祭） }} '''神柱宮'''（かんばしらぐう）は、[[宮崎県]][[都城市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[旧社格]]は[[県社]]で、現在は[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]。'''神柱神社'''（かんばしらじんじゃ）ともいう。通称'''お神柱'''。 == 祭神 == [[天照大神|天照皇大神]]と[[トヨウケビメ|豊受姫大神]]を主祭神とし、左座（向かって右側）の[[天津彦火瓊瓊杵命|天津彦火瓊々杵命]]と[[天手力雄命]]、右座（同左側）の[[天児屋根命|天津児屋根命（あまつこやねのみこと）]]、[[万幡豊秋津姫命|万旗秋津姫命（よろづはたあきつひめのみこと）]]、[[天太玉命]]と、5柱の神を[[相殿]]に祀る。 == 歴史 == [[島津荘]]の開拓のために[[大宰府]]より移住した[[平季基]]が、[[万寿]]3年（[[1026年]]）、天照皇大神の[[神託]]を受けて[[伊勢神宮]]より[[勧請]]したのに始まると伝えられる。以来、島津荘総鎮守として崇敬された。 [[明治]]6年（[[1873年]]）5月に[[県社]]に列し、同年10月28日現在地に遷座した。周辺は[[神柱公園]]として整備された。旧鎮座地は市内梅北町で、その跡には[[黒尾神社 (都城市)|黒尾神社]]が遷座している。 [[戦後]]は神社本庁に参加し、[[昭和]]51年（[[1976年]]）7月1日にはその別表神社とされた。 {{DEFAULTSORT:かんはしらくう}} [[Category:宮崎県の神社]] [[Category:神明神社]] [[Category:宮崎県の旧県社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:都城市]] {{shinto-stub}}

{{神社 |名称 = 神柱宮 |画像 = [[ファイル:Kanbashira guu Miyakonojo Shaden 01.JPG|250px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = 宮崎県都城市前田町1417-1 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|31|43|56.6|N|131|04|19.3|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = 天照皇大神
豊受姫大神 |社格 = 旧県社・別表神社 |創建 = 伝万寿3年（1026年） |本殿 = [[流造]] |別名 = 神柱神社 |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月29日 |神事 = 六月灯（夏祭） }} '''神柱宮'''（かんばしらぐう）は、[[宮崎県]][[都城市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[旧社格]]は[[県社]]で、現在は[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]。'''神柱神社'''（かんばしらじんじゃ）ともいう。通称'''お神柱'''。 [[摂末社|境内末社]]として、神門神社（みかどじんじゃ）と基柱神社（もとばしらじんじゃ）の2社を擁する[http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ご祭神｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013年4月15日閲覧。。 == 祭神 == [[天照大神|天照皇大神]]と[[トヨウケビメ|豊受姫大神]]を主祭神とし、左座（向かって右側）の[[天津彦火瓊瓊杵命|天津彦火瓊々杵命]]と[[天手力雄命]]、右座（同左側）の[[天児屋根命|天津児屋根命（あまつこやねのみこと）]]、[[万幡豊秋津姫命|万旗秋津姫命（よろづはたあきつひめのみこと）]]、[[天太玉命]]と、5柱の神を[[相殿]]に祀る。また、境内末社の神門神社は[[天石門別神|櫛磐窓神と豊磐窓神]]を、基柱神社は[[菅原道真]]と[[平季基]]を祀る。 == 歴史 == [[島津荘]]の開拓のために[[大宰府]]より移住した[[平季基]]が、[[万寿]]3年（[[1026年]]）、天照皇大神の[[神託]]を受けて[[伊勢神宮]]より[[勧請]]したのに始まると伝えられる。以来、「島津荘総鎮守」として崇敬された[http://www.kanbashira.net/yuisho/index.html ご由緒｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013年4月15日閲覧。。 [[明治]]6年（[[1873年]]）5月に[[県社]]に列し、同年10月28日現在地に遷座した。周辺は[[神柱公園]]として整備された。旧鎮座地は市内梅北町で、その跡には[[黒尾神社 (都城市)|黒尾神社]]が遷座している。 [[戦後]]は神社本庁に参加し、[[昭和]]51年（[[1976年]]）7月1日にはその別表神社とされた。 == 脚注 == == 外部リンク == * [http://www.kanbashira.net/index.html 神柱宮｜宮崎県都城市の神社] {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かんはしらくう}} [[Category:宮崎県の神社]] [[Category:神明神社]] [[Category:宮崎県の旧県社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:都城市]]

{{神社 |名称 = 神柱宮 |画像 = [[ファイル:Kanbashira guu Miyakonojo Shaden 01.JPG|250px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = 宮崎県都城市前田町1417-1 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|31|43|56.6|N|131|04|19.3|E|region:JP-45_scale:25000_type:shrine|display=inline,title|name=神柱宮}} |祭神 = 天照皇大神
豊受姫大神 |社格 = 旧県社・別表神社 |創建 = 伝万寿3年（1026年） |本殿 = [[流造]] |別名 = 神柱神社 |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月29日 |神事 = 六月灯（夏祭） }} '''神柱宮'''（かんばしらぐう）は、[[宮崎県]][[都城市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[旧社格]]は[[県社]]で、現在は[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]。'''神柱神社'''（かんばしらじんじゃ）ともいう。通称'''お神柱'''。 [[摂末社|境内末社]]として、神門神社（みかどじんじゃ）と基柱神社（もとばしらじんじゃ）の2社を擁する[http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ご祭神｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013年4月15日閲覧。。 == 祭神 == [[天照大神|天照皇大神]]と[[トヨウケビメ|豊受姫大神]]を主祭神とし、左座（向かって右側）の[[天津彦火瓊瓊杵命|天津彦火瓊々杵命]]と[[天手力雄命]]、右座（同左側）の[[天児屋根命|天津児屋根命（あまつこやねのみこと）]]、[[万幡豊秋津姫命|万旗秋津姫命（よろづはたあきつひめのみこと）]]、[[天太玉命]]と、5柱の神を[[相殿]]に祀る。また、境内末社の神門神社は[[天石門別神|櫛磐窓神と豊磐窓神]]を、基柱神社は[[菅原道真]]と[[平季基]]を祀る。 == 歴史 == [[島津荘]]の開拓のために[[大宰府]]より移住した[[平季基]]が、[[万寿]]3年（[[1026年]]）、天照皇大神の[[神託]]を受けて[[伊勢神宮]]より[[勧請]]したのに始まると伝えられる。以来、「島津荘総鎮守」として崇敬された[http://www.kanbashira.net/yuisho/index.html ご由緒｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013年4月15日閲覧。。 [[明治]]6年（[[1873年]]）5月に[[県社]]に列し、同年10月28日現在地に遷座した。周辺は[[神柱公園]]として整備された。旧鎮座地は市内梅北町で、その跡には[[黒尾神社 (都城市)|黒尾神社]]が遷座している。 [[戦後]]は神社本庁に参加し、[[昭和]]51年（[[1976年]]）7月1日にはその別表神社とされた。 == 脚注 == == 外部リンク == * [http://www.kanbashira.net/index.html 神柱宮｜宮崎県都城市の神社] {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かんはしらくう}} [[Category:宮崎県の神社]] [[Category:神明神社]] [[Category:宮崎県の旧県社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:都城市]]

revision.comment: 黒尾神社のページを新規作成したため、リンクを張りました。
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 神柱宮 |画像 = [[ファイル:Kanbashira guu Miyakonojo Shaden 01.JPG|250px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = 宮崎県都城市前田町1417-1 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|31|43|56.6|N|131|04|19.3|E|region:JP-45_scale:25000_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 天照皇大神
豊受姫大神 |社格 = 旧県社・別表神社 |創建 = 伝万寿3年（1026年） |本殿 = [[流造]] |別名 = 神柱神社 |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月29日 |神事 = [[六月灯]]（夏祭） }} '''神柱宮'''（かんばしらぐう）は、[[宮崎県]][[都城市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[旧社格]]は[[県社]]で、現在は[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]。'''神柱神社'''（かんばしらじんじゃ）ともいう。通称'''お神柱'''。 [[摂末社|境内末社]]として、神門神社（みかどじんじゃ）と基柱神社（もとばしらじんじゃ）の2社を擁する[http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ご祭神｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013年4月15日閲覧。。 == 祭神 == [[天照大神|天照皇大神]]と[[トヨウケビメ|豊受姫大神]]を主祭神とし、左座（向かって右側）の[[天津彦火瓊瓊杵命|天津彦火瓊々杵命]]と[[天手力雄命]]、右座（同左側）の[[天児屋根命|天津児屋根命（あまつこやねのみこと）]]、[[万幡豊秋津姫命|万旗秋津姫命（よろづはたあきつひめのみこと）]]、[[天太玉命]]と、5柱の神を[[相殿]]に祀る。また、境内末社の神門神社は[[天石門別神|櫛磐窓神と豊磐窓神]]を、基柱神社は[[菅原道真]]と[[平季基]]を祀る。 == 歴史 == [[島津荘]]の開拓のために[[大宰府]]より移住した[[平季基]]が、[[万寿]]3年（[[1026年]]）、天照皇大神の[[神託]]を受けて[[伊勢神宮]]より[[勧請]]したのに始まると伝えられる。以来、「島津荘総鎮守」として崇敬された[http://www.kanbashira.net/yuisho/index.html ご由緒｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013年4月15日閲覧。。 [[明治]]6年（[[1873年]]）5月に[[県社]]に列し、同年10月28日現在地に遷座した。周辺は[[神柱公園]]として整備された。旧鎮座地は市内梅北町で、その跡には[[黒尾神社]]が遷座している。 [[戦後]]は神社本庁に参加し、[[昭和]]51年（[[1976年]]）7月1日にはその別表神社とされた。 == 脚注 == == 外部リンク == * [http://www.kanbashira.net/ 神柱宮｜宮崎県都城市の神社] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かんはしらくう}} [[Category:宮崎県の神社]] [[Category:神明神社]] [[Category:宮崎県の旧県社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:都城市の建築物]] [[Category:都城市の歴史]]

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 神柱宮 |画像 = [[ファイル:Kanbashira guu Miyakonojo Shaden 01.JPG|250px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = 宮崎県都城市前田町1417-1 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|31|43|56.6|N|131|04|19.3|E|region:JP-45_scale:25000_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 天照皇大神
豊受姫大神 |社格 = 旧県社・別表神社 |創建 = 伝万寿3年（1026年） |本殿 = [[流造]] |別名 = 神柱神社 |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月29日 |神事 = [[六月灯]]（夏祭） }} '''神柱宮'''（かんばしらぐう）は、[[宮崎県]][[都城市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[旧社格]]は[[県社]]で、現在は[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]。'''神柱神社'''（かんばしらじんじゃ）ともいう。通称'''お神柱'''。 [[摂末社|境内末社]]として、神門神社（みかどじんじゃ）と基柱神社（もとばしらじんじゃ）の2社を擁する[http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ご祭神｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013年4月15日閲覧。。 == 祭神 == [[天照大神|天照皇大神]]と[[トヨウケビメ|豊受姫大神]]を主祭神とし、左座（向かって右側）の[[天津彦火瓊瓊杵命|天津彦火瓊々杵命]]と[[天手力雄命]]、右座（同左側）の[[天児屋根命|天津児屋根命（あまつこやねのみこと）]]、[[万幡豊秋津姫命|万旗秋津姫命（よろづはたあきつひめのみこと）]]、[[天太玉命]]と、5柱の神を[[相殿]]に祀る。また、境内末社の神門神社は[[天石門別神|櫛磐窓神と豊磐窓神]]を、基柱神社は[[菅原道真]]と[[平季基]]を祀る。 == 歴史 == [[島津荘]]の開拓のために[[大宰府]]より移住した[[平季基]]が、[[万寿]]3年（[[1026年]]）、天照皇大神の[[神託]]を受けて[[伊勢神宮]]より[[勧請]]したのに始まると伝えられる。以来、「島津荘総鎮守」として崇敬された[http://www.kanbashira.net/yuisho/index.html ご由緒｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013年4月15日閲覧。。 [[明治]]6年（[[1873年]]）5月に[[県社]]に列し、同年10月28日現在地に遷座した。 今の願蔵寺(前田町)から現在地の神柱宮までの間はたくさんの小松が生えている野原だったので、小松原とも松原とも呼ばれたが、松原御茶屋と言われた都城島津家の別荘があった。遷座にあたり松原御茶屋周辺は[[神柱公園]]として整備された。旧鎮座地は市内梅北町益貫で、その跡には[[黒尾神社]]が遷座している。 [[戦後]]は神社本庁に参加し、[[昭和]]51年（[[1976年]]）7月1日にはその別表神社とされた。 == 脚注 == == 外部リンク == * [http://www.kanbashira.net/ 神柱宮｜宮崎県都城市の神社] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かんはしらくう}} [[Category:宮崎県の神社]] [[Category:神明神社]] [[Category:宮崎県の旧県社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:都城市の建築物]] [[Category:都城市の歴史]]

revision.comment: 神託の内容を黒尾神社の伝承に則り追記。また、当初の鎮座地を記載しました。

{{神社 |名称 = 神柱宮 |画像 = [[ファイル:Kanbashira guu Miyakonojo Shaden 01.JPG|250px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = 宮崎県都城市前田町1417-1 |位置 = |祭神 = 天照皇大神
豊受姫大神 |社格 = 旧県社・別表神社 |創建 = 伝万寿3年（1026年）9月9日 |本殿 = [[流造]] |別名 = 神柱神社 |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月29日 |神事 = [[六月灯]]（夏祭） |緯度度=31|緯度分=43|地図=Japan Miyazaki|緯度秒=57.939|経度度=131|経度分=04|経度秒=18.560}} '''神柱宮'''（かんばしらぐう）は、[[宮崎県]][[都城市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[旧社格]]は[[県社]]で、現在は[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]。'''神柱神社'''（かんばしらじんじゃ）ともいう。通称'''お神柱'''。 [[摂末社|境内末社]]として、神門神社（みかどじんじゃ）と基柱神社（もとばしらじんじゃ）の2社を擁する[http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ご祭神｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013年4月15日閲覧。。 == 祭神 == [[天照大神|天照皇大神]]と[[トヨウケビメ|豊受姫大神]]を主祭神とし、左座（向かって右側）の[[天津彦火瓊瓊杵命|天津彦火瓊々杵命]]と[[天手力雄命]]、右座（同左側）の[[天児屋根命|天津児屋根命（あまつこやねのみこと）]]、[[万幡豊秋津姫命|万旗秋津姫命（よろづはたあきつひめのみこと）]]、[[天太玉命]]と、5柱の神を[[相殿]]に祀る。また、境内末社の神門神社は[[天石門別神|櫛磐窓神と豊磐窓神]]を、基柱神社は[[菅原道真]]と[[平季基]]を祀る。 == 歴史 == [[島津荘]]の開拓のために[[大宰府]]より移住した[[平季基]]が、[[万寿]]3年（[[1026年]]）、天照皇大神の「速に斯地に社を建て祭り、神社の名を神柱と称すべし」という[[神託]]を受けて[[伊勢神宮]]より梅北村益貫に[[勧請]]したのに始まると伝えられる。以来、「島津荘総鎮守」として崇敬された[http://www.kanbashira.net/yuisho/index.html ご由緒｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013年4月15日閲覧。。 [[明治]]6年（[[1873年]]）5月に[[県社]]に列し、同年10月28日現在地に遷座した。 今の願蔵寺(前田町)から現在地の神柱宮までの間はたくさんの小松が生えている野原だったので、小松原とも松原とも呼ばれたが、松原御茶屋と言われた都城島津家の別荘があった。遷座にあたり松原御茶屋周辺は[[神柱公園]]として整備された。旧鎮座地は市内梅北町益貫で、その跡には[[黒尾神社]]が遷座している。 [[戦後]]は神社本庁に参加し、[[昭和]]51年（[[1976年]]）7月1日にはその別表神社とされた。 == 脚注 == == 外部リンク == * [http://www.kanbashira.net/ 神柱宮｜宮崎県都城市の神社] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かんはしらくう}} [[Category:宮崎県の神社]] [[Category:神明神社]] [[Category:宮崎県の旧県社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:都城市の建築物]] [[Category:都城市の歴史]]

revision.comment: 戻し：「神明神社」をまとめきれていないテンプレートであり、当該テンプレート自体も不要と判断。
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 神柱宮 |画像 = [[ファイル:Kanbashira guu Miyakonojo Shaden 01.JPG|270px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = [[宮崎県]][[都城市]]前田町1417-1 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|31|43|56.1|N|131|4|19.3|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[天照皇大神]]
[[豊受姫大神]] |社格 = 旧[[県社]]・[[別表神社]] |創建 = 伝[[万寿]]3年（[[1026年]]）9月9日 |本殿 = [[流造]] |別名 = 神柱神社 |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月29日 |神事 = [[六月灯]]（夏祭） |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=43|lat_sec=56.1|lon_deg=131|lon_min=4|lon_sec=19.3|label=神柱宮|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|31|43|56.1|N|131|4|19.3|E}}}} }} '''神柱宮'''（かんばしらぐう）は、[[宮崎県]][[都城市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[旧社格]]は[[県社]]で、現在は[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]。'''神柱神社'''（かんばしらじんじゃ）ともいう。通称'''お神柱'''。 [[摂末社|境内末社]]として、神門神社（みかどじんじゃ）と基柱神社（もとばしらじんじゃ）の2社を擁する[http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ご祭神｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013年4月15日閲覧。。 == 祭神 == [[天照大神|天照皇大神]]と[[トヨウケビメ|豊受姫大神]]を主祭神とし、左座（向かって右側）の[[天津彦火瓊瓊杵命|天津彦火瓊々杵命]]と[[天手力雄命]]、右座（同左側）の[[天児屋根命|天津児屋根命（あまつこやねのみこと）]]、[[万幡豊秋津姫命|万旗秋津姫命（よろづはたあきつひめのみこと）]]、[[天太玉命]]と、5柱の神を[[相殿]]に祀る。また、境内末社の神門神社は[[天石門別神|櫛磐窓神と豊磐窓神]]を、基柱神社は[[菅原道真]]と[[平季基]]を祀る。 == 歴史 == [[島津荘]]の開拓のために[[大宰府]]より移住した[[平季基]]が、[[万寿]]3年（[[1026年]]）、天照皇大神の「速に斯地に社を建て祭り、神社の名を神柱と称すべし」という[[神託]]を受けて[[伊勢神宮]]より梅北村益貫に[[勧請]]したのに始まると伝えられる。以来、「島津荘総鎮守」として崇敬された[http://www.kanbashira.net/yuisho/index.html ご由緒｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013年4月15日閲覧。。 [[明治]]6年（[[1873年]]）5月に[[県社]]に列し、同年10月28日現在地に遷座した。 今の願蔵寺（前田町）から現在地の神柱宮までの間はたくさんの小松が生えている野原だったので、小松原とも松原とも呼ばれたが、松原御茶屋と言われた都城島津家の別荘があった。遷座にあたり松原御茶屋周辺は[[神柱公園]]として整備された。旧鎮座地は市内[[梅北町]]益貫で、その跡には[[黒尾神社]]が遷座している。 [[戦後]]は神社本庁に参加し、[[昭和]]51年（[[1976年]]）7月1日にはその別表神社とされた。 == 脚注 == == 外部リンク == * [http://www.kanbashira.net/ 神柱宮｜宮崎県都城市の神社] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かんはしらくう}} [[Category:宮崎県の神社]] [[Category:神明神社]] [[Category:宮崎県の旧県社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:都城市の建築物]] [[Category:都城市の歴史]]

「Kanbashira_guu_Miyakonojo_Shaden_01.JPG」 を 「Kanbashira_guu_Miyakonojo_Shaden.jpg」 に差し替え（[[commons:User:CommonsDelinker|CommonsDelinker]]による。理由：[[:c:COM:FR|File renamed]]: [[:c:COM:FR#FR2|Criterion 2]] (meaningless or ambiguous name) · this is not part of

revision.comment: Created by translating the page "[[:ja:Special:Redirect/revision/99046824|神柱宮]]"
revision.text:
{{神社|名称=神柱宮|画像=[[ファイル:Kanbashira guu Miyakonojo Shaden.jpg|270px]]
拝殿|所在地=[[宮崎県]][[都城市]]前田町1417-1|位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|31|43|56.1|N|131|4|19.3|E|display=inline,title}}|祭神=[[天照皇大神]]
[[豊受姫大神]]|社格=旧[[県社]]・[[別表神社]]|創建=伝[[万寿]]3年（[[1026年]]）9月9日|本殿=[[流造]]|別名=神柱神社|札所等=|例祭=10月29日|神事=[[六月灯]]（夏祭）|地図={{Location map|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=43|lat_sec=56.1|lon_deg=131|lon_min=4|lon_sec=19.3|label=神柱宮|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|31|43|56.1|N|131|4|19.3|E}}}}}}{{infobox shinto shrine|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|image=Kanbashira guu Miyakonojo Shaden.jpg|caption=Shrine[[fr:Kanhashira-gū]][[ja:神柱宮]]}} Kanbashiragu '''Shrine''' is [[Shinto shrine|a shrine]] located in [[:ja:都城市|Miyakonojo City]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]] . [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Its former rank]] was that of [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], but it is currently [[Beppyo shrine|a special shrine]] under [[Association of Shinto Shrines|the Association of Shinto Shrines]] . It is also called '''Kanbashira Shrine''' . Commonly known as '''the sacred pillar''' . There are two sub-shrines [[:ja:摂末社|within the grounds]] : Mikado Shrine and Motobashira Shrine. [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ご祭神｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013年4月15日閲覧。 == Deity == The main deities enshrined are [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Omikami]] and [[Toyouke-hime|Toyoukehime Okami]], and five other deities [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto|are enshrined in the side shrine: Amatsuhikohokinoki no]] Mikoto and [[Ame-no-Tajikarao]] [[:ja:天児屋命|no Mikoto on the left (right side when facing the shrine)]], [[:ja:栲幡千千姫命|and Amatsukoyane no Mikoto, Yorozuhataakitsuhime no Mikoto]], and [[Futodama|Ame-no-Tatamikoto on the]] right (left side when facing [[:ja:祭神|the shrine]] ). Additionally, the Kamimon Shrine, a sub-shrine within the grounds, enshrines [[:ja:天石門別神|Kushiiwamado-no-kami and Toyoiwamado-no-kami]], while Kibohashira Shrine enshrines [[Sugawara no Michizane]] and [[:ja:平季基|Taira no Suemoto]] . [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ご祭神｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013年4月15日閲覧。 == history == It is said that [[Oracle|the shrine]] was founded [[1026|in 1026]] [[Manju (era)|when]] [[:ja:平季基|Taira no Suemoto]], who had moved from [[:ja:大宰府|Dazaifu]] to develop [[:ja:島津荘|Shimazu Manor]], received a divine revelation from Amaterasu Omikami that said, "A shrine should be quickly built in this place and the name of the shrine should be called Shinbashira," and so he enshrined a shrine in Umekitamura [[:ja:分霊|Masutsuki]] from [[Ise Grand Shrine]] . Since then, it has been revered as the guardian deity of Shimazu Manor. [http://www.kanbashira.net/yuisho/index.html ご由緒｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013年4月15日閲覧。 In [[Meiji period|May]] [[1873]], it was designated [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], and on October 28 of the same year, it was moved to its current location. The area between the current Ganzo-ji Temple (Maeda-cho) and the current location of Jinbashira Shrine was a field filled with many small pines, so it was called Komatsubara or Matsubara, and there was a villa of the Shimazu family of Miyakonojo called Matsubara Ochaya. Upon the relocation, the area around Matsubara Ochaya was developed into [[:ja:神柱公園|Shinbashira Park]] . The shrine's former location was in [[:ja:梅北町|Masutsuki, Umekita-cho]], within the city, and [[:ja:黒尾神社|Kuroo Shrine]] has been relocated to its site. After [[:ja:戦後|the war]], [[1976|it joined the Association of Shinto Shrines and was designated a special shrine on July 1, 1976]] [[Shōwa period|.]] {{draft}} {{Afc comment|used to be at [[Kanbashiragu shrine]][[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 05:53, 11 May 2023 (UTC)}} {{ill|Kanhashira-gū|ja|神柱宮|fr||simple|User:Immanuelle/Kanhashira-gū|en:draft}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web|title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー|url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/|access-date=2023-04-02|website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} == Gallery == File:Chozuya_and_Sanshuden_Hall_of_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=手水舎と参集殿|Chozuya and Sanshuden Hall File:Ginkgo_biloba_in_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=銀杏|Ginkgo biloba File:Grand_torii_of_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=大鳥居|Grand Torii File:Haiden_of_Kambashira_Shrine_closeup.jpg|alt=拝殿クローズアップ|Haiden Closeup File:Haiden_of_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=拝殿|Haiden File:Kanbashira_guu_Miyakonojo_Shaden.jpg|alt=宮之城社殿|Shaden in Miyakonojo File:Motobashira_Shrine_in_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=元柱神社|Motobashira Shrine File:Okagebashi_Bridge_on_pond_in_Kambashira_Park.jpg|alt=恩賀橋|Okagebashi Bridge File:Sando_of_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=参道|Sando File:Sanshuden_of_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=参集殿|Sanshuden File:Stela_in_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=石碑|Stela File:Stele_in_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=碑文|Stele File:TiranoSuemoto's_Stone_monument.jpg|alt=津軽末元の石碑|TiranoSuemoto's Stone Monument File:Torii_and_stairs_of_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=鳥居と階段|Torii and Stairs File:Torii_of_Kambashira_Shrine_from_inner_side.jpg|alt=内側からの鳥居|Torii from Inner Side File:Torii_of_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=鳥居|Torii File:Torpedo_in_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=魚雷|Torpedo File:基柱神社.jpg|alt=基柱神社|Kambashira Shrine File:基柱神社の扁額.jpg|alt=扁額|Shrine's Name Plaque == References == {{reflist}} == footnote == == External link == * [http://www.kanbashira.net/ Shinbashira Shrine | Shrine in Miyakonojo City, Miyazaki Prefecture] {{Draft categories|[[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Ee]][[simple:User:Immanuelle/Kanhashira-gū]] }}{{User:Immanuelle/Shinmei shrines}}{{DEFAULTSORT:かんはしらくう}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Coordinates on Wikidata]]

Upon the relocation, the area around Matsubara Ochaya was developed into [[:ja:神柱公園|Shinbashira Park]] . The shrine's former location was in [[:ja:梅北町|Masutsuki, Umekita-cho]], within the city, and [[:ja:黒尾神社|Kuroo Shrine]] has been relocated to its site. After [[:ja:戦後|the war]], [[1976|it joined the Association of Shinto Shrines and was designated a special shrine on July 1, 1976]] [[Shōwa period|.]] == Gallery == File:Chozuya_and_Sanshuden_Hall_of_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=手水舎と参集殿|Chozuya and Sanshuden Hall File:Ginkgo_biloba_in_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=銀杏|Ginkgo biloba File:Grand_torii_of_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=大鳥居|Grand Torii File:Haiden_of_Kambashira_Shrine_closeup.jpg|alt=拝殿クローズアップ|Haiden Closeup File:Haiden_of_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=拝殿|Haiden File:Kanbashira_guu_Miyakonojo_Shaden.jpg|alt=宮之城社殿|Shaden in Miyakonojo File:Motobashira_Shrine_in_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=元柱神社|Motobashira Shrine File:Okagebashi_Bridge_on_pond_in_Kambashira_Park.jpg|alt=恩賀橋|Okagebashi Bridge File:Sando_of_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=参道|Sando File:Sanshuden_of_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=参集殿|Sanshuden File:Stela_in_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=石碑|Stela File:Stele_in_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=碑文|Stele File:TiranoSuemoto's_Stone_monument.jpg|alt=津軽末元の石碑|TiranoSuemoto's Stone Monument File:Torii_and_stairs_of_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=鳥居と階段|Torii and Stairs File:Torii_of_Kambashira_Shrine_from_inner_side.jpg|alt=内側からの鳥居|Torii from Inner Side File:Torii_of_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=鳥居|Torii File:Torpedo_in_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=魚雷|Torpedo File:基柱神社.jpg|alt=基柱神社|Kambashira Shrine File:基柱神社の扁額.jpg|alt=扁額|Shrine's Name Plaque == References == {{reflist}} == footnote == == External link == * [http://www.kanbashira.net/ Shinbashira Shrine | Shrine in Miyakonojo City, Miyazaki Prefecture] {{Draft categories|[[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Ee]][[simple:User:Immanuelle/Kanhashira-gū]] }}{{User:Immanuelle/Shinmei shrines}}{{DEFAULTSORT:かんはしらくう}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Coordinates on Wikidata]]

{{infobox shinto shrine|名称=神柱宮|画像=[[ファイル:Kanbashira guu Miyakonojo Shaden.jpg|270px]]
拝殿|所在地=[[宮崎県]][[都城市]]前田町1417-1|位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|31|43|56.1|N|131|4|19.3|E|display=inline,title}}|祭神=[[天照皇大神]]
[[豊受姫大神]]|社格=旧[[県社]]・[[別表神社]]|創建=伝[[万寿]]3年（[[1026年]]）9月9日|本殿=[[流造]]|別名=神柱神社|札所等=|例祭=10月29日|神事=[[六月灯]]（夏祭）|地図={{Location map|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=43|lat_sec=56.1|lon_deg=131|lon_min=4|lon_sec=19.3|label=神柱宮|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|31|43|56.1|N|131|4|19.3|E}}}}|infobox shinto shrine|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|image=Kanbashira guu Miyakonojo Shaden.jpg|caption=Shrine[[fr:Kanhashira-gū]][[ja:神柱宮]]}}{{draft}} {{ill|Kanhashira-gū|ja|神柱宮|fr||simple|User:Immanuelle/Kanhashira-gū|en:draft}} '''Shrine''' is [[Shinto shrine|a shrine]] located in [[:ja:都城市|Miyakonojo City]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]] . [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Its former rank]] was that of [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], but it is currently [[Beppyo shrine|a special shrine]] under [[Association of Shinto Shrines|the Association of Shinto Shrines]] {{Cite web|title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー|url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/|access-date=2023-04-02|website=jinja-gosyuin.com}}. It is also called '''Kanbashira Shrine''' . Commonly known as '''the sacred pillar''' . There are two sub-shrines [[:ja:摂末社|within the grounds]] : Mikado Shrine and Motobashira Shrine. [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ご祭神｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013年4月15日閲覧。 == Deity == The main deities enshrined are [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Omikami]] and [[Toyouke-hime|Toyoukehime Okami]], and five other deities [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto|are enshrined in the side shrine: Amatsuhikohokinoki no]] Mikoto and [[Ame-no-Tajikarao]] [[:ja:天児屋命|no Mikoto on the left (right side when facing the shrine)]], [[:ja:栲幡千千姫命|and Amatsukoyane no Mikoto, Yorozuhataakitsuhime no Mikoto]], and [[Futodama|Ame-no-Tatamikoto on the]] right (left side when facing [[:ja:祭神|the shrine]] ). Additionally, the Kamimon Shrine, a sub-shrine within the grounds, enshrines [[:ja:天石門別神|Kushiiwamado-no-kami and Toyoiwamado-no-kami]], while Kibohashira Shrine enshrines [[Sugawara no Michizane]] and [[:ja:平季基|Taira no Suemoto]] . [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ご祭神｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013年4月15日閲覧。 == history == It is said that [[Oracle|the shrine]] was founded [[1026|in 1026]] [[Manju (era)|when]] [[:ja:平季基|Taira no Suemoto]], who had moved from [[:ja:大宰府|Dazaifu]] to develop [[:ja:島津荘|Shimazu Manor]], received a divine revelation from Amaterasu Omikami that said, "A shrine should be quickly built in this place and the name of the shrine should be called Shinbashira," and so he enshrined a shrine in Umekitamura [[:ja:分霊|Masutsuki]] from [[Ise Grand Shrine]] . Since then, it has been revered as the guardian deity of Shimazu Manor. [http://www.kanbashira.net/yuisho/index.html ご由緒｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013年4月15日閲覧。 In [[Meiji period|May]] [[1873]], it was designated [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], and on October 28 of the same year, it was moved to its current location. The area between the current Ganzo-ji Temple (Maeda-cho) and the current location of Jinbashira Shrine was a field filled with many small pines, so it was called Komatsubara or Matsubara, and there was a villa of the Shimazu family of Miyakonojo called Matsubara Ochaya. Upon the relocation, the area around Matsubara Ochaya was developed into [[:ja:神柱公園|Shinbashira Park]] . The shrine's former location was in [[:ja:梅北町|Masutsuki, Umekita-cho]], within the city, and [[:ja:黒尾神社|Kuroo Shrine]] has been relocated to its site. After [[:ja:戦後|the war]], [[1976|it joined the Association of Shinto Shrines and was designated a special shrine on July 1, 1976]] [[Shōwa period|.]] == Gallery == File:Chozuya_and_Sanshuden_Hall_of_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=手水舎と参集殿|Chozuya and Sanshuden Hall File:Ginkgo_biloba_in_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=銀杏|Ginkgo biloba File:Grand_torii_of_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=大鳥居|Grand Torii File:Haiden_of_Kambashira_Shrine_closeup.jpg|alt=拝殿クローズアップ|Haiden Closeup File:Haiden_of_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=拝殿|Haiden File:Kanbashira_guu_Miyakonojo_Shaden.jpg|alt=宮之城社殿|Shaden in Miyakonojo File:Motobashira_Shrine_in_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=元柱神社|Motobashira Shrine File:Okagebashi_Bridge_on_pond_in_Kambashira_Park.jpg|alt=恩賀橋|Okagebashi Bridge File:Sando_of_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=参道|Sando File:Sanshuden_of_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=参集殿|Sanshuden File:Stela_in_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=石碑|Stela File:Stele_in_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=碑文|Stele File:TiranoSuemoto's_Stone_monument.jpg|alt=津軽末元の石碑|TiranoSuemoto's Stone Monument File:Torii_and_stairs_of_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=鳥居と階段|Torii and Stairs File:Torii_of_Kambashira_Shrine_from_inner_side.jpg|alt=内側からの鳥居|Torii from Inner Side File:Torii_of_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=鳥居|Torii File:Torpedo_in_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=魚雷|Torpedo File:基柱神社.jpg|alt=基柱神社|Kambashira Shrine File:基柱神社の扁額.jpg|alt=扁額|Shrine's Name Plaque == References == {{reflist}} == footnote == == External link == * [http://www.kanbashira.net/ Shinbashira Shrine | Shrine in Miyakonojo City, Miyazaki Prefecture] {{Draft categories|[[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Ee]] [[Category:Coordinates on Wikidata]] [[simple:User:Immanuelle/Kanhashira-gū]] }}{{User:Immanuelle/Shinmei shrines}}{{DEFAULTSORT:かんはしらくう}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Coordinates on Wikidata]]

Here is the revised text with all instances of `[[:ja:` replaced with `{{ill|10=ja|11=` and the closing brackets `]]` replaced with `}}`: ```markdown {{infobox shinto shrine|名称=神柱宮|画像=[[ファイル:Kanbashira guu Miyakonojo Shaden.jpg|270px]]
拝殿|所在地=[[宮崎県]][[都城市]]前田町1417-1|位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|31|43|56.1|N|131|4|19.3|E|display=inline,title}}|祭神=[[天照皇大神]]
[[豊受姫大神]]|社格=旧[[県社]]・[[別表神社]]|創建=伝[[万寿]]3年（[[1026年]]）9月9日|本殿=[[流造]]|別名=神柱神社|札所等=|例祭=10月29日|神事=[[六月灯]]（夏祭）|地図={{Location map|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=43|lat_sec=56.1|lon_deg=131|lon_min=4|lon_sec=19.3|label=神柱宮|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|31|43|56.1|N|131|4|19.3|E}}}}|infobox shinto shrine|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|image=Kanbashira guu Miyakonojo Shaden.jpg|caption=Shrine[[fr:Kanhashira-gū]][[ja:神柱宮]]}}{{draft}} {{ill|Kanhashira-gū|ja|神柱宮|fr||simple|User:Immanuelle/Kanhashira-gū|en:draft}} '''Shrine''' is [[Shinto shrine|a shrine]] located in {{ill|10=ja|11=都城市|Miyakonojo City}}, [[Miyazaki Prefecture]] . [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Its former rank]] was that of [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], but it is currently [[Beppyo shrine|a special shrine]] under [[Association of Shinto Shrines|the Association of Shinto Shrines]] {{Cite web|title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー|url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/|access-date=2023-04-02|website=jinja-gosyuin.com}}. It is also called '''Kanbashira Shrine''' . Commonly known as '''the sacred pillar''' . There are two sub-shrines {{ill|10=ja|11=摂末社|within the grounds}} : Mikado Shrine and Motobashira Shrine. [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ご祭神｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013年4月15日閲覧。 == Deity == The main deities enshrined are [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Omikami]] and [[Toyouke-hime|Toyoukehime Okami]], and five other deities [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto|are enshrined in the side shrine: Amatsuhikohokinoki no]] Mikoto and [[Ame-no-Tajikarao]] {{ill|10=ja|11=天児屋命|no Mikoto on the left (right side when facing the shrine)]], {{ill|10=ja|11=栲幡千千姫命|and Amatsukoyane no Mikoto, Yorozuhataakitsuhime no Mikoto}}, and [[Futodama|Ame-no-T atamikoto on the]] right (left side when facing {{ill|10=ja|11=祭神|the shrine}} ). Additionally, the Kamimon Shrine, a sub-shrine within the grounds, enshrines {{ill|10=ja|11=天石門別神|Kushiiwamado-no-kami and Toyoiwamado-no-kami}}, while Kibohashira Shrine enshrines [[Sugawara no Michizane]] and {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto}} . [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ご祭神｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013年4月15日閲覧。 == history == It is said that [[Oracle|the shrine]] was founded [[1026|in 1026]] [[Manju (era)|when]] {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto}}, who had moved from {{ill|10=ja|11=大宰府|Dazaifu}} to develop {{ill|10=ja|11=島津荘|Shimazu Manor}}, received a divine revelation from Amaterasu Omikami that said, "A shrine should be quickly built in this place and the name of the shrine should be called Shinbashira," and so he enshrined a shrine in Umekitamura {{ill|10=ja|11=分霊|Masutsuki}} from [[Ise Grand Shrine]] . Since then, it has been revered as the guardian deity of Shimazu Manor. [http://www.kanbashira.net/yuisho/index.html ご由緒｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013年4月15日閲覧。 In [[Meiji period|May]] [[1873]], it was designated [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], and on October 28 of the same year, it was moved to its current location. The area between the current Ganzo-ji Temple (Maeda-cho) and the current location of Jinbashira Shrine was a field filled with many small pines, so it was called Komatsubara or Matsubara, and there was a villa of the Shimazu family of Miyakonojo called Matsubara Ochaya. Upon the relocation, the area around Matsubara Ochaya was developed into {{ill|10=ja|11=神柱公園|Shinbashira Park}} . The shrine's former location was in {{ill|10=ja|11=梅北町|Masutsuki, Umekita-cho}}, within the city, and {{ill|10=ja|11=黒尾神社|Kuroo Shrine}} has been relocated to its site. After {{ill|10=ja|11=戦後|the war}}, [[1976|it joined the Association of Shinto Shrines and was designated a special shrine on July 1, 1976]] [[Shōwa period|.]] == Gallery == {{infobox shinto shrine|名称=神柱宮|画像=[[ファイル:Kanbashira guu Miyakonojo Shaden.jpg|270px]]
拝殿|所在地=[[宮崎県]][[都城市]]前田町1417-1|位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|31|43|56.1|N|131|4|19.3|E|display=inline,title}}|祭神=[[天照皇大神]]
[[豊受姫大神]]|社格=旧[[県社]]・[[別表神社]]|創建=伝[[万寿]]3年（[[1026年]]）9月9日|本殿=[[流造]]|別名=神柱神社|札所等=|例祭=10月29日|神事=[[六月灯]]（夏祭）|地図={{Location map|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=43|lat_sec=56.1|lon_deg=131|lon_min=4|lon_sec=19.3|label=神柱宮|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|31|43|56.1|N|131|4|19.3|E}}}}|infobox shinto shrine|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|image=Kanbashira guu Miyakonojo Shaden.jpg|caption=Shrine[[fr:Kanhashira-gū]][[ja:神柱宮]]}}{{draft}} {{ill|Kanhashira-gū|ja|神柱宮|fr||simple|User:Immanuelle/Kanhashira-gū|en:draft}} '''Shrine''' is [[Shinto shrine|a shrine]] located in {{ill|10=ja|11=都城市|Miyakonojo City}}, [[Miyazaki Prefecture]] . [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Its former rank]] was that of [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], but it is currently [[Beppyo shrine|a special shrine]] under [[Association of Shinto Shrines|the Association of Shinto Shrines]] {{Cite web|title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー|url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/|access-date=2023-04-02|website=jinja-gosyuin.com}}. It is also called '''Kanbashira Shrine''' . Commonly known as '''the sacred pillar''' . There are two sub-shrines {{ill|10=ja|11=摂末社|within the grounds}} : Mikado Shrine and Motobashira Shrine. [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ご祭神｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013年4月15日閲覧。 == Deity == The main deities enshrined are [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Omikami]] and [[Toyouke-hime|Toyoukehime Okami]], and five other deities [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto|are enshrined in the side shrine: Amatsuhikohokinoki no]] Mikoto and [[Ame-no-Tajikarao]] {{ill|10=ja|11=天児屋命|no Mikoto on the left (right side when facing the shrine)}}, {{ill|10=ja|11=栲幡千千姫命|and Amatsukoyane no Mikoto, Yorozuhataakitsuhime no Mikoto}}, and [[Futodama|Ame-no-T atamikoto on the]] right (left side when facing {{ill|10=ja|11=祭神|the shrine}} ). Additionally, the Kamimon Shrine, a sub-shrine within the grounds, enshrines {{ill|10=ja|11=天石門別神|Kushiiwamado-no-kami and Toyoiwamado-no-kami}}, while Kibohashira Shrine enshrines [[Sugawara no Michizane]] and {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto}} . [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ご祭神｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013年4月15日閲覧。 == history == It is said that [[Oracle|the shrine]] was founded [[1026|in 1026]] [[Manju (era)|when]] {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto}}, who had moved from {{ill|10=ja|11=大宰府|Dazaifu}} to develop {{ill|10=ja|11=島津荘|Shimazu Manor}}, received a divine revelation from Amaterasu Omikami that said, "A shrine should be quickly built in this place and the name of the shrine should be called Shinbashira," and so he enshrined a shrine in Umekitamura {{ill|10=ja|11=分霊|Masutsuki}} from [[Ise Grand Shrine]] . Since then, it has been revered as the guardian deity of Shimazu Manor. [http://www.kanbashira.net/yuisho/index.html ご由緒｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013年4月15日閲覧。 In [[Meiji period|May]] [[1873]], it was designated [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], and on October 28 of the same year, it was moved to its current location. The area between the current Ganzo-ji Temple (Maeda-cho) and the current location of Jinbashira Shrine was a field filled with many small pines, so it was called Komatsubara or Matsubara, and there was a villa of the Shimazu family of Miyakonojo called Matsubara Ochaya. Upon the relocation, the area around Matsubara Ochaya was developed into {{ill|10=ja|11=神柱公園|Shinbashira Park}} . The shrine's former location was in {{ill|10=ja|11=梅北町|Masutsuki, Umekita-cho}}, within the city, and {{ill|10=ja|11=黒尾神社|Kuroo Shrine}} has been relocated to its site. After {{ill|10=ja|11=戦後|the war}}, [[1976|it joined the Association of Shinto Shrines and was designated a special shrine on July 1, 1976]] [[Shōwa period|.]] == Gallery == File:Grand_torii_of_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=大鳥居|Grand Torii File:Haiden_of_Kambashira_Shrine_closeup.jpg|alt=拝殿クローズアップ|Haiden Closeup File:Haiden_of_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=拝殿|Haiden File:Kanbashira_guu_Miyakonojo_Shaden.jpg|alt=宮之城社殿|Shaden in Miyakonojo File:Motobashira_Shrine_in_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=元柱神社|Motobashira Shrine File:Okagebashi_Bridge_on_pond_in_Kambashira_Park.jpg|alt=恩賀橋|Okagebashi Bridge File:Sando_of_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=参道|Sando File:Sanshuden_of_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=参集殿|Sanshuden File:Stela_in_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=石碑|Stela File:Stele_in_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=碑文|Stele File:TiranoSuemoto's_Stone_monument.jpg|alt=津軽末元の石碑|TiranoSuemoto's Stone Monument File:Torii_and_stairs_of_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=鳥居と階段|Torii and Stairs File:Torii_of_Kambashira_Shrine_from_inner_side.jpg|alt=内側からの鳥居|Torii from Inner Side File:Torii_of_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=鳥居|Torii File:Torpedo_in_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=魚雷|Torpedo File:基柱神社.jpg|alt=基柱神社|Kambashira Shrine File:基柱神社の扁額.jpg|alt=扁額|Shrine's Name Plaque == {{infobox shinto shrine|名称=神柱宮|画像=[[ファイル:Kanbashira guu Miyakonojo Shaden.jpg|270px]]
拝殿|所在地=[[宮崎県]][[都城市]]前田町1417-1|位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|31|43|56.1|N|131|4|19.3|E|display=inline,title}}|祭神=[[天照皇大神]]
[[豊受姫大神]]|社格=旧[[県社]]・[[別表神社]]|創建=伝[[万寿]]3年（[[1026年]]）9月9日|本殿=[[流造]]|別名=神柱神社|札所等=|例祭=10月29日|神事=[[六月灯]]（夏祭）|地図={{Location map|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=43|lat_sec=56.1|lon_deg=131|lon_min=4|lon_sec=19.3|label=神柱宮|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|31|43|56.1|N|131|4|19.3|E}}}}|infobox shinto shrine|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|image=Kanbashira guu Miyakonojo Shaden.jpg|caption=Shrine[[fr:Kanhashira-gū]][[ja:神柱宮]]}}{{draft}} {{ill|Kanhashira-gū|ja|神柱宮|fr||simple|User:Immanuelle/Kanhashira-gū|en:draft}} '''Shrine''' is [[Shinto shrine|a shrine]] located in {{ill|10=ja|11=都城市|Miyakonojo City}}, [[Miyazaki Prefecture]] . [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Its former rank]] was that of [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], but it is currently [[Beppyo shrine|a special shrine]] under [[Association of Shinto Shrines|the Association of Shinto Shrines]] {{Cite web|title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー|url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/|access-date=2023-04-02|website=jinja-gosyuin.com}}. It is also called '''Kanbashira Shrine''' . Commonly known as '''the sacred pillar''' . There are two sub-shrines {{ill|10=ja|11=摂末社|within the grounds}} : Mikado Shrine and Motobashira Shrine. [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ご祭神｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013年4月15日閲覧。 == Deity == The main deities enshrined are [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Omikami]] and [[Toyouke-hime|Toyoukehime Okami]], and five other deities [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto|are enshrined in the side shrine: Amatsuhikohokinoki no]] Mikoto and [[Ame-no-Tajikarao]] {{ill|10=ja|11=天児屋命|no Mikoto on the left (right side when facing the shrine)}}, {{ill|10=ja|11=栲幡千千姫命|and Amatsukoyane no Mikoto, Yorozuhataakitsuhime no Mikoto}}, and [[Futodama|Ame-no-T atamikoto on the]] right (left side when facing {{ill|10=ja|11=祭神|the shrine}} ). Additionally, the Kamimon Shrine, a sub-shrine within the grounds, enshrines {{ill|10=ja|11=天石門別神|Kushiiwamado-no-kami and Toyoiwamado-no-kami}}, while Kibohashira Shrine enshrines [[Sugawara no Michizane]] and {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto}} . [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ご祭神｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013年4月15日閲覧。 == history == It is said that [[Oracle|the shrine]] was founded [[1026|in 1026]] [[Manju (era)|when]] {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto}}, who had moved from {{ill|10=ja|11=大宰府|Dazaifu}} to develop {{ill|10=ja|11=島津荘|Shimazu Manor}}, received a divine revelation from Amaterasu Omikami that said, "A shrine should be quickly built in this place and the name of the shrine should be called Kanhashira," and so he enshrined a shrine in Umekitamura {{ill|10=ja|11=分霊|Masutsuki}} from [[Ise Grand Shrine]] . Since then, it has been revered as the guardian deity of Shimazu Manor. [http://www.kanbashira.net/yuisho/index.html ご由緒｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013年4月15日閲覧。 In [[Meiji period|May]] [[1873]], it was designated [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], and on October 28 of the same year, it was moved to its current location. The area between the current Ganzo-ji Temple (Maeda-cho) and the current location of Jinbashira Shrine was a field filled with many small pines, so it was called Komatsubara or Matsubara, and there was a villa of the Shimazu family of Miyakonojo called Matsubara Ochaya. Upon the relocation, the area around Matsubara Ochaya was developed into {{ill|10=ja|11=神柱公園|Kanhashira Park}} . The shrine's former location was in {{ill|10=ja|11=梅北町|Masutsuki, Umekita-cho}}, within the city, and {{ill|10=ja|11=黒尾神社|Kuroo Shrine}} has been relocated to its site. After {{ill|10=ja|11=戦後|the war}}, [[1976|it joined the Association of Shinto Shrines and was designated a special shrine on July 1, 1976]] [[Shōwa period|.]] == Gallery == {{infobox shinto shrine|名称=神柱宮|画像=[[ファイル:Kanbashira guu Miyakonojo Shaden.jpg|270px]]
拝殿|所在地=[[宮崎県]][[都城市]]前田町1417-1|位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|31|43|56.1|N|131|4|19.3|E|display=inline,title}}|祭神=[[天照皇大神]]
[[豊受姫大神]]|社格=旧[[県社]]・[[別表神社]]|創建=伝[[万寿]]3年（[[1026年]]）9月9日|本殿=[[流造]]|別名=神柱神社|札所等=|例祭=10月29日|神事=[[六月灯]]（夏祭）|地図={{Location map|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=43|lat_sec=56.1|lon_deg=131|lon_min=4|lon_sec=19.3|label=神柱宮|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|31|43|56.1|N|131|4|19.3|E}}}}|infobox shinto shrine|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|image=Kanbashira guu Miyakonojo Shaden.jpg|caption=Shrine[[fr:Kanhashira-gū]][[ja:神柱宮]]}}{{draft}} {{ill|Kanhashira-gū|ja|神柱宮|fr||simple|User:Immanuelle/Kanhashira-gū|en:draft}} '''Shrine''' is [[Shinto shrine|a shrine]] located in {{ill|10=ja|11=都城市|Miyakonojo City}}, [[Miyazaki Prefecture]] . [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Its former rank]] was that of [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], but it is currently [[Beppyo shrine|a special shrine]] under [[Association of Shinto Shrines|the Association of Shinto Shrines]] {{Cite web|title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー|url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/|access-date=2023-04-02|website=jinja-gosyuin.com}}. It is also called '''Kanbashira Shrine''' . Commonly known as '''the sacred pillar''' . There are two {{ill|10=ja|11=摂末社|sub-shrine|lt=sub-shrines|en|setsumatsusha}} within the grounds : Mikado Shrine and Motobashira Shrine. [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ご祭神｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013年4月15日閲覧。 == Deity == The main deities enshrined are [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Omikami]] and [[Toyouke-hime|Toyoukehime Okami]], and five other deities [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto|are enshrined in the side shrine: Amatsuhikohokinoki no]] Mikoto and [[Ame-no-Tajikarao]] no Mikoto on the left (right side when facing the shrine) and {{ill|10=ja|11=天児屋命|Ame-no-Koyane|lt=}}, {{ill|10=ja|11=栲幡千千姫命|Yorozuhataakitsuhime no Mikoto}}, and [[Futodama|Ame-no-Tatamikoto on the]] right (left side when facing {{ill|10=ja|11=祭神|the shrine}} ). Additionally, the Kamimon Shrine, a sub-shrine within the grounds, enshrines {{ill|10=ja|11=天石門別神|Kushiiwamado-no-kami and Toyoiwamado-no-kami}}, while Kibohashira Shrine enshrines [[Sugawara no Michizane]] and {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto}} . [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ご祭神｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013年4月15日閲覧。 == history == It is said that [[Oracle|the shrine]] was founded [[1026|in 1026]] [[Manju (era)|when]] {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto}}, who had moved from {{ill|10=ja|11=大宰府|Dazaifu}} to develop {{ill|10=ja|11=島津荘|Shimazu Manor}}, received a divine revelation from Amaterasu Omikami that said, "A shrine should be quickly built in this place and the name of the shrine should be called Kanhashira," and so he enshrined a shrine in Umekitamura {{ill|10=ja|11=分霊|Masutsuki}} from [[Ise Grand Shrine]] . Since then, it has been revered as the guardian deity of Shimazu Manor. [http://www.kanbashira.net/yuisho/index.html ご由緒｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013年4月15日閲覧。 In [[Meiji period|May]] [[1873]], it was designated [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], and on October 28 of the same year, it was moved to its current location. The area between the current Ganzo-ji Temple (Maeda-cho) and the current location of Jinbashira Shrine was a field filled with many small pines, so it was called Komatsubara or Matsubara, and there was a villa of the Shimazu family of Miyakonojo called Matsubara Ochaya. Upon the relocation, the area around Matsubara Ochaya was developed into {{ill|10=ja|11=神柱公園|Kanhashira Park}} . The shrine's former location was in {{ill|10=ja|11=梅北町|Masutsuki, Umekita-cho}}, within the city, and {{ill|10=ja|11=黒尾神社|Kuroo Shrine}} has been relocated to its site. After {{ill|10=ja|11=戦後|the war}}, [[1976|it joined the Association of Shinto Shrines and was designated a special shrine on July 1, 1976]] [[Shōwa period|.]] == Gallery == The shrine's former location was in {{ill|10=ja|11=梅北町|Masutsuki, Umekita-cho}}, within the city, and {{ill|10=ja|11=黒尾神社|Kuroo Shrine}} has been relocated to its site. After {{ill|10=ja|11=戦後|the war}}, [[1976|it joined the Association of Shinto Shrines and was designated a special shrine on July 1, 1976]] [[Shōwa period|.]] == Gallery == {{infobox shinto shrine|名称=神柱宮|画像=[[ファイル:Kanbashira guu Miyakonojo Shaden.jpg|270px]]
拝殿|所在地=[[宮崎県]][[都城市]]前田町1417-1|位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|31|43|56.1|N|131|4|19.3|E|display=inline,title}}|祭神=[[天照皇大神]]
[[豊受姫大神]]|社格=旧[[県社]]・[[別表神社]]|創建=伝[[万寿]]3年（[[1026年]]）9月9日|本殿=[[流造]]|別名=神柱神社|札所等=|例祭=10月29日|神事=[[六月灯]]（夏祭）|地図={{Location map|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=43|lat_sec=56.1|lon_deg=131|lon_min=4|lon_sec=19.3|label=神柱宮|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|31|43|56.1|N|131|4|19.3|E}}}}|infobox shinto shrine|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|image=Kanbashira guu Miyakonojo Shaden.jpg|caption=Shrine[[fr:Kanhashira-gū]][[ja:神柱宮]]}}{{draft}} {{ill|Kanhashira-gū|ja|神柱宮|fr||simple|User:Immanuelle/Kanhashira-gū|en:draft}} '''Shrine''' is [[Shinto shrine|a shrine]] located in {{ill|10=ja|11=都城市|Miyakonojō}}, [[Miyazaki Prefecture]] . [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Its former rank]] was that of [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], but it is currently [[Beppyo shrine|a special shrine]] under [[Association of Shinto Shrines|the Association of Shinto Shrines]] {{Cite web|title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー|url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/|access-date=2023-04-02|website=jinja-gosyuin.com}}. It is also called '''Kanbashira Shrine''' . Commonly known as '''the sacred pillar''' . There are two {{ill|10=ja|11=摂末社|sub-shrine|lt=sub-shrines|en|setsumatsusha}} within the grounds : Mikado Shrine and Motobashira Shrine. [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ご祭神｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013年4月15日閲覧。 == Deity == The main deities enshrined are [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Omikami]] and [[Toyouke-hime|Toyoukehime Okami]], and five other deities [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto|are enshrined in the side shrine: Amatsuhikohokinoki no]] Mikoto and [[Ame-no-Tajikarao]] no Mikoto on the left (right side when facing the shrine) and {{ill|10=ja|11=天児屋命|Ame-no-Koyane|lt=}}, {{ill|10=ja|11=栲幡千千姫命|Yorozuhataakitsuhime no Mikoto}}, and [[Futodama|Ame-no-Tatamikoto on the]] right (left side when facing {{ill|10=ja|11=祭神|the shrine}} ). Additionally, the Kamimon Shrine, a sub-shrine within the grounds, enshrines {{ill|10=ja|11=天石門別神|Ama no Iwatowake no kami}}, while Kibohashira Shrine enshrines [[Sugawara no Michizane]] and {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto}} . [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ご祭神｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013年4月15日閲覧。 == history == It is said that [[Oracle|the shrine]] was founded [[1026|in 1026]] [[Manju (era)|when]] {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto}}, who had moved from {{ill|10=ja|11=大宰府|Dazaifu}} to develop {{ill|10=ja|11=島津荘|Shimazu Manor}}, received a divine revelation from Amaterasu Omikami that said, "A shrine should be quickly built in this place and the name of the shrine should be called Kanhashira," and so he {{ill|10=ja|11=分霊|Bunrei|4=en}} a shrine in Umekitamura Masutsuki from [[Ise Grand Shrine]] . Since then, it has been revered as the guardian deity of Shimazu Manor. [http://www.kanbashira.net/yuisho/index.html ご由緒｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013年4月15日閲覧。 In [[Meiji period|May]] [[1873]], it was designated [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], and on October 28 of the same year, it was moved to its current location. The area between the current Ganzo-ji Temple (Maeda-cho) and the current location of Jinbashira Shrine was a field filled with many small pines, so it was called Komatsubara or Matsubara, and there was a villa of the Shimazu family of Miyakonojo called Matsubara Ochaya. Upon the relocation, the area around Matsubara Ochaya was developed into {{ill|10=ja|11=神柱公園|Kanhashira Park}} . The shrine's former location was in {{ill|10=ja|11=梅北町|Masutsuki, Umekita-cho}}, within the city, and {{ill|10=ja|11=黒尾神社|Kuroo Shrine}} has been relocated to its site. After {{ill|10=ja|11=戦後|the war}}, [[1976|it joined the Association of Shinto Shrines and was designated a special shrine on July 1, 1976]] [[Shōwa period|.]] == Gallery == [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ご祭神｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013年4月15日閲覧。 == history == It is said that [[Oracle|the shrine]] was founded [[1026|in 1026]] [[Manju (era)|when]] {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto}}, who had moved from {{ill|10=ja|11=大宰府|Dazaifu}} to develop {{ill|10=ja|11=島津荘|Shimazu Manor}}, received a divine revelation from Amaterasu Omikami that said, "A shrine should be quickly built in this place and the name of the shrine should be called Kanbashira," and so he {{ill|10=ja|11=分霊|Bunrei|lt=enshrined|4=en}} a shrine in Umekitamura Masutsuki from [[Ise Grand Shrine]] . Since then, it has been revered as the guardian deity of Shimazu Manor. [http://www.kanbashira.net/yuisho/index.html ご由緒｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013年4月15日閲覧。 In [[Meiji period|May]] [[1873]], it was designated [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], and on October 28 of the same year, it was moved to its current location. The area between the current Ganzo-ji Temple (Maeda-cho) and the current location of Jinbashira Shrine was a field filled with many small pines, so it was called Komatsubara or Matsubara, and there was a villa of the Shimazu family of Miyakonojo called Matsubara Ochaya. Upon the relocation, the area around Matsubara Ochaya was developed into {{ill|10=ja|11=神柱公園|Kanbashira Park}} . The shrine's former location was in {{ill|10=ja|11=梅北町|Masutsuki, Umekita-cho}}, within the city, and {{ill|10=ja|11=黒尾神社|Kuroo Shrine}} has been relocated to its site. After {{ill|10=ja|11=戦後|the war}}, [[1976|it joined the Association of Shinto Shrines and was designated a special shrine on July 1, 1976]] [[Shōwa period|.]] == Gallery == {{infobox shinto shrine|名称=神柱宮|画像=[[ファイル:Kanbashira guu Miyakonojo Shaden.jpg|270px]]
拝殿|所在地=[[宮崎県]][[都城市]]前田町1417-1|位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|31|43|56.1|N|131|4|19.3|E|display=inline,title}}|祭神=[[天照皇大神]]
[[豊受姫大神]]|社格=旧[[県社]]・[[別表神社]]|創建=伝[[万寿]]3年（[[1026年]]）9月9日|本殿=[[流造]]|別名=神柱神社|札所等=|例祭=10月29日|神事=[[六月灯]]（夏祭）|地図={{Location map|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=43|lat_sec=56.1|lon_deg=131|lon_min=4|lon_sec=19.3|label=神柱宮|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|31|43|56.1|N|131|4|19.3|E}}}}|infobox shinto shrine|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|image=Kanbashira guu Miyakonojo Shaden.jpg|caption=Shrine[[fr:Kanhashira-gū]][[ja:神柱宮]]}}{{draft}} {{ill|Kanhashira-gū|ja|神柱宮|fr||simple|User:Immanuelle/Kanhashira-gū|en:draft}} '''Shrine''' is [[Shinto shrine|a shrine]] located in {{ill|10=ja|11=都城市|Miyakonojō}}, [[Miyazaki Prefecture]] . [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Its former rank]] was that of [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], but it is currently [[Beppyo shrine|a special shrine]] under [[Association of Shinto Shrines|the Association of Shinto Shrines]] {{Cite web|title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー|url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/|access-date=2023-04-02|website=jinja-gosyuin.com}}. It is also called '''Kanbashira Shrine''' . Commonly known as '''the sacred pillar''' . There are two {{ill|10=ja|11=摂末社|sub-shrine|lt=sub-shrines|en|setsumatsusha}} within the grounds : {{nihongo|Mikado Shrine|神門神社|Mikado Jinja}} and {{nihongo|Motobashira Shrine|基柱神社|Motobashira Jinja}}. [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ご祭神｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013年4月15日閲覧。 == Deity == The main deities enshrined are [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Omikami]] and [[Toyouke-hime|Toyoukehime Okami]], and five other deities [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto|are enshrined in the side shrine: Amatsuhikohokinoki no]] Mikoto and [[Ame-no-Tajikarao]] no Mikoto on the left (right side when facing the shrine) and {{ill|10=ja|11=天児屋命|Ame-no-Koyane|lt=}}, {{ill|10=ja|11=栲幡千千姫命|Yorozuhataakitsuhime no Mikoto}}, and [[Futodama|Ame-no-Tatamikoto on the]] right (left side when facing {{ill|10=ja|11=祭神|the shrine}} ). Additionally, the {{nihongo|Mikado Shrine|神門神社|Mikado Jinja}}, a sub-shrine within the grounds, enshrines {{ill|10=ja|11=天石門別神|Ama no Iwatowake no kami}}, while {{nihongo|Motobashira Shrine|基柱神社|Motobashira Jinja}} enshrines [[Sugawara no Michizane]] and {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto}} . [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ご祭神｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013年4月15日閲覧。 == history == It is said that [[Oracle|the shrine]] was founded [[1026|in 1026]] [[Manju (era)|when]] {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto}}, who had moved from {{ill|10=ja|11=大宰府|Dazaifu}} to develop {{ill|10=ja|11=島津荘|Shimazu Estate|4=en}}, received a divine revelation from Amaterasu Omikami that said, "A shrine should be quickly built in this place and the name of the shrine should be called Kanbashira," and so he {{ill|10=ja|11=分霊|Bunrei|lt=enshrined|4=en}} a shrine in Umekitamura Masutsuki from [[Ise Grand Shrine]] . Since then, it has been revered as the guardian deity of Shimazu Manor. [http://www.kanbashira.net/yuisho/index.html ご由緒｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013年4月15日閲覧。 In [[Meiji period|May]] [[1873]], it was designated [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], and on October 28 of the same year, it was moved to its current location. The area between the current Ganzo-ji Temple (Maeda-cho) and the current location of Jinbashira Shrine was a field filled with many small pines, so it was called Komatsubara or Matsubara, and there was a villa of the Shimazu family of Miyakonojo called Matsubara Ochaya. Upon the relocation, the area around Matsubara Ochaya was developed into {{ill|10=ja|11=神柱公園|Kanbashira Park}} . The shrine's former location was in {{ill|10=ja|11=梅北町|Masutsuki, Umekita-cho}}, within the city, and {{ill|10=ja|11=黒尾神社|Kuroo Shrine}} has been relocated to its site. After {{ill|10=ja|11=戦後|the war}}, [[1976|it joined the Association of Shinto Shrines and was designated a special shrine on July 1, 1976]] [[Shōwa period|.]] == Gallery == [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ご祭神｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013年4月15日閲覧。 == Deity == The main deities enshrined are [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Omikami]] and [[Toyouke-hime|Toyoukehime Okami]], and five other deities [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto|are enshrined in the side shrine: Amatsuhikohokinoki no]] Mikoto and [[Ame-no-Tajikarao]] no Mikoto on the left (right side when facing the shrine) and {{ill|10=ja|11=天児屋命|Ame-no-Koyane|lt=|4=en}}, {{ill|10=ja|11=栲幡千千姫命|Takuhadachiji-hime|4=en}}, and [[Futodama|Ame-no-Tatamikoto]] on the right (left side when facing the shrine ). Additionally, the {{nihongo|Mikado Shrine|神門神社|Mikado Jinja}}, a sub-shrine within the grounds, enshrines {{ill|10=ja|11=天石門別神|Ama no Iwatowake no kami}}, while {{nihongo|Motobashira Shrine|基柱神社|Motobashira Jinja}} enshrines [[Sugawara no Michizane]] and {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto}} . [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ご祭神｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013年4月15日閲覧。 == history == It is said that [[Oracle|the shrine]] was founded [[1026|in 1026]] [[Manju (era)|when]] {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto}}, who had moved from {{ill|10=ja|11=大宰府|Dazaifu}} to develop {{ill|10=ja|11=島津荘|Shimazu Estate|4=en}}, received a divine revelation from Amaterasu Omikami that said, "A shrine should be quickly built in this place and the name of the shrine should be called Kanbashira," and so he {{ill|10=ja|11=分霊|Bunrei|lt=enshrined|4=en}} a shrine in Umekitamura Masutsuki from [[Ise Grand Shrine]] . Since then, it has been revered as the guardian deity of Shimazu Manor. [http://www.kanbashira.net/yuisho/index.html ご由緒｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013年4月15日閲覧。 In [[Meiji period|May]] [[1873]], it was designated [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], and on October 28 of the same year, it was moved to its current location. The area between the current Ganzo-ji Temple (Maeda-cho) and the current location of Jinbashira Shrine was a field filled with many small pines, so it was called Komatsubara or Matsubara, and there was a villa of the Shimazu family of Miyakonojo called Matsubara Ochaya. Upon the relocation, the area around Matsubara Ochaya was developed into {{ill|10=ja|11=神柱公園|Kanbashira Park}} . The shrine's former location was in {{ill|10=ja|11=梅北町|Masutsuki, Umekita-cho}}, within the city, and {{ill|10=ja|11=黒尾神社|Kuroo Shrine}} has been relocated to its site. After {{ill|10=ja|11=戦後|the war}}, [[1976|it joined the Association of Shinto Shrines and was designated a special shrine on July 1, 1976]] [[Shōwa period|.]] == Gallery == {{infobox shinto shrine|名称=神柱宮|画像=[[ファイル:Kanbashira guu Miyakonojo Shaden.jpg|270px]]
拝殿|所在地=[[宮崎県]][[都城市]]前田町1417-1|位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|31|43|56.1|N|131|4|19.3|E|display=inline,title}}|祭神=[[天照皇大神]]
[[豊受姫大神]]|社格=旧[[県社]]・[[別表神社]]|創建=伝[[万寿]]3年（[[1026年]]）9月9日|本殿=[[流造]]|別名=神柱神社|札所等=|例祭=10月29日|神事=[[六月灯]]（夏祭）|地図={{Location map|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=43|lat_sec=56.1|lon_deg=131|lon_min=4|lon_sec=19.3|label=神柱宮|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|31|43|56.1|N|131|4|19.3|E}}}}|infobox shinto shrine|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|image=Kanbashira guu Miyakonojo Shaden.jpg|caption=Shrine[[fr:Kanhashira-gū]][[ja:神柱宮]]}}{{draft}} {{ill|Kanhashira-gū|ja|神柱宮|fr||simple|User:Immanuelle/Kanhashira-gū|en:draft}} '''Shrine''' is [[Shinto shrine|a shrine]] located in {{ill|10=ja|11=都城市|Miyakonojō|4=en}}, [[Miyazaki Prefecture]] . [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Its former rank]] was that of [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], but it is currently [[Beppyo shrine|a special shrine]] under [[Association of Shinto Shrines|the Association of Shinto Shrines]] {{Cite web|title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー|url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/|access-date=2023-04-02|website=jinja-gosyuin.com}}. It is also called '''Kanbashira Shrine''' . Commonly known as '''the sacred pillar''' . There are two {{ill|10=ja|11=摂末社|sub-shrine|lt=sub-shrines|en|setsumatsusha}} within the grounds : {{nihongo|Mikado Shrine|神門神社|Mikado Jinja}} and {{nihongo|Motobashira Shrine|基柱神社|Motobashira Jinja}}. [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ご祭神｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013年4月15日閲覧。 == Deity == The main deities enshrined are [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Omikami]] and [[Toyouke-hime|Toyoukehime Okami]], and five other deities are enshrined in the side shrine: [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]] and [[Ame-no-Tajikarao]] no Mikoto on the left (right side when facing the shrine) and {{ill|10=ja|11=天児屋命|Ame-no-Koyane|lt=|4=en}}, {{ill|10=ja|11=栲幡千千姫命|Takuhadachiji-hime|4=en}}, and [[Futodama|Ame-no-Tatamikoto]] on the right (left side when facing the shrine ). Additionally, the {{nihongo|Mikado Shrine|神門神社|Mikado Jinja}}, a sub-shrine within the grounds, enshrines {{ill|10=ja|11=天石門別神|Ama no Iwatowake no kami}}, while {{nihongo|Motobashira Shrine|基柱神社|Motobashira Jinja}} enshrines [[Sugawara no Michizane]] and {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto}} . [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ご祭神｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013年4月15日閲覧。 == history == It is said that [[Oracle|the shrine]] was founded [[1026|in 1026]] [[Manju (era)|when]] {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto}}, who had moved from {{ill|10=ja|11=大宰府|Dazaifu}} to develop {{ill|10=ja|11=島津荘|Shimazu Estate|4=en}}, received a divine revelation from Amaterasu Omikami that said, "A shrine should be quickly built in this place and the name of the shrine should be called Kanbashira," and so he {{ill|10=ja|11=分霊|Bunrei|lt=enshrined|4=en}} a shrine in Umekitamura Masutsuki from [[Ise Grand Shrine]] . Since then, it has been revered as the guardian deity of Shimazu Manor. [http://www.kanbashira.net/yuisho/index.html ご由緒｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013年4月15日閲覧。 In [[Meiji period|May]] [[1873]], it was designated [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], and on October 28 of the same year, it was moved to its current location. The area between the current Ganzo-ji Temple (Maeda-cho) and the current location of Jinbashira Shrine was a field filled with many small pines, so it was called Komatsubara or Matsubara, and there was a villa of the Shimazu family of Miyakonojo called Matsubara Ochaya. Upon the relocation, the area around Matsubara Ochaya was developed into {{ill|10=ja|11=神柱公園|Kanbashira Park}} . The shrine's former location was in {{ill|10=ja|11=梅北町|Masutsuki, Umekita-cho}}, within the city, and {{ill|10=ja|11=黒尾神社|Kuroo Shrine}} has been relocated to its site. After the war, it joined the Association of Shinto Shrines and was designated a special shrine on July 1, 1976 [[Shōwa period|.]] == Gallery == File:Grand_torii_of_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=大鳥居|Grand Torii File:Haiden_of_Kambashira_Shrine_closeup.jpg|alt=拝殿クローズアップ|Haiden Closeup File:Haiden_of_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=拝殿|Haiden File:Kanbashira_guu_Miyakonojo_Shaden.jpg|alt=宮之城社殿|Shaden in Miyakonojo File:Motobashira_Shrine_in_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=元柱神社|Motobashira Shrine File:Okagebashi_Bridge_on_pond_in_Kambashira_Park.jpg|alt=恩賀橋|Okagebashi Bridge File:Sando_of_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=参道|Sando File:Sanshuden_of_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=参集殿|Sanshuden File:Stela_in_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=石碑|Stela File:Stele_in_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=碑文|Stele File:TiranoSuemoto's_Stone_monument.jpg|alt=津軽末元の石碑|TiranoSuemoto's Stone Monument File:Torii_and_stairs_of_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=鳥居と階段|Torii and Stairs File:Torii_of_Kambashira_Shrine_from_inner_side.jpg|alt=内側からの鳥居|Torii from Inner Side File:Torii_of_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=鳥居|Torii File:Torpedo_in_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=魚雷|Torpedo File:基柱神社.jpg|alt=基柱神社|Kambashira Shrine File:基柱神社の扁額.jpg|alt=扁額|Shrine's Name Plaque == {{infobox shinto shrine|名称=神柱宮|画像=[[ファイル:Kanbashira guu Miyakonojo Shaden.jpg|270px]]
拝殿|所在地=[[宮崎県]][[都城市]]前田町1417-1|位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|31|43|56.1|N|131|4|19.3|E|display=inline,title}}|祭神=[[天照皇大神]]
[[豊受姫大神]]|社格=旧[[県社]]・[[別表神社]]|創建=伝[[万寿]]3年（[[1026年]]）9月9日|本殿=[[流造]]|別名=神柱神社|札所等=|例祭=10月29日|神事=[[六月灯]]（夏祭）|地図={{Location map|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=43|lat_sec=56.1|lon_deg=131|lon_min=4|lon_sec=19.3|label=神柱宮|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|31|43|56.1|N|131|4|19.3|E}}}}|infobox shinto shrine|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|image=Kanbashira guu Miyakonojo Shaden.jpg|caption=Shrine[[fr:Kanhashira-gū]][[ja:神柱宮]]}}{{draft}}{{complex}} {{ill|Kanhashira-gū|ja|神柱宮|fr||simple|User:Immanuelle/Kanhashira-gū|en:draft}} '''Shrine''' is [[Shinto shrine|a shrine]] located in {{ill|10=ja|11=都城市|Miyakonojō|4=en}}, [[Miyazaki Prefecture]] . [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Its former rank]] was that of [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], but it is currently [[Beppyo shrine|a special shrine]] under [[Association of Shinto Shrines|the Association of Shinto Shrines]] {{Cite web|title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー|url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/|access-date=2023-04-02|website=jinja-gosyuin.com}}. It is also called '''Kanbashira Shrine''' . Commonly known as '''the sacred pillar''' . There are two {{ill|10=ja|11=摂末社|sub-shrine|lt=sub-shrines|en|setsumatsusha}} within the grounds : {{nihongo|Mikado Shrine|神門神社|Mikado Jinja}} and {{nihongo|Motobashira Shrine|基柱神社|Motobashira Jinja}}. [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ご祭神｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013年4月15日閲覧。 == Deity == The main deities enshrined are [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Omikami]] and [[Toyouke-hime|Toyoukehime Okami]], and five other deities are enshrined in the side shrine: [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]] and [[Ame-no-Tajikarao]] no Mikoto on the left (right side when facing the shrine) and {{ill|10=ja|11=天児屋命|Ame-no-Koyane|lt=|4=en}}, {{ill|10=ja|11=栲幡千千姫命|Takuhadachiji-hime|4=en}}, and [[Futodama|Ame-no-Tatamikoto]] on the right (left side when facing the shrine ). Additionally, the {{nihongo|Mikado Shrine|神門神社|Mikado Jinja}}, a sub-shrine within the grounds, enshrines {{ill|10=ja|11=天石門別神|Ama no Iwatowake no kami}}, while {{nihongo|Motobashira Shrine|基柱神社|Motobashira Jinja}} enshrines [[Sugawara no Michizane]] and {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto}} . [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ご祭神｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013年4月15日閲覧。 == history == It is said that [[Oracle|the shrine]] was founded [[1026|in 1026]] [[Manju (era)|when]] {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto}}, who had moved from {{ill|10=ja|11=大宰府|Dazaifu}} to develop {{ill|10=ja|11=島津荘|Shimazu Estate|4=en}}, received a divine revelation from Amaterasu Omikami that said, "A shrine should be quickly built in this place and the name of the shrine should be called Kanbashira," and so he {{ill|10=ja|11=分霊|Bunrei|lt=enshrined|4=en}} a shrine in Umekitamura Masutsuki from [[Ise Grand Shrine]] . Since then, it has been revered as the guardian deity of Shimazu Manor. [http://www.kanbashira.net/yuisho/index.html ご由緒｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013年4月15日閲覧。 In [[Meiji period|May]] [[1873]], it was designated [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], and on October 28 of the same year, it was moved to its current location. The area between the current Ganzo-ji Temple (Maeda-cho) and the current location of Jinbashira Shrine was a field filled with many small pines, so it was called Komatsubara or Matsubara, and there was a villa of the Shimazu family of Miyakonojo called Matsubara Ochaya. Upon the relocation, the area around Matsubara Ochaya was developed into {{ill|10=ja|11=神柱公園|Kanbashira Park}} . The shrine's former location was in {{ill|10=ja|11=梅北町|Masutsuki, Umekita-cho}}, within the city, and {{ill|10=ja|11=黒尾神社|Kuroo Shrine}} has been relocated to its site. After the war, it joined the Association of Shinto Shrines and was designated a special shrine on July 1, 1976 [[Shōwa period|.]] == Gallery == {{infobox shinto shrine|名称=神柱宮|画像=[[ファイル:Kanbashira guu Miyakonojo Shaden.jpg|270px]]
拝殿|所在地=[[宮崎県]][[都城市]]前田町1417-1|位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|31|43|56.1|N|131|4|19.3|E|display=inline,title}}|祭神=[[天照皇大神]]
[[豊受姫大神]]|社格=旧[[県社]]・[[別表神社]]|創建=伝[[万寿]]3年（[[1026年]]）9月9日|本殿=[[流造]]|別名=神柱神社|札所等=|例祭=10月29日|神事=[[六月灯]]（夏祭）|地図={{Location map|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=43|lat_sec=56.1|lon_deg=131|lon_min=4|lon_sec=19.3|label=神柱宮|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|31|43|56.1|N|131|4|19.3|E}}}}|infobox shinto shrine|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|image=Kanbashira guu Miyakonojo Shaden.jpg|caption=Shrine[[fr:Kanhashira-gū]][[ja:神柱宮]]}}{{draft}}{{complex}} {{ill|Kanhashira-gū|ja|神柱宮|fr||simple|User:Immanuelle/Kanhashira-gū|en:draft}} '''Shrine''' is [[Shinto shrine|a shrine]] located in {{ill|10=ja|11=都城市|Miyakonojō|4=en}}, [[Miyazaki Prefecture]] . [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Its former rank]] was that of [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], but it is currently [[Beppyo shrine|a special shrine]] under [[Association of Shinto Shrines|the Association of Shinto Shrines]] {{Cite web|title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー|url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/|access-date=2023-04-02|website=jinja-gosyuin.com}}. It is also called '''Kanbashira Shrine''' . Commonly known as '''the sacred pillar''' . There are two {{ill|10=ja|11=摂末社|sub-shrine|lt=sub-shrines|en|setsumatsusha}} within the grounds : {{nihongo|Mikado Shrine|神門神社|Mikado Jinja}} and {{nihongo|Motobashira Shrine|基柱神社|Motobashira Jinja}}. [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ご祭神｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013年4月15日閲覧。 == Deity == The main deities enshrined are [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Omikami]] and [[Toyouke-hime|Toyoukehime Okami]], and five other deities are enshrined in the side shrine: [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]] and [[Ame-no-Tajikarao]] no Mikoto on the left (right side when facing the shrine) and {{ill|10=ja|11=天児屋命|Ame-no-Koyane|lt=|4=en}}, {{ill|10=ja|11=栲幡千千姫命|Takuhadachiji-hime|4=en}}, and [[Futodama|Ame-no-Tatamikoto]] on the right (left side when facing the shrine ). Additionally, the {{nihongo|Mikado Shrine|神門神社|Mikado Jinja}}, a sub-shrine within the grounds, enshrines {{ill|10=ja|11=天石門別神|Ama no Iwatowake no kami}}, while {{nihongo|Motobashira Shrine|基柱神社|Motobashira Jinja}} enshrines [[Sugawara no Michizane]] and {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto}} . [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ご祭神｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013年4月15日閲覧。 == history == It is said that [[Oracle|the shrine]] was founded [[1026|in 1026]] [[Manju (era)|when]] {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto}}, who had moved from {{ill|10=ja|11=大宰府|Dazaifu}} to develop {{ill|10=ja|11=島津荘|Shimazu Estate|4=en}}, received a divine revelation from Amaterasu Omikami that said, "A shrine should be quickly built in this place and the name of the shrine should be called Kanbashira," and so he {{ill|10=ja|11=分霊|Bunrei|lt=enshrined|4=en}} a shrine in Umekitamura Masutsuki from [[Ise Grand Shrine]] . Since then, it has been revered as the guardian deity of Shimazu Manor. [http://www.kanbashira.net/yuisho/index.html ご由緒｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013年4月15日閲覧。 In [[Meiji period|May]] [[1873]], it was designated [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], and on October 28 of the same year, it was moved to its current location. The area between the current Ganzo-ji Temple (Maeda-cho) and the current location of Jinbashira Shrine was a field filled with many small pines, so it was called Komatsubara or Matsubara, and there was a villa of the Shimazu family of Miyakonojo called Matsubara Ochaya. Upon the relocation, the area around Matsubara Ochaya was developed into {{ill|10=ja|11=神柱公園|Kanbashira Park}} . The shrine's former location was in {{ill|10=ja|11=梅北町|Masutsuki, Umekita-cho}}, within the city, and {{ill|10=ja|11=黒尾神社|Kuroo Shrine}} has been relocated to its site. After the war, it joined the Association of Shinto Shrines and was designated a special shrine on July 1, 1976 [[Shōwa period|.]] == Gallery == {{神社 |名称 = 神柱宮 |画像 = [[ファイル:Kanbashira guu Miyakonojo Shaden.jpg|270px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = [[宮崎県]][[都城市]]前田町1417-1 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|31|43|56.1|N|131|4|19.3|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[天照皇大神]]
[[豊受姫大神]] |社格 = 旧[[県社]]・[[別表神社]] |創建 = 伝[[万寿]]3年（[[1026年]]）9月9日 |本殿 = [[流造]] |別名 = 神柱神社 |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月29日 |神事 = [[六月灯]]（夏祭） |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=43|lat_sec=56.1|lon_deg=131|lon_min=4|lon_sec=19.3|label=神柱宮|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|31|43|56.1|N|131|4|19.3|E}}}} }} '''神柱宮'''（かんばしらぐう）は、[[宮崎県]][[都城市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[旧社格]]は[[県社]]で、現在は[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]。'''神柱神社'''（かんばしらじんじゃ）ともいう。通称'''お神柱'''。 [[摂末社|境内末社]]として、神門神社（みかどじんじゃ）と基柱神社（もとばしらじんじゃ）の2社を擁する[http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ご祭神｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013年4月15日閲覧。。 == 祭神 == [[天照大神|天照皇大神]]と[[トヨウケビメ|豊受姫大神]]を主祭神とし、左座（向かって右側）の[[天津彦火瓊瓊杵命|天津彦火瓊々杵命]]と[[天手力雄命]]、右座（同左側）の[[天児屋根命|天津児屋根命（あまつこやねのみこと）]]、[[万幡豊秋津姫命|万旗秋津姫命（よろづはたあきつひめのみこと）]]、[[天太玉命]]と、5柱の神を[[相殿]]に祀る。また、境内末社の神門神社は[[天石門別神|櫛磐窓神と豊磐窓神]]を、基柱神社は[[菅原道真]]と[[平季基]]を祀る。 == 歴史 == [[島津荘]]の開拓のために[[大宰府]]より移住した[[平季基]]が、[[万寿]]3年（[[1026年]]）、天照皇大神の「速に斯地に社を建て祭り、神社の名を神柱と称すべし」という[[神託]]を受けて[[伊勢神宮]]より梅北村益貫に[[勧請]]したのに始まると伝えられる。以来、「島津荘総鎮守」として崇敬された[http://www.kanbashira.net/yuisho/index.html ご由緒｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013年4月15日閲覧。。 [[明治]]6年（[[1873年]]）5月に[[県社]]に列し、同年10月28日現在地に遷座した。 今の願蔵寺（前田町）から現在地の神柱宮までの間はたくさんの小松が生えている野原だったので、小松原とも松原とも呼ばれたが、松原御茶屋と言われた都城島津家の別荘があった。遷座にあたり松原御茶屋周辺は[[神柱公園]]として整備された。旧鎮座地は市内[[梅北町]]益貫で、その跡には[[黒尾神社]]が遷座している。 [[戦後]]は神社本庁に参加し、[[昭和]]51年（[[1976年]]）7月1日にはその別表神社とされた。 == 脚注 == == 外部リンク == * [http://www.kanbashira.net/ 神柱宮｜宮崎県都城市の神社] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かんはしらくう}} [[Category:宮崎県の神社]] [[Category:神明神社]] [[Category:宮崎県の旧県社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:都城市の建築物]] [[Category:都城市の歴史]]

{{infobox shinto shrine|名称=神柱宮|画像=[[ファイル:Kanbashira guu Miyakonojo Shaden.jpg|270px]]
拝殿|所在地=[[宮崎県]][[都城市]]前田町1417-1|位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|31|43|56.1|N|131|4|19.3|E|display=inline,title}}|祭神=[[天照皇大神]]
[[豊受姫大神]]|社格=旧[[県社]]・[[別表神社]]|創建=伝[[万寿]]3年（[[1026年]]）9月9日|本殿=[[流造]]|別名=神柱神社|札所等=|例祭=10月29日|神事=[[六月灯]]（夏祭）|地図={{Location map|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=43|lat_sec=56.1|lon_deg=131|lon_min=4|lon_sec=19.3|label=神柱宮|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|31|43|56.1|N|131|4|19.3|E}}}}|infobox shinto shrine|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|image=Kanbashira guu Miyakonojo Shaden.jpg|caption=Shrine[[fr:Kanhashira-gū]][[ja:神柱宮]]}}{{draft}}{{complex}} {{ill|Kanhashira-gū|ja|神柱宮|fr||simple|User:Immanuelle/Kanhashira-gū|en:draft}} '''Shrine''' is [[Shinto shrine|a shrine]] located in {{ill|10=ja|11=都城市|Miyakonojō|4=en}}, [[Miyazaki Prefecture]] . [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Its former rank]] was that of [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], but it is currently [[Beppyo shrine|a special shrine]] under [[Association of Shinto Shrines|the Association of Shinto Shrines]] {{Cite web|title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー|url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/|access-date=2023-04-02|website=jinja-gosyuin.com}}. It is also called '''Kanbashira Shrine''' . Commonly known as '''the sacred pillar''' . There are two {{ill|10=ja|11=摂末社|sub-shrine|lt=sub-shrines|en|setsumatsusha}} within the grounds : {{nihongo|Mikado Shrine|神門神社|Mikado Jinja}} and {{nihongo|Motobashira Shrine|基柱神社|Motobashira Jinja}}. [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ご祭神｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013年4月15日閲覧。 == Deity == The main deities enshrined are [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Omikami]] and [[Toyouke-hime|Toyoukehime Okami]], and five other deities are enshrined in the side shrine: [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]] and [[Ame-no-Tajikarao]] no Mikoto on the left (right side when facing the shrine) and {{ill|10=ja|11=天児屋命|Ame-no-Koyane|lt=|4=en}}, {{ill|10=ja|11=栲幡千千姫命|Takuhadachiji-hime|4=en}}, and [[Futodama|Ame-no-Tatamikoto]] on the right (left side when facing the shrine ). Additionally, the {{nihongo|Mikado Shrine|神門神社|Mikado Jinja}}, a sub-shrine within the grounds, enshrines {{ill|10=ja|11=天石門別神|Ama no Iwatowake no kami}}, while {{nihongo|Motobashira Shrine|基柱神社|Motobashira Jinja}} enshrines [[Sugawara no Michizane]] and {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto}} . [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ご祭神｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013年4月15日閲覧。 == history == It is said that [[Oracle|the shrine]] was founded [[1026|in 1026]] [[Manju (era)|when]] {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto}}, who had moved from {{ill|10=ja|11=大宰府|Dazaifu}} to develop {{ill|10=ja|11=島津荘|Shimazu Estate|4=en}}, received a divine revelation from Amaterasu Omikami that said, "A shrine should be quickly built in this place and the name of the shrine should be called Kanbashira," and so he {{ill|10=ja|11=分霊|Bunrei|lt=enshrined|4=en}} a shrine in Umekitamura Masutsuki from [[Ise Grand Shrine]] . Since then, it has been revered as the guardian deity of Shimazu Manor. [http://www.kanbashira.net/yuisho/index.html ご由緒｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013年4月15日閲覧。 In [[Meiji period|May]] [[1873]], it was designated [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], and on October 28 of the same year, it was moved to its current location. The area between the current Ganzo-ji Temple (Maeda-cho) and the current location of Jinbashira Shrine was a field filled with many small pines, so it was called Komatsubara or Matsubara, and there was a villa of the Shimazu family of Miyakonojo called Matsubara Ochaya. Upon the relocation, the area around Matsubara Ochaya was developed into {{ill|10=ja|11=神柱公園|Kanbashira Park}} . The shrine's former location was in {{ill|10=ja|11=梅北町|Masutsuki, Umekita-cho}}, within the city, and {{ill|10=ja|11=黒尾神社|Kuroo Shrine}} has been relocated to its site. After the war, it joined the Association of Shinto Shrines and was designated a special shrine on July 1, 1976 [[Shōwa period|.]] == Gallery == File:Okagebashi_Bridge_on_pond_in_Kambashira_Park.jpg|alt=恩賀橋|Okagebashi Bridge File:Sando_of_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=参道|Sando File:Sanshuden_of_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=参集殿|Sanshuden File:Torii_and_stairs_of_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=鳥居と階段|Torii and Stairs File:Torii_of_Kambashira_Shrine_from_inner_side.jpg|alt=内側からの鳥居|Torii from Inner Side File:Torii_of_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=鳥居|Torii File:Torpedo_in_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=魚雷|Torpedo File:基柱神社.jpg|alt=基柱神社|Kambashira Shrine File:基柱神社の扁額.jpg|alt=扁額|Shrine's Name Plaque ==
