[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{infobox shinto shrine| map_type = Japan Miyazaki Prefecture#Japan|name=神柱宮|image=Kanbashira guu Miyakonojo Shaden.jpg|location=[[宮崎県]][[都城市]]前田町1417-1|coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|31|43|56.1|N|131|4|19.3|E|display=inline,title}}|deity=[[Amaterasu]]
[[Toyouke-hime]]|Rank=旧[[県社]]・[[別表神社]]|established=1026 {{ill|September 9 (Lunisolar)|ja|9月9日 (旧暦)|12=draft}}|main hall=[[流造]]|alternative name=神柱神社|札所等=|festival={{ill|June lanterns|ja|六月灯|12=draft}}（{{ill|Summer festival|ja|夏祭り|1=Natsu Matsuri|12=draft}}）|map={{location map|Japan Miyazaki Prefecture|lat_deg=31|lat_min=43|lat_sec=56.1|lon_deg=131|lon_min=4|lon_sec=19.3|label=神柱宮|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|31|43|56.1|N|131|4|19.3|E}}}}|infobox shinto shrine|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|caption=Shrine}} {{complex}} {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮|fr||simple|User:Immanuelle/Kanbashira-gū|en:draft|12=draft}} '''Shrine''' is [[Shinto shrine|a shrine]] located in {{ill|10=ja|11=都城市|Miyakonojō|4=en|12=draft}}, [[Miyazaki Prefecture]] . [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Its former Rank]] was that of [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], but it is currently [[Beppyo shrine|a special shrine]] under [[Association of Shinto Shrines|the Association of Shinto Shrines]] {{Cite web|title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー|url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/|access-date=2023-04-02|website=jinja-gosyuin.com}}. It is also called '''Kanbashira Shrine''' . Commonly known as '''the sacred pillar''' . There are two {{ill|10=ja|11=摂末社|setsumatsusha|lt=sub-shrines|en|setsumatsusha|12=draft}} within the grounds : {{nihongo|Mikado Shrine|神門神社|Mikado Jinja}} and {{nihongo|Motobashira Shrine|基柱神社|Motobashira Jinja}}. [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ごdeity｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 == deity == The main deities enshrined are [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Omikami]] and [[Toyouke-hime|Toyoukehime Okami]], and five other deities are enshrined in the side shrine: [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]] and [[Ame-no-Tajikarao]] no Mikoto on the left (right side when facing the shrine) and {{ill|10=ja|11=天児屋命|Ame-no-Koyane|lt=|4=en|12=draft}}, {{ill|10=ja|11=栲幡千千姫命|Takuhadachiji-hime|4=en|12=draft}}, and [[Futodama|Ame-no-Tatamikoto]] on the right (left side when facing the shrine ). Additionally, the {{nihongo|Mikado Shrine|神門神社|Mikado Jinja}}, a sub-shrine within the grounds, enshrines {{ill|10=ja|11=天石門別神|Ama-no-Iwato no Kami|12=draft}}, while {{nihongo|Motobashira Shrine|基柱神社|Motobashira Jinja}} enshrines [[Sugawara no Michizane]] and {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto|12=draft}} . [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ごdeity｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 == history == It is said that [[Oracle|the shrine]] was founded [[1026|in 1026]] when {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto|12=draft}}, who had moved from {{ill|10=ja|11=大宰府|Dazaifu|12=draft}} to develop {{ill|10=ja|11=島津荘|Shimazu Estate|4=en|12=draft}}, received a divine revelation from Amaterasu Omikami that said, "A shrine should be quickly built in this place and the name of the shrine should be called Kanbashira," and so he {{ill|10=ja|11=分霊|Bunrei|lt=enshrined|4=en|12=draft}} a shrine in Umekitamura Masutsuki from [[Ise Grand Shrine]] . Since then, it has been revered as the guardian deity of Shimazu Manor. [http://www.kanbashira.net/yuisho/index.html ご由緒｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 In [[Meiji period|May]] [[1873]], it was designated [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], and on October 28 of the same year, it was moved to its current location. The area between the current Ganzo-ji Temple (Maeda-cho) and the current location of Jinbashira Shrine was a field filled with many small pines, so it was called Komatsubara or Matsubara, and there was a villa of the Shimazu family of Miyakonojo called Matsubara Ochaya. Upon the relocation, the area around Matsubara Ochaya was developed into {{ill|10=ja|11=神柱公園|Kanbashira Park|12=draft}} . The shrine's former location was in {{ill|10=ja|11=梅北町|Masutsuki, Umekita-cho|12=draft}}, within the city, and {{ill|10=ja|11=黒尾神社|Kuroo Shrine|12=draft}} has been relocated to its site. After the war, it joined the Association of Shinto Shrines and was designated a [[Beppyo Shrine]] on July 1, 1976 [[Shōwa period|.]] == Gallery == [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Its former Rank]] was that of [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], but it is currently [[Beppyo shrine|a special shrine]] under [[Association of Shinto Shrines|the Association of Shinto Shrines]] {{Cite web|title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー|url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/|access-date=2023-04-02|website=jinja-gosyuin.com}}. It is also called '''Kanbashira Shrine''' . Commonly known as '''the sacred pillar''' . There are two {{ill|10=ja|11=摂末社|setsumatsusha|lt=sub-shrines|en|setsumatsusha}} within the grounds : {{nihongo|Mikado Shrine|神門神社|Mikado Jinja}} and {{nihongo|Motobashira Shrine|基柱神社|Motobashira Jinja}}. [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ごdeity｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 == deity == The main deities enshrined are [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Omikami]] and [[Toyouke-hime|Toyoukehime Okami]], and five other deities are enshrined in the side shrine: [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]] and [[Ame-no-Tajikarao]] no Mikoto on the left (right side when facing the shrine) and {{ill|10=ja|11=天児屋命|Ame-no-Koyane|lt=|4=en}}, {{ill|10=ja|11=栲幡千千姫命|Takuhadachiji-hime|4=en}}, and [[Futodama|Ame-no-Tatamikoto]] on the right (left side when facing the shrine ). Additionally, the {{nihongo|Mikado Shrine|神門神社|Mikado Jinja}}, a sub-shrine within the grounds, enshrines {{ill|10=ja|11=天石門別神|Ama-no-Iwato no Kami}}, while {{nihongo|Motobashira Shrine|基柱神社|Motobashira Jinja}} enshrines [[Sugawara no Michizane]] and {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto}} . [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ごdeity｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 == history == It is said that [[Oracle|the shrine]] was founded [[1026|in 1026]] when {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto}}, who had moved from {{ill|10=ja|11=大宰府|Dazaifu}} to develop {{ill|10=ja|11=島津荘|Shimazu Estate|4=en}}, received a divine revelation from Amaterasu Omikami that said, "A shrine should be quickly built in this place and the name of the shrine should be called Kanbashira," and so he {{ill|10=ja|11=分霊|Bunrei|lt=enshrined|4=en}} a shrine in Umekitamura Masutsuki from [[Ise Grand Shrine]] . Since then, it has been revered as the guardian deity of Shimazu Manor. [http://www.kanbashira.net/yuisho/index.html ご由緒｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 In [[Meiji period|May]] [[1873]], it was designated [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], and on October 28 of the same year, it was moved to its current location. The area between the current Ganzo-ji Temple (Maeda-cho) and the current location of Jinbashira Shrine was a field filled with many small pines, so it was called Komatsubara or Matsubara, and there was a villa of the Shimazu family of Miyakonojo called Matsubara Ochaya. Upon the relocation, the area around Matsubara Ochaya was developed into {{ill|10=ja|11=神柱公園|Kanbashira Park}} . The shrine's former location was in {{ill|10=ja|11=梅北町|Masutsuki, Umekita-cho}}, within the city, and {{ill|10=ja|11=黒尾神社|Kuroo Shrine}} has been relocated to its site. After the war, it joined the Association of Shinto Shrines and was designated a [[Beppyo Shrine]] on July 1, 1976 [[Shōwa period|.]] == Gallery == [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ごdeity｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 == deity == The main deities enshrined are [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Omikami]] and [[Toyouke-hime|Toyoukehime Okami]], and five other deities are enshrined in the side shrine: [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]] and [[Ame-no-Tajikarao]] no Mikoto on the left (right side when facing the shrine) and {{ill|10=ja|11=天児屋命|Ame-no-Koyane|lt=|4=en}}, {{ill|10=ja|11=栲幡千千姫命|Takuhadachiji-hime|4=en}}, and [[Futodama|Ame-no-Tatamikoto]] on the right (left side when facing the shrine ). Additionally, the {{nihongo|Mikado Shrine|神門神社|Mikado Jinja}}, a sub-shrine within the grounds, enshrines {{ill|10=ja|11=天石門別神|Ama-no-Iwato no Kami}}, while {{nihongo|Motobashira Shrine|基柱神社|Motobashira Jinja}} enshrines [[Sugawara no Michizane]] and {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto}} . [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ごdeity｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 == history == It is said that [[Oracle|the shrine]] was founded [[1026|in 1026]] when {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto}}, who had moved from {{ill|10=ja|11=大宰府|Dazaifu}} to develop {{ill|10=ja|11=島津荘|Shimazu Estate|4=en}}, received a divine revelation from Amaterasu Omikami that said, "A shrine should be quickly built in this place and the name of the shrine should be called Kanbashira," and so he {{ill|10=ja|11=分霊|Bunrei|lt=enshrined|4=en}} a shrine in Umekitamura Masutsuki from [[Ise Grand Shrine]] . Since then, it has been revered as the guardian deity of Shimazu Manor. [http://www.kanbashira.net/yuisho/index.html ご由緒｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 In [[Meiji period|May]] [[1873]], it was designated [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], and on October 28 of the same year, it was moved to its current location. The area between the current Ganzo-ji Temple (Maeda-cho) and the current location of Jinbashira Shrine was a field filled with many small pines, so it was called Komatsubara or Matsubara, and there was a villa of the Shimazu family of Miyakonojo called Matsubara Ochaya. Upon the relocation, the area around Matsubara Ochaya was developed into {{ill|10=ja|11=神柱公園|Kanbashira Park}} . The shrine's former location was in {{ill|10=ja|11=梅北町|Masutsuki, Umekita-cho}}, within the city, and {{ill|10=ja|11=黒尾神社|Kuroo Shrine}} has been relocated to its site. After the war, it joined the Association of Shinto Shrines and was designated a [[Beppyo Shrine]] on July 1, 1976 [[Shōwa period|.]] == Gallery == [[Toyouke-hime]]|Rank=旧[[県社]]・[[別表神社]]|established=1026 {{ill|September 9 (Lunisolar)|ja|9月9日 (旧暦)}}|main hall=[[流造]]|alternative name=神柱神社|札所等=|festival={{ill|June lanterns|ja|六月灯}}（{{ill|Summer festival|ja|夏祭り|1=Natsu Matsuri}}）|map={{location map|Japan Miyazaki Prefecture|lat_deg=31|lat_min=43|lat_sec=56.1|lon_deg=131|lon_min=4|lon_sec=19.3|label=神柱宮|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|31|43|56.1|N|131|4|19.3|E}}}}|infobox shinto shrine|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|caption=Shrine}} {{complex}} {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮|fr||simple|User:Immanuelle/Kanbashira-gū|en:draft}} '''Shrine''' is [[Shinto shrine|a shrine]] located in {{ill|10=ja|11=都城市|Miyakonojō|4=en}}, [[Miyazaki Prefecture]] . [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Its former Rank]] was that of [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], but it is currently [[Beppyo shrine|a special shrine]] under [[Association of Shinto Shrines|the Association of Shinto Shrines]] {{Cite web|title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー|url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/|access-date=2023-04-02|website=jinja-gosyuin.com}}. It is also called '''Kanbashira Shrine''' . Commonly known as '''the sacred pillar''' . There are two {{ill|10=ja|11=摂末社|setsumatsusha|lt=sub-shrines|en|setsumatsusha}} within the grounds : {{nihongo|Mikado Shrine|神門神社|Mikado Jinja}} and {{nihongo|Motobashira Shrine|基柱神社|Motobashira Jinja}}. [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ごdeity｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 == deity == The main deities enshrined are [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Omikami]] and [[Toyouke-hime|Toyoukehime Okami]], and five other deities are enshrined in the side shrine: [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]] and [[Ame-no-Tajikarao]] no Mikoto on the left (right side when facing the shrine) and {{ill|10=ja|11=天児屋命|Ame-no-Koyane|lt=|4=en}}, {{ill|10=ja|11=栲幡千千姫命|Takuhadachiji-hime|4=en}}, and [[Futodama|Ame-no-Tatamikoto]] on the right (left side when facing the shrine ). Additionally, the {{nihongo|Mikado Shrine|神門神社|Mikado Jinja}}, a sub-shrine within the grounds, enshrines {{ill|10=ja|11=天石門別神|Ama-no-Iwato-wake no Kami}}, while {{nihongo|Motobashira Shrine|基柱神社|Motobashira Jinja}} enshrines [[Sugawara no Michizane]] and {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto}} . [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ごdeity｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 == history == It is said that [[Oracle|the shrine]] was founded [[1026|in 1026]] when {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto}}, who had moved from {{ill|10=ja|11=大宰府|Dazaifu}} to develop {{ill|10=ja|11=島津荘|Shimazu Estate|4=en}}, received a divine revelation from Amaterasu Omikami that said, "A shrine should be quickly built in this place and the name of the shrine should be called Kanbashira," and so he {{ill|10=ja|11=分霊|Bunrei|lt=enshrined|4=en}} a shrine in Umekitamura Masutsuki from [[Ise Grand Shrine]] . Since then, it has been revered as the guardian deity of Shimazu Manor. [http://www.kanbashira.net/yuisho/index.html ご由緒｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 In [[Meiji period|May]] [[1873]], it was designated [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], and on October 28 of the same year, it was moved to its current location. The area between the current Ganzo-ji Temple (Maeda-cho) and the current location of Jinbashira Shrine was a field filled with many small pines, so it was called Komatsubara or Matsubara, and there was a villa of the Shimazu family of Miyakonojo called Matsubara Ochaya. Upon the relocation, the area around Matsubara Ochaya was developed into {{ill|10=ja|11=神柱公園|Kanbashira Park}} . The shrine's former location was in {{ill|10=ja|11=梅北町|Masutsuki, Umekita-cho}}, within the city, and {{ill|10=ja|11=黒尾神社|Kuroo Shrine}} has been relocated to its site. After the war, it joined the Association of Shinto Shrines and was designated a [[Beppyo Shrine]] on July 1, 1976 [[Shōwa period|.]] == Gallery == revision.comment: 28 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:神柱宮 up to rev 102022032
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 神柱宮 |画像 = [[ファイル:Kanbashira guu Miyakonojo Shaden.jpg|270px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = [[宮崎県]][[都城市]]前田町1417-1 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|31|43|56.1|N|131|4|19.3|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[天照皇大神]]
[[豊受姫大神]] |社格 = 旧[[県社]]・[[別表神社]] |創建 = 伝[[万寿]]3年（[[1026年]]）9月9日 |本殿 = [[流造]] |別名 = 神柱神社 |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月29日 |神事 = [[六月灯]]（夏祭） |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=43|lat_sec=56.1|lon_deg=131|lon_min=4|lon_sec=19.3|label=神柱宮|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|31|43|56.1|N|131|4|19.3|E}}}} }} '''神柱宮'''（かんばしらぐう）は、[[宮崎県]][[都城市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[旧社格]]は[[県社]]で、現在は[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]。'''神柱神社'''（かんばしらじんじゃ）ともいう。通称'''お神柱'''。 [[摂末社|境内末社]]として、神門神社（みかどじんじゃ）と基柱神社（もとばしらじんじゃ）の2社を擁する[http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ご祭神｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013年4月15日閲覧。。 == 祭神 == [[天照大神|天照皇大神]]と[[トヨウケビメ|豊受姫大神]]を主祭神とし、左座（向かって右側）の[[天津彦火瓊瓊杵命|天津彦火瓊々杵命]]と[[天手力雄命]]、右座（同左側）の[[天児屋根命|天津児屋根命（あまつこやねのみこと）]]、[[万幡豊秋津姫命|万旗秋津姫命（よろづはたあきつひめのみこと）]]、[[天太玉命]]と、5柱の神を[[相殿]]に祀る。また、境内末社の神門神社は[[天石門別神|櫛磐窓神と豊磐窓神]]を、基柱神社は[[菅原道真]]と[[平季基]]を祀る。 == 歴史 == [[島津荘]]の開拓のために[[大宰府]]より移住した[[平季基]]が、[[万寿]]3年（[[1026年]]）、天照皇大神の「速に斯地に社を建て祭り、神社の名を神柱と称すべし」という[[神託]]を受けて[[伊勢神宮]]より梅北村益貫に[[勧請]]したのに始まると伝えられる。以来、「島津荘総鎮守」として崇敬された[http://www.kanbashira.net/yuisho/index.html ご由緒｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013年4月15日閲覧。。 [[明治]]6年（[[1873年]]）5月に[[県社]]に列し、同年10月28日現在地に遷座した。 今の願蔵寺（前田町）から現在地の神柱宮までの間はたくさんの小松が生えている野原だったので、小松原とも松原とも呼ばれたが、松原御茶屋と言われた都城島津家の別荘があった。遷座にあたり松原御茶屋周辺は[[神柱公園]]として整備された。旧鎮座地は市内[[梅北町]]益貫で、その跡には[[黒尾神社]]が遷座している。 [[戦後]]は神社本庁に参加し、[[昭和]]51年（[[1976年]]）7月1日にはその別表神社とされた。 == 脚注 == == 外部リンク == * [http://www.kanbashira.net/ 神柱宮｜宮崎県都城市の神社] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かんはしらくう}} [[Category:宮崎県の神社]] [[Category:神明神社]] [[Category:宮崎県の旧県社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:都城市の建築物]] [[Category:都城市の歴史]]

It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Kanbashira-gū|Kanbashira-gū]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Kanbashira-gū]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Kanbashira-gū|Kanbashira-gū]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Kanbashira-gū]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft}} [[Category:Completion tier 7]] {{infobox shinto shrine| map_type = Japan Miyazaki Prefecture#Japan|name=神柱宮|image=Kanbashira guu Miyakonojo Shaden.jpg|location=[[宮崎県]][[都城市]]前田町1417-1|coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|31|43|56.1|N|131|4|19.3|E|display=inline,title}}|deity=[[Amaterasu]]
[[Toyouke-hime]]|Rank=旧[[県社]]・[[別表神社]]|established=1026 {{ill|September 9 (Lunisolar)|ja|9月9日 (旧暦)}}|main hall=[[流造]]|alternative name=神柱神社|札所等=|festival={{ill|June lanterns|ja|六月灯}}（{{ill|Summer festival|ja|夏祭り|1=Natsu Matsuri}}）|map={{location map|Japan Miyazaki Prefecture|lat_deg=31|lat_min=43|lat_sec=56.1|lon_deg=131|lon_min=4|lon_sec=19.3|label=神柱宮|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|31|43|56.1|N|131|4|19.3|E}}}}|infobox shinto shrine|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|caption=Shrine}} {{complex}} {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮|fr||simple|User:Immanuelle/Kanbashira-gū|en:draft}} '''Shrine''' is [[Shinto shrine|a shrine]] located in {{ill|10=ja|11=都城市|Miyakonojō|4=en}}, [[Miyazaki Prefecture]] . [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Its former Rank]] was that of [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], but it is currently [[Beppyo shrine|a special shrine]] under [[Association of Shinto Shrines|the Association of Shinto Shrines]] {{Cite web|title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー|url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/|access-date=2023-04-02|website=jinja-gosyuin.com}}. It is also called '''Kanbashira Shrine''' . Commonly known as '''the sacred pillar''' . There are two {{ill|10=ja|11=摂末社|setsumatsusha|lt=sub-shrines|en|setsumatsusha}} within the grounds : {{nihongo|Mikado Shrine|神門神社|Mikado Jinja}} and {{nihongo|Motobashira Shrine|基柱神社|Motobashira Jinja}}. [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ごdeity｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 == deity == The main deities enshrined are [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Omikami]] and [[Toyouke-hime|Toyoukehime Okami]], and five other deities are enshrined in the side shrine: [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]] and [[Ame-no-Tajikarao]] no Mikoto on the left (right side when facing the shrine) and {{ill|10=ja|11=天児屋命|Ame-no-Koyane|lt=|4=en}}, {{ill|10=ja|11=栲幡千千姫命|Takuhadachiji-hime|4=en}}, and [[Futodama|Ame-no-Tatamikoto]] on the right (left side when facing the shrine ). Additionally, the {{nihongo|Mikado Shrine|神門神社|Mikado Jinja}}, a sub-shrine within the grounds, enshrines {{ill|10=ja|11=天石門別神|Ama-no-Iwato-wake no Kami}}, while {{nihongo|Motobashira Shrine|基柱神社|Motobashira Jinja}} enshrines [[Sugawara no Michizane]] and {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto}} . [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ごdeity｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 == history == It is said that [[Oracle|the shrine]] was founded [[1026|in 1026]] when {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto}}, who had moved from {{ill|10=ja|11=大宰府|Dazaifu}} to develop {{ill|10=ja|11=島津荘|Shimazu Estate|4=en}}, received a divine revelation from Amaterasu Omikami that said, "A shrine should be quickly built in this place and the name of the shrine should be called Kanbashira," and so he {{ill|10=ja|11=分霊|Bunrei|lt=enshrined|4=en}} a shrine in Umekitamura Masutsuki from [[Ise Grand Shrine]] . Since then, it has been revered as the guardian deity of Shimazu Manor. [http://www.kanbashira.net/yuisho/index.html ご由緒｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 In [[Meiji period|May]] [[1873]], it was designated [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], and on October 28 of the same year, it was moved to its current location. The area between the current Ganzo-ji Temple (Maeda-cho) and the current location of Jinbashira Shrine was a field filled with many small pines, so it was called Komatsubara or Matsubara, and there was a villa of the Shimazu family of Miyakonojo called Matsubara Ochaya. Upon the relocation, the area around Matsubara Ochaya was developed into {{ill|10=ja|11=神柱公園|Kanbashira Park}} . The shrine's former location was in {{ill|10=ja|11=梅北町|Masutsuki, Umekita-cho}}, within the city, and {{ill|10=ja|11=黒尾神社|Kuroo Shrine}} has been relocated to its site. After the war, it joined the Association of Shinto Shrines and was designated a [[Beppyo Shrine]] on July 1, 1976 [[Shōwa period|.]] == Gallery == [[Toyouke-hime]]|Rank=旧[[県社]]・[[別表神社]]|established=1026 {{ill|September 9 (Lunisolar)|ja|9月9日 (旧暦)|12=draft}}|main hall=[[流造]]|alternative name=神柱神社|札所等=|festival={{ill|June lanterns|ja|六月灯|12=draft}}（{{ill|Summer festival|ja|夏祭り|1=Natsu Matsuri|12=draft}}）|map={{location map|Japan Miyazaki Prefecture|lat_deg=31|lat_min=43|lat_sec=56.1|lon_deg=131|lon_min=4|lon_sec=19.3|label=神柱宮|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|31|43|56.1|N|131|4|19.3|E}}}}|infobox shinto shrine|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|caption=Shrine}} {{complex}} {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮|fr||simple|User:Immanuelle/Kanbashira-gū|en:draft|12=draft}} '''Shrine''' is [[Shinto shrine|a shrine]] located in {{ill|10=ja|11=都城市|Miyakonojō|4=en|12=draft}}, [[Miyazaki Prefecture]] . [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Its former Rank]] was that of [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], but it is currently [[Beppyo shrine|a special shrine]] under [[Association of Shinto Shrines|the Association of Shinto Shrines]] {{Cite web|title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー|url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/|access-date=2023-04-02|website=jinja-gosyuin.com}}. It is also called '''Kanbashira Shrine''' . Commonly known as '''the sacred pillar''' . There are two {{ill|10=ja|11=摂末社|setsumatsusha|lt=sub-shrines|en|setsumatsusha|12=draft}} within the grounds : {{nihongo|Mikado Shrine|神門神社|Mikado Jinja}} and {{nihongo|Motobashira Shrine|基柱神社|Motobashira Jinja}}. [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ごdeity｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 == deity == The main deities enshrined are [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Omikami]] and [[Toyouke-hime|Toyoukehime Okami]], and five other deities are enshrined in the side shrine: [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]] and [[Ame-no-Tajikarao]] no Mikoto on the left (right side when facing the shrine) and {{ill|10=ja|11=天児屋命|Ame-no-Koyane|lt=|4=en|12=draft}}, {{ill|10=ja|11=栲幡千千姫命|Takuhadachiji-hime|4=en|12=draft}}, and [[Futodama|Ame-no-Tatamikoto]] on the right (left side when facing the shrine ). Additionally, the {{nihongo|Mikado Shrine|神門神社|Mikado Jinja}}, a sub-shrine within the grounds, enshrines {{ill|10=ja|11=天石門別神|Ama-no-Iwato-wake no Kami|12=draft}}, while {{nihongo|Motobashira Shrine|基柱神社|Motobashira Jinja}} enshrines [[Sugawara no Michizane]] and {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto|12=draft}} . [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ごdeity｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 == history == It is said that [[Oracle|the shrine]] was founded [[1026|in 1026]] when {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto|12=draft}}, who had moved from {{ill|10=ja|11=大宰府|Dazaifu|12=draft}} to develop {{ill|10=ja|11=島津荘|Shimazu Estate|4=en|12=draft}}, received a divine revelation from Amaterasu Omikami that said, "A shrine should be quickly built in this place and the name of the shrine should be called Kanbashira," and so he {{ill|10=ja|11=分霊|Bunrei|lt=enshrined|4=en|12=draft}} a shrine in Umekitamura Masutsuki from [[Ise Grand Shrine]] . Since then, it has been revered as the guardian deity of Shimazu Manor. [http://www.kanbashira.net/yuisho/index.html ご由緒｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 In [[Meiji period|May]] [[1873]], it was designated [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], and on October 28 of the same year, it was moved to its current location. The area between the current Ganzo-ji Temple (Maeda-cho) and the current location of Jinbashira Shrine was a field filled with many small pines, so it was called Komatsubara or Matsubara, and there was a villa of the Shimazu family of Miyakonojo called Matsubara Ochaya. Upon the relocation, the area around Matsubara Ochaya was developed into {{ill|10=ja|11=神柱公園|Kanbashira Park|12=draft}} . The shrine's former location was in {{ill|10=ja|11=梅北町|Masutsuki, Umekita-cho|12=draft}}, within the city, and {{ill|10=ja|11=黒尾神社|Kuroo Shrine|12=draft}} has been relocated to its site. After the war, it joined the Association of Shinto Shrines and was designated a [[Beppyo Shrine]] on July 1, 1976 [[Shōwa period|.]] == Gallery == Additionally, the {{nihongo|Mikado Shrine|神門神社|Mikado Jinja}}, a sub-shrine within the grounds, enshrines {{ill|10=ja|11=天石門別神|Ama-no-Iwato-wake no Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}, while {{nihongo|Motobashira Shrine|基柱神社|Motobashira Jinja}} enshrines [[Sugawara no Michizane]] and {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto|12=draft|12=draft}} . [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ごdeity｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 == history == It is said that [[Oracle|the shrine]] was founded [[1026|in 1026]] when {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto|12=draft|12=draft}}, who had moved from {{ill|10=ja|11=大宰府|Dazaifu|12=draft|12=draft}} to develop {{ill|10=ja|11=島津荘|Shimazu Estate|4=en|12=draft|12=draft}}, received a divine revelation from Amaterasu Omikami that said, "A shrine should be quickly built in this place and the name of the shrine should be called Kanbashira," and so he {{ill|10=ja|11=分霊|Bunrei|lt=enshrined|4=en|12=draft|12=draft}} a shrine in Umekitamura Masutsuki from [[Ise Grand Shrine]] . Since then, it has been revered as the guardian deity of Shimazu Manor. [http://www.kanbashira.net/yuisho/index.html ご由緒｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 In [[Meiji period|May]] [[1873]], it was designated [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], and on October 28 of the same year, it was moved to its current location. The area between the current Ganzo-ji Temple (Maeda-cho) and the current location of Jinbashira Shrine was a field filled with many small pines, so it was called Komatsubara or Matsubara, and there was a villa of the Shimazu family of Miyakonojo called Matsubara Ochaya. Upon the relocation, the area around Matsubara Ochaya was developed into {{ill|10=ja|11=神柱公園|Kanbashira Park|12=draft|12=draft}} . The shrine's former location was in {{ill|10=ja|11=梅北町|Masutsuki, Umekita-cho|12=draft|12=draft}}, within the city, and {{ill|10=ja|11=黒尾神社|Kuroo Shrine|12=draft|12=draft}} has been relocated to its site. After the war, it joined the Association of Shinto Shrines and was designated a [[Beppyo Shrine]] on July 1, 1976 [[Shōwa period|.]] == Gallery == [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ごdeity｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 == history == It is said that [[Oracle|the shrine]] was founded [[1026|in 1026]] when {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who had moved from {{ill|10=ja|11=大宰府|Dazaifu|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to develop {{ill|10=ja|11=島津荘|Shimazu Estate|4=en|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, received a divine revelation from Amaterasu Omikami that said, "A shrine should be quickly built in this place and the name of the shrine should be called Kanbashira," and so he {{ill|10=ja|11=分霊|Bunrei|lt=enshrined|4=en|qq=draft|qq=draft}} a shrine in Umekitamura Masutsuki from [[Ise Grand Shrine]] . Since then, it has been revered as the guardian deity of Shimazu Manor. [http://www.kanbashira.net/yuisho/index.html ご由緒｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 In [[Meiji period|May]] [[1873]], it was designated [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], and on October 28 of the same year, it was moved to its current location. revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Source Everybodywiki]] to [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:26, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Kanbashira-gū]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Kanbashira-gū]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft}} {{infobox shinto shrine| map_type = Japan Miyazaki Prefecture#Japan|name=神柱宮|image=Kanbashira guu Miyakonojo Shaden.jpg|location=[[宮崎県]][[都城市]]前田町1417-1|coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|31|43|56.1|N|131|4|19.3|E|display=inline,title}}|deity=[[Amaterasu]]
[[Toyouke-hime]]|Rank=旧[[県社]]・[[別表神社]]|established=1026 {{ill|September 9 (Lunisolar)|ja|9月9日 (旧暦)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|main hall=[[流造]]|alternative name=神柱神社|札所等=|festival={{ill|June lanterns|ja|六月灯|qq=draft|qq=draft}}（{{ill|Summer festival|ja|夏祭り|1=Natsu Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft}}）|map={{location map|Japan Miyazaki Prefecture|lat_deg=31|lat_min=43|lat_sec=56.1|lon_deg=131|lon_min=4|lon_sec=19.3|label=神柱宮|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|31|43|56.1|N|131|4|19.3|E}}}}|infobox shinto shrine|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|caption=Shrine}} {{complex}} {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮|fr||simple|User:Immanuelle/Kanbashira-gū|en:draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} '''Shrine''' is [[Shinto shrine|a shrine]] located in {{ill|10=ja|11=都城市|Miyakonojō|4=en|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, [[Miyazaki Prefecture]] . [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Its former Rank]] was that of [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], but it is currently [[Beppyo shrine|a special shrine]] under [[Association of Shinto Shrines|the Association of Shinto Shrines]] {{Cite web|title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー|url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/|access-date=2023-04-02|website=jinja-gosyuin.com}}. It is also called '''Kanbashira Shrine''' . Commonly known as '''the sacred pillar''' . There are two {{ill|10=ja|11=摂末社|setsumatsusha|lt=sub-shrines|en|setsumatsusha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} within the grounds : {{nihongo|Mikado Shrine|神門神社|Mikado Jinja}} and {{nihongo|Motobashira Shrine|基柱神社|Motobashira Jinja}}. [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ごdeity｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 == deity == The main deities enshrined are [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Omikami]] and [[Toyouke-hime|Toyoukehime Okami]], and five other deities are enshrined in the side shrine: [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]] and [[Ame-no-Tajikarao]] no Mikoto on the left (right side when facing the shrine) and {{ill|10=ja|11=天児屋命|Ame-no-Koyane|lt=|4=en|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|10=ja|11=栲幡千千姫命|Takuhadachiji-hime|4=en|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and [[Futodama|Ame-no-Tatamikoto]] on the right (left side when facing the shrine ). Additionally, the {{nihongo|Mikado Shrine|神門神社|Mikado Jinja}}, a sub-shrine within the grounds, enshrines {{ill|10=ja|11=天石門別神|Ama-no-Iwato-wake no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, while {{nihongo|Motobashira Shrine|基柱神社|Motobashira Jinja}} enshrines [[Sugawara no Michizane]] and {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}} . [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ごdeity｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 == history == It is said that [[Oracle|the shrine]] was founded [[1026|in 1026]] when {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who had moved from {{ill|10=ja|11=大宰府|Dazaifu|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to develop {{ill|10=ja|11=島津荘|Shimazu Estate|4=en|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, received a divine revelation from Amaterasu Omikami that said, "A shrine should be quickly built in this place and the name of the shrine should be called Kanbashira," and so he {{ill|10=ja|11=分霊|Bunrei|lt=enshrined|4=en|qq=draft|qq=draft}} a shrine in Umekitamura Masutsuki from [[Ise Grand Shrine]] . Since then, it has been revered as the guardian deity of Shimazu Manor. [http://www.kanbashira.net/yuisho/index.html ご由緒｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 In [[Meiji period|May]] [[1873]], it was designated [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], and on October 28 of the same year, it was moved to its current location. The area between the current Ganzo-ji Temple (Maeda-cho) and the current location of Jinbashira Shrine was a field filled with many small pines, so it was called Komatsubara or Matsubara, and there was a villa of the Shimazu family of Miyakonojo called Matsubara Ochaya. Upon the relocation, the area around Matsubara Ochaya was developed into {{ill|10=ja|11=神柱公園|Kanbashira Park|qq=draft|qq=draft}} . The shrine's former location was in {{ill|10=ja|11=梅北町|Masutsuki, Umekita-cho|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, within the city, and {{ill|10=ja|11=黒尾神社|Kuroo Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft}} has been relocated to its site. After the war, it joined the Association of Shinto Shrines and was designated a [[Beppyo Shrine]] on July 1, 1976 [[Shōwa period|.]] == Gallery == File:Okagebashi_Bridge_on_pond_in_Kambashira_Park.jpg|alt=恩賀橋|Okagebashi Bridge File:Sando_of_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=参道|Sando File:Sanshuden_of_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=参集殿|Sanshuden File:Stela_in_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=石碑|Stela File:Stele_in_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=碑文|Stele File:TiranoSuemoto's_Stone_monument.jpg|alt=津軽末元の石碑|TiranoSuemoto's Stone Monument File:Torii_of_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=鳥居|Torii File:Torpedo_in_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=魚雷|Torpedo File:基柱神社.jpg|alt=基柱神社|Kambashira Shrine File:基柱神社の扁額.jpg|alt=扁額|Shrine's name Plaque == [[Toyouke-hime]]|Rank=旧[[県社]]・[[別表神社]]|established=1026 {{ill|September 9 (Lunisolar)|ja|9月9日 (旧暦)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|main hall=[[流造]]|alternative name=神柱神社|札所等=|festival={{ill|June lanterns|ja|六月灯|qq=draft|qq=draft}}（{{ill|Summer festival|ja|夏祭り|1=Natsu Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft}}）|map={{location map|Japan Miyazaki Prefecture|lat_deg=31|lat_min=43|lat_sec=56.1|lon_deg=131|lon_min=4|lon_sec=19.3|label=神柱宮|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|31|43|56.1|N|131|4|19.3|E}}}}|infobox shinto shrine|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|caption=Shrine}} {{complex}} {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮|fr||simple|User:Immanuelle/Kanbashira-gū|en:draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} '''Shrine''' is [[Shinto shrine|a shrine]] located in {{ill|10=ja|11=都城市|Miyakonojō|4=en|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, [[Miyazaki Prefecture]] . [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Its former Rank]] was that of [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], but it is currently [[Beppyo shrine|a special shrine]] under [[Association of Shinto Shrines|the Association of Shinto Shrines]] {{Cite web|title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー|url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/|access-date=2023-04-02|website=jinja-gosyuin.com}}. It is also called '''Kanbashira Shrine''' . Commonly known as '''the sacred pillar''' . There are two {{ill|10=ja|11=摂末社|setsumatsusha|lt=sub-shrines|en|setsumatsusha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} within the grounds : {{nihongo|Mikado Shrine|神門神社|Mikado Jinja}} and {{nihongo|Motobashira Shrine|基柱神社|Motobashira Jinja}}. [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ごdeity｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 == deity == The main deities enshrined are [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Omikami]] and [[Toyouke-hime|Toyoukehime Okami]], and five other deities are enshrined in the side shrine: [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]] and [[Ame-no-Tajikarao]] no Mikoto on the left (right side when facing the shrine) and {{ill|10=ja|11=天児屋命|Ame-no-Koyane|lt=|4=en|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|10=ja|11=栲幡千千姫命|Takuhadachiji-hime|4=en|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and [[Futodama|Ame-no-Tatamikoto]] on the right (left side when facing the shrine ). Additionally, the {{nihongo|Mikado Shrine|神門神社|Mikado Jinja}}, a sub-shrine within the grounds, enshrines {{ill|10=ja|11=天石門別神|Ama-no-Iwato-wake no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, while {{nihongo|Motobashira Shrine|基柱神社|Motobashira Jinja}} enshrines [[Sugawara no Michizane]] and {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}} . [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ごdeity｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 == history == It is said that [[Oracle|the shrine]] was founded [[1026|in 1026]] when {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who had moved from {{ill|10=ja|11=大宰府|Dazaifu|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to develop {{ill|10=ja|11=島津荘|Shimazu Estate|4=en|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, received a divine revelation from Amaterasu Omikami that said, "A shrine should be quickly built in this place and the name of the shrine should be called Kanbashira," and so he {{ill|10=ja|11=分霊|Bunrei|lt=enshrined|4=en|qq=draft|qq=draft}} a shrine in Umekitamura Masutsuki from [[Ise Grand Shrine]] . Since then, it has been revered as the guardian deity of Shimazu Manor. [http://www.kanbashira.net/yuisho/index.html ご由緒｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 In [[Meiji period|May]] [[1873]], it was designated [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], and on October 28 of the same year, it was moved to its current location. The area between the current Ganzo-ji Temple (Maeda-cho) and the current location of Jinbashira Shrine was a field filled with many small pines, so it was called Komatsubara or Matsubara, and there was a villa of the Shimazu family of Miyakonojo called Matsubara Ochaya. Upon the relocation, the area around Matsubara Ochaya was developed into {{ill|10=ja|11=神柱公園|Kanbashira Park|qq=draft|qq=draft}} . The shrine's former location was in {{ill|10=ja|11=梅北町|Masutsuki, Umekita-cho|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, within the city, and {{ill|10=ja|11=黒尾神社|Kuroo Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft}} has been relocated to its site. After the war, it joined the Association of Shinto Shrines and was designated a [[Beppyo Shrine]] on July 1, 1976 [[Shōwa period|.]] == Gallery == [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ごdeity｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 == deity == The main deities enshrined are [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Omikami]] and [[Toyouke-hime|Toyoukehime Okami]], and five other deities are enshrined in the side shrine: [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]] and [[Ame-no-Tajikarao]] no Mikoto on the left (right side when facing the shrine) and {{ill|10=ja|11=天児屋命|Ame-no-Koyane|lt=|4=en|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|10=ja|11=栲幡千千姫命|Takuhadachiji-hime|4=en|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and [[Futodama|Ame-no-Tatamikoto]] on the right (left side when facing the shrine ). Additionally, the {{nihongo|Mikado Shrine|神門神社|Mikado Jinja}}, a sub-shrine within the grounds, enshrines {{ill|10=ja|11=天石門別神|Ama-no-Iwato-wake no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, while {{nihongo|Motobashira Shrine|基柱神社|Motobashira Jinja}} enshrines [[Sugawara no Michizane]] and {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}} . [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ごdeity｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 == history == It is said that [[Oracle|the shrine]] was founded [[1026|in 1026]] when {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who had moved from {{ill|10=ja|11=大宰府|Dazaifu|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to develop {{ill|10=ja|11=島津荘|Shimazu Estate|4=en|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, received a divine revelation from Amaterasu Omikami that said, "A shrine should be quickly built in this place and the name of the shrine should be called Kanbashira," and so he {{ill|10=ja|11=分霊|Bunrei|lt=enshrined|4=en|qq=draft|qq=draft}} a shrine in Umekitamura Masutsuki from [[Ise Grand Shrine]] . Since then, it has been revered as the guardian deity of Shimazu Manor. [http://www.kanbashira.net/yuisho/index.html ご由緒｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 In [[Meiji period|May]] [[1873]], it was designated [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], and on October 28 of the same year, it was moved to its current location. The area between the current Ganzo-ji Temple (Maeda-cho) and the current location of Jinbashira Shrine was a field filled with many small pines, so it was called Komatsubara or Matsubara, and there was a villa of the Shimazu family of Miyakonojo called Matsubara Ochaya. Upon the relocation, the area around Matsubara Ochaya was developed into {{ill|10=ja|11=神柱公園|Kanbashira Park|qq=draft|qq=draft}} . The shrine's former location was in {{ill|10=ja|11=梅北町|Masutsuki, Umekita-cho|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, within the city, and {{ill|10=ja|11=黒尾神社|Kuroo Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft}} has been relocated to its site. After the war, it joined the Association of Shinto Shrines and was designated a [[Beppyo Shrine]] on July 1, 1976 [[Shōwa period|.]] == Gallery == Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:26, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox shinto shrine| map_type = Japan Miyazaki Prefecture#Japan|name=神柱宮|image=Kanbashira guu Miyakonojo Shaden.jpg|location=[[宮崎県]][[都城市]]前田町1417-1|coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|31|43|56.1|N|131|4|19.3|E|display=inline,title}}|deity=[[Amaterasu]]
[[Toyouke-hime]]|Rank=旧[[県社]]・[[別表神社]]|established=1026 {{ill|September 9 (Lunisolar)|ja|9月9日 (旧暦)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 9 (Lunisolar)|qq=}}|main hall=[[流造]]|alternative name=神柱神社|札所等=|festival={{ill|June lanterns|ja|六月灯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June lanterns|qq=}}（{{ill|Summer festival|ja|夏祭り|1=Natsu Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Summer festival|13=User:Immanuelle/Natsu Matsuri|qq=}}）|map={{location map|Japan Miyazaki Prefecture|lat_deg=31|lat_min=43|lat_sec=56.1|lon_deg=131|lon_min=4|lon_sec=19.3|label=神柱宮|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|31|43|56.1|N|131|4|19.3|E}}}}|infobox shinto shrine|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|caption=Shrine}} {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮|fr|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kanbashira-gū|en:draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanbashira-gū|qq=}} '''Shrine''' is [[Shinto shrine|a shrine]] located in {{ill|10=ja|11=都城市|Miyakonojō|4=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, [[Miyazaki Prefecture]] . [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Its former Rank]] was that of [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], but it is currently [[Beppyo shrine|a special shrine]] under [[Association of Shinto Shrines|the Association of Shinto Shrines]] {{Cite web|title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー|url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/|access-date=2023-04-02|website=jinja-gosyuin.com}}. It is also called '''Kanbashira Shrine''' . Commonly known as '''the sacred pillar''' . There are two {{ill|10=ja|11=摂末社|setsumatsusha|lt=sub-shrines|en|setsumatsusha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} within the grounds : {{nihongo|Mikado Shrine|神門神社|Mikado Jinja}} and {{nihongo|Motobashira Shrine|基柱神社|Motobashira Jinja}}. [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ごdeity｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 == deity == The main deities enshrined are [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Omikami]] and [[Toyouke-hime|Toyoukehime Okami]], and five other deities are enshrined in the side shrine: [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]] and [[Ame-no-Tajikarao]] no Mikoto on the left (right side when facing the shrine) and {{ill|10=ja|11=天児屋命|Ame-no-Koyane|lt=|4=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, {{ill|10=ja|11=栲幡千千姫命|Takuhadachiji-hime|4=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, and [[Futodama|Ame-no-Tatamikoto]] on the right (left side when facing the shrine ). Additionally, the {{nihongo|Mikado Shrine|神門神社|Mikado Jinja}}, a sub-shrine within the grounds, enshrines {{ill|10=ja|11=天石門別神|Ama-no-Iwato-wake no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, while {{nihongo|Motobashira Shrine|基柱神社|Motobashira Jinja}} enshrines [[Sugawara no Michizane]] and {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} . [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ごdeity｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 == history == It is said that [[Oracle|the shrine]] was founded [[1026|in 1026]] when {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, who had moved from {{ill|10=ja|11=大宰府|Dazaifu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} to develop {{ill|10=ja|11=島津荘|Shimazu Estate|4=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, received a divine revelation from Amaterasu Omikami that said, "A shrine should be quickly built in this place and the name of the shrine should be called Kanbashira," and so he {{ill|10=ja|11=分霊|Bunrei|lt=enshrined|4=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} a shrine in Umekitamura Masutsuki from [[Ise Grand Shrine]] . Since then, it has been revered as the guardian deity of Shimazu Manor. [http://www.kanbashira.net/yuisho/index.html ご由緒｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 In [[Meiji period|May]] [[1873]], it was designated [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], and on October 28 of the same year, it was moved to its current location. The area between the current Ganzo-ji Temple (Maeda-cho) and the current location of Jinbashira Shrine was a field filled with many small pines, so it was called Komatsubara or Matsubara, and there was a villa of the Shimazu family of Miyakonojo called Matsubara Ochaya. Upon the relocation, the area around Matsubara Ochaya was developed into {{ill|10=ja|11=神柱公園|Kanbashira Park|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} . The shrine's former location was in {{ill|10=ja|11=梅北町|Masutsuki, Umekita-cho|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, within the city, and {{ill|10=ja|11=黒尾神社|Kuroo Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} has been relocated to its site. After the war, it joined the Association of Shinto Shrines and was designated a [[Beppyo Shrine]] on July 1, 1976 [[Shōwa period|.]] == Gallery == [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ごdeity｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 == history == It is said that [[Oracle|the shrine]] was founded [[1026|in 1026]] when {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, who had moved from {{ill|10=ja|11=大宰府|Dazaifu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} to develop {{ill|10=ja|11=島津荘|Shimazu Estate|4=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, received a divine revelation from Amaterasu Omikami that said, "A shrine should be quickly built in this place and the name of the shrine should be called Kanbashira," and so he {{ill|10=ja|11=分霊|Bunrei|lt=enshrined|4=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} a shrine in Umekitamura Masutsuki from [[Ise Grand Shrine]] . Since then, it has been revered as the guardian deity of Shimazu Manor. [http://www.kanbashira.net/yuisho/index.html ご由緒｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 In [[Meiji period|May]] [[1873]], it was designated [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], and on October 28 of the same year, it was moved to its current location. The area between the current Ganzo-ji Temple (Maeda-cho) and the current location of Jinbashira Shrine was a field filled with many small pines, so it was called Komatsubara or Matsubara, and there was a villa of the Shimazu family of Miyakonojo called Matsubara Ochaya. Upon the relocation, the area around Matsubara Ochaya was developed into {{ill|10=ja|11=神柱公園|Kanbashira Park|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} . The shrine's former location was in {{ill|10=ja|11=梅北町|Masutsuki, Umekita-cho|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, within the city, and {{ill|10=ja|11=黒尾神社|Kuroo Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} has been relocated to its site. After the war, it joined the Association of Shinto Shrines and was designated a [[Beppyo Shrine]] on July 1, 1976 [[Shōwa period|.]] == Gallery == {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:26, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Kanbashira-gū]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Kanbashira-gū]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox shinto shrine| map_type = Japan Miyazaki Prefecture#Japan|name=神柱宮|image=Kanbashira guu Miyakonojo Shaden.jpg|location=[[宮崎県]][[都城市]]前田町1417-1|coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|31|43|56.1|N|131|4|19.3|E|display=inline,title}}|deity=[[Amaterasu]]
[[Toyouke-hime]]|Rank=旧[[県社]]・[[別表神社]]|established=1026 {{ill|September 9 (Lunisolar)|ja|9月9日 (旧暦)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 9 (Lunisolar)|qq=}}|main hall=[[流造]]|alternative name=神柱神社|札所等=|festival={{ill|June lanterns|ja|六月灯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June lanterns|qq=}}（{{ill|Summer festival|ja|夏祭り|1=Natsu Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Summer festival|13=User:Immanuelle/Natsu Matsuri|qq=}}）|map={{location map|Japan Miyazaki Prefecture|lat_deg=31|lat_min=43|lat_sec=56.1|lon_deg=131|lon_min=4|lon_sec=19.3|label=神柱宮|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|31|43|56.1|N|131|4|19.3|E}}}}|infobox shinto shrine|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|caption=Shrine}} {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮|fr|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kanbashira-gū|en:draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanbashira-gū|qq=}} '''Shrine''' is [[Shinto shrine|a shrine]] located in [[:en:Miyakonojō|Miyakonojō]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]] . [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Its former Rank]] was that of [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], but it is currently [[Beppyo Shrine|a special shrine]] under [[Association of Shinto Shrines|the Association of Shinto Shrines]] {{Cite web|title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー|url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/|access-date=2023-04-02|website=jinja-gosyuin.com}}. It is also called '''Kanbashira Shrine''' . Commonly known as '''the sacred pillar''' . There are two [[:en:Setsumatsusha|sub-shrines]] within the grounds : {{nihongo|Mikado Shrine|神門神社|Mikado Jinja}} and {{nihongo|Motobashira Shrine|基柱神社|Motobashira Jinja}}. [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ごdeity｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 == deity == The main deities enshrined are [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Omikami]] and [[Toyouke-hime|Toyoukehime Okami]], and five other deities are enshrined in the side shrine: [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]] and [[Ame-no-Tajikarao]] no Mikoto on the left (right side when facing the shrine) and [[:en:Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane]], [[:en:Takuhadachiji-hime|Takuhadachiji-hime]], and [[Futodama|Ame-no-Tatamikoto]] on the right (left side when facing the shrine ). Additionally, the {{nihongo|Mikado Shrine|神門神社|Mikado Jinja}}, a sub-shrine within the grounds, enshrines {{ill|10=ja|11=天石門別神|Ama-no-Iwato-wake no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, while {{nihongo|Motobashira Shrine|基柱神社|Motobashira Jinja}} enshrines [[Sugawara no Michizane]] and {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} . [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ごdeity｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 == history == It is said that [[Oracle|the shrine]] was founded [[1026|in 1026]] when {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, who had moved from [[:en:Dazaifu (government)|Dazaifu]] to develop [[:en:Shimazu Estate|Shimazu Estate]], received a divine revelation from Amaterasu Omikami that said, "A shrine should be quickly built in this place and the name of the shrine should be called Kanbashira," and so he [[:en:Bunrei|enshrined]] a shrine in Umekitamura Masutsuki from [[Ise Grand Shrine]] . Since then, it has been revered as the guardian deity of Shimazu Manor. [http://www.kanbashira.net/yuisho/index.html ご由緒｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 In [[Meiji period|May]] [[1873]], it was designated [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], and on October 28 of the same year, it was moved to its current location. The area between the current Ganzo-ji Temple (Maeda-cho) and the current location of Jinbashira Shrine was a field filled with many small pines, so it was called Komatsubara or Matsubara, and there was a villa of the Shimazu family of Miyakonojo called Matsubara Ochaya. Upon the relocation, the area around Matsubara Ochaya was developed into {{ill|10=ja|11=神柱公園|Kanbashira Park|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} . The shrine's former location was in {{ill|10=ja|11=梅北町|Masutsuki, Umekita-cho|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, within the city, and {{ill|10=ja|11=黒尾神社|Kuroo Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} has been relocated to its site. After the war, it joined the Association of Shinto Shrines and was designated a [[Beppyo Shrine]] on July 1, 1976 [[Shōwa period|.]] == Gallery == revision.comment: Bot: add category Category:宮崎県の旧県社
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:26, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Kanbashira-gū]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Kanbashira-gū]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox shinto shrine| map_type = Japan Miyazaki Prefecture#Japan|name=神柱宮|image=Kanbashira guu Miyakonojo Shaden.jpg|location=[[宮崎県]][[都城市]]前田町1417-1|coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|31|43|56.1|N|131|4|19.3|E|display=inline,title}}|deity=[[Amaterasu]]
[[Toyouke-hime]]|Rank=旧[[県社]]・[[別表神社]]|established=1026 {{ill|September 9 (Lunisolar)|ja|9月9日 (旧暦)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 9 (Lunisolar)|qq=}}|main hall=[[流造]]|alternative name=神柱神社|札所等=|festival={{ill|June lanterns|ja|六月灯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June lanterns|qq=}}（{{ill|Summer festival|ja|夏祭り|1=Natsu Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Summer festival|13=User:Immanuelle/Natsu Matsuri|qq=}}）|map={{location map|Japan Miyazaki Prefecture|lat_deg=31|lat_min=43|lat_sec=56.1|lon_deg=131|lon_min=4|lon_sec=19.3|label=神柱宮|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|31|43|56.1|N|131|4|19.3|E}}}}|infobox shinto shrine|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|caption=Shrine}} {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮|fr|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kanbashira-gū|en:draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanbashira-gū|qq=}} '''Shrine''' is [[Shinto shrine|a shrine]] located in [[:en:Miyakonojō|Miyakonojō]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]] . [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Its former Rank]] was that of [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], but it is currently [[Beppyo Shrine|a special shrine]] under [[Association of Shinto Shrines|the Association of Shinto Shrines]] {{Cite web|title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー|url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/|access-date=2023-04-02|website=jinja-gosyuin.com}}. It is also called '''Kanbashira Shrine''' . Commonly known as '''the sacred pillar''' . There are two [[:en:Setsumatsusha|sub-shrines]] within the grounds : {{nihongo|Mikado Shrine|神門神社|Mikado Jinja}} and {{nihongo|Motobashira Shrine|基柱神社|Motobashira Jinja}}. [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ごdeity｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 == deity == The main deities enshrined are [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Omikami]] and [[Toyouke-hime|Toyoukehime Okami]], and five other deities are enshrined in the side shrine: [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]] and [[Ame-no-Tajikarao]] no Mikoto on the left (right side when facing the shrine) and [[:en:Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane]], [[:en:Takuhadachiji-hime|Takuhadachiji-hime]], and [[Futodama|Ame-no-Tatamikoto]] on the right (left side when facing the shrine ). Additionally, the {{nihongo|Mikado Shrine|神門神社|Mikado Jinja}}, a sub-shrine within the grounds, enshrines {{ill|10=ja|11=天石門別神|Ama-no-Iwato-wake no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, while {{nihongo|Motobashira Shrine|基柱神社|Motobashira Jinja}} enshrines [[Sugawara no Michizane]] and {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} . [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ごdeity｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 == history == It is said that [[Oracle|the shrine]] was founded [[1026|in 1026]] when {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, who had moved from [[:en:Dazaifu (government)|Dazaifu]] to develop [[:en:Shimazu Estate|Shimazu Estate]], received a divine revelation from Amaterasu Omikami that said, "A shrine should be quickly built in this place and the name of the shrine should be called Kanbashira," and so he [[:en:Bunrei|enshrined]] a shrine in Umekitamura Masutsuki from [[Ise Grand Shrine]] . Since then, it has been revered as the guardian deity of Shimazu Manor. [http://www.kanbashira.net/yuisho/index.html ご由緒｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 In [[Meiji period|May]] [[1873]], it was designated [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], and on October 28 of the same year, it was moved to its current location. The area between the current Ganzo-ji Temple (Maeda-cho) and the current location of Jinbashira Shrine was a field filled with many small pines, so it was called Komatsubara or Matsubara, and there was a villa of the Shimazu family of Miyakonojo called Matsubara Ochaya. Upon the relocation, the area around Matsubara Ochaya was developed into {{ill|10=ja|11=神柱公園|Kanbashira Park|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} . The shrine's former location was in {{ill|10=ja|11=梅北町|Masutsuki, Umekita-cho|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, within the city, and {{ill|10=ja|11=黒尾神社|Kuroo Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} has been relocated to its site. After the war, it joined the Association of Shinto Shrines and was designated a [[Beppyo Shrine]] on July 1, 1976 [[Shōwa period|.]] == Gallery == File:Chozuya_and_Sanshuden_Hall_of_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=手水舎と参集殿|Chozuya and Sanshuden Hall File:Ginkgo_biloba_in_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=銀杏|Ginkgo biloba File:Kanbashira_guu_Miyakonojo_Shaden.jpg|alt=宮之城社殿|Shaden in Miyakonojo File:Motobashira_Shrine_in_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=元柱神社|Motobashira Shrine File:Okagebashi_Bridge_on_pond_in_Kambashira_Park.jpg|alt=恩賀橋|Okagebashi Bridge File:Sando_of_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=参道|Sando File:Sanshuden_of_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=参集殿|Sanshuden File:Stela_in_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=石碑|Stela File:Stele_in_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=碑文|Stele File:TiranoSuemoto's_Stone_monument.jpg|alt=津軽末元の石碑|TiranoSuemoto's Stone Monument File:Torii_and_stairs_of_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=鳥居と階段|Torii and Stairs File:Torii_of_Kambashira_Shrine_from_inner_side.jpg|alt=内側からの鳥居|Torii from Inner Side File:Torii_of_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=鳥居|Torii File:Torpedo_in_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=魚雷|Torpedo File:基柱神社.jpg|alt=基柱神社|Kambashira Shrine File:基柱神社の扁額.jpg|alt=扁額|Shrine's name Plaque == [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox shinto shrine| map_type = Japan Miyazaki Prefecture#Japan|name=神柱宮|image=Kanbashira guu Miyakonojo Shaden.jpg|location=[[宮崎県]][[都城市]]前田町1417-1|coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|31|43|56.1|N|131|4|19.3|E|display=inline,title}}|deity=[[Amaterasu]]
[[Toyouke-hime]]|Rank=旧[[県社]]・[[別表神社]]|established=1026 {{ill|September 9 (Lunisolar)|ja|9月9日 (旧暦)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 9 (Lunisolar)|qq=}}|main hall=[[流造]]|alternative name=神柱神社|札所等=|festival={{ill|June lanterns|ja|六月灯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June lanterns|qq=}}（{{ill|Summer festival|ja|夏祭り|1=Natsu Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Summer festival|13=User:Immanuelle/Natsu Matsuri|qq=}}）|map={{location map|Japan Miyazaki Prefecture|lat_deg=31|lat_min=43|lat_sec=56.1|lon_deg=131|lon_min=4|lon_sec=19.3|label=神柱宮|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|31|43|56.1|N|131|4|19.3|E}}}}|infobox shinto shrine|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|caption=Shrine}} {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮|fr|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kanbashira-gū|en:draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanbashira-gū|qq=}} '''Shrine''' is [[Shinto shrine|a shrine]] located in [[:en:Miyakonojō|Miyakonojō]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]] . [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Its former Rank]] was that of [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], but it is currently [[Beppyo Shrine|a special shrine]] under [[Association of Shinto Shrines|the Association of Shinto Shrines]] {{Cite web|title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー|url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/|access-date=2023-04-02|website=jinja-gosyuin.com}}. It is also called '''Kanbashira Shrine''' . Commonly known as '''the sacred pillar''' . There are two [[:en:Setsumatsusha|sub-shrines]] within the grounds : {{nihongo|Mikado Shrine|神門神社|Mikado Jinja}} and {{nihongo|Motobashira Shrine|基柱神社|Motobashira Jinja}}. [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ごdeity｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 == deity == The main deities enshrined are [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Omikami]] and [[Toyouke-hime|Toyoukehime Okami]], and five other deities are enshrined in the side shrine: [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]] and [[Ame-no-Tajikarao]] no Mikoto on the left (right side when facing the shrine) and [[:en:Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane]], [[:en:Takuhadachiji-hime|Takuhadachiji-hime]], and [[Futodama|Ame-no-Tatamikoto]] on the right (left side when facing the shrine ). Additionally, the {{nihongo|Mikado Shrine|神門神社|Mikado Jinja}}, a sub-shrine within the grounds, enshrines {{ill|10=ja|11=天石門別神|Ama-no-Iwato-wake no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, while {{nihongo|Motobashira Shrine|基柱神社|Motobashira Jinja}} enshrines [[Sugawara no Michizane]] and {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} . [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ごdeity｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 == history == It is said that [[Oracle|the shrine]] was founded [[1026|in 1026]] when {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, who had moved from [[:en:Dazaifu (government)|Dazaifu]] to develop [[:en:Shimazu Estate|Shimazu Estate]], received a divine revelation from Amaterasu Omikami that said, "A shrine should be quickly built in this place and the name of the shrine should be called Kanbashira," and so he [[:en:Bunrei|enshrined]] a shrine in Umekitamura Masutsuki from [[Ise Grand Shrine]] . Since then, it has been revered as the guardian deity of Shimazu Manor. [http://www.kanbashira.net/yuisho/index.html ご由緒｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 In [[Meiji period|May]] [[1873]], it was designated [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], and on October 28 of the same year, it was moved to its current location. The area between the current Ganzo-ji Temple (Maeda-cho) and the current location of Jinbashira Shrine was a field filled with many small pines, so it was called Komatsubara or Matsubara, and there was a villa of the Shimazu family of Miyakonojo called Matsubara Ochaya. Upon the relocation, the area around Matsubara Ochaya was developed into {{ill|10=ja|11=神柱公園|Kanbashira Park|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} . The shrine's former location was in {{ill|10=ja|11=梅北町|Masutsuki, Umekita-cho|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, within the city, and {{ill|10=ja|11=黒尾神社|Kuroo Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} has been relocated to its site. After the war, it joined the Association of Shinto Shrines and was designated a [[Beppyo Shrine]] on July 1, 1976 [[Shōwa period|.]] == Gallery == [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ごdeity｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 == deity == The main deities enshrined are [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Omikami]] and [[Toyouke-hime|Toyoukehime Okami]], and five other deities are enshrined in the side shrine: [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]] and [[Ame-no-Tajikarao]] no Mikoto on the left (right side when facing the shrine) and [[:en:Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane]], [[:en:Takuhadachiji-hime|Takuhadachiji-hime]], and [[Futodama|Ame-no-Tatamikoto]] on the right (left side when facing the shrine ). Additionally, the {{nihongo|Mikado Shrine|神門神社|Mikado Jinja}}, a sub-shrine within the grounds, enshrines {{ill|10=ja|11=天石門別神|Ama-no-Iwato-wake no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, while {{nihongo|Motobashira Shrine|基柱神社|Motobashira Jinja}} enshrines [[Sugawara no Michizane]] and {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} . [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ごdeity｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 == history == It is said that [[Oracle|the shrine]] was founded [[1026|in 1026]] when {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, who had moved from [[:en:Dazaifu (government)|Dazaifu]] to develop [[:en:Shimazu Estate|Shimazu Estate]], received a divine revelation from Amaterasu Omikami that said, "A shrine should be quickly built in this place and the name of the shrine should be called Kanbashira," and so he [[:en:Bunrei|enshrined]] a shrine in Umekitamura Masutsuki from [[Ise Grand Shrine]] . Since then, it has been revered as the guardian deity of Shimazu Manor. [http://www.kanbashira.net/yuisho/index.html ご由緒｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 In [[Meiji period|May]] [[1873]], it was designated [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], and on October 28 of the same year, it was moved to its current location. The area between the current Ganzo-ji Temple (Maeda-cho) and the current location of Jinbashira Shrine was a field filled with many small pines, so it was called Komatsubara or Matsubara, and there was a villa of the Shimazu family of Miyakonojo called Matsubara Ochaya. Upon the relocation, the area around Matsubara Ochaya was developed into {{ill|10=ja|11=神柱公園|Kanbashira Park|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} . The shrine's former location was in {{ill|10=ja|11=梅北町|Masutsuki, Umekita-cho|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, within the city, and {{ill|10=ja|11=黒尾神社|Kuroo Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} has been relocated to its site. After the war, it joined the Association of Shinto Shrines and was designated a [[Beppyo Shrine]] on July 1, 1976 [[Shōwa period|.]] == Gallery == [[Toyouke-hime]]|Rank=旧[[県社]]・[[別表神社]]|established=1026 {{ill|September 9 (Lunisolar)|ja|9月9日 (旧暦)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 9 (Lunisolar)|qq=}}|main hall=[[流造]]|alternative name=神柱神社|札所等=|festival={{ill|June lanterns|ja|六月灯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June lanterns|qq=}}（{{ill|Summer festival|ja|夏祭り|1=Natsu Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Summer festival|13=User:Immanuelle/Natsu Matsuri|qq=}}）|map={{location map|Japan Miyazaki Prefecture|lat_deg=31|lat_min=43|lat_sec=56.1|lon_deg=131|lon_min=4|lon_sec=19.3|label=神柱宮|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|31|43|56.1|N|131|4|19.3|E}}}}|infobox shinto shrine|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|caption=Shrine}} {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮|fr|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kanbashira-gū|en:draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanbashira-gū|qq=}} '''Shrine''' is [[Shinto shrine|a shrine]] located in [[:en:Miyakonojō|Miyakonojō]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]] . [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Its former Rank]] was that of [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], but it is currently [[Beppyo Shrine|a special shrine]] under [[Association of Shinto Shrines|the Association of Shinto Shrines]] {{Cite web|title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー|url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/|access-date=2023-04-02|website=jinja-gosyuin.com}}. It is also called '''Kanbashira Shrine''' . Commonly known as '''the sacred pillar''' . There are two [[:en:Setsumatsusha|sub-shrines]] within the grounds : {{nihongo|Mikado Shrine|神門神社|Mikado Jinja}} and {{nihongo|Motobashira Shrine|基柱神社|Motobashira Jinja}}. [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ごdeity｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 == deity == The main deities enshrined are [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Omikami]] and [[Toyouke-hime|Toyoukehime Okami]], and five other deities are enshrined in the side shrine: [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]] and [[Ame-no-Tajikarao]] no Mikoto on the left (right side when facing the shrine) and [[:en:Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane]], [[:en:Takuhadachiji-hime|Takuhadachiji-hime]], and [[Futodama|Ame-no-Tatamikoto]] on the right (left side when facing the shrine ). Additionally, the {{nihongo|Mikado Shrine|神門神社|Mikado Jinja}}, a sub-shrine within the grounds, enshrines {{ill|10=ja|11=天石門別神|Ama-no-Iwato-wake no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, while {{nihongo|Motobashira Shrine|基柱神社|Motobashira Jinja}} enshrines [[Sugawara no Michizane]] and {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} . [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ごdeity｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 == history == It is said that [[Oracle|the shrine]] was founded [[1026|in 1026]] when {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, who had moved from [[:en:Dazaifu (government)|Dazaifu]] to develop [[:en:Shimazu Estate|Shimazu Estate]], received a divine revelation from Amaterasu Omikami that said, "A shrine should be quickly built in this place and the name of the shrine should be called Kanbashira," and so he [[:en:Bunrei|enshrined]] a shrine in Umekitamura Masutsuki from [[Ise Grand Shrine]] . Since then, it has been revered as the guardian deity of Shimazu Manor. [http://www.kanbashira.net/yuisho/index.html ご由緒｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 In [[Meiji period|May]] [[1873]], it was designated [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], and on October 28 of the same year, it was moved to its current location. The area between the current Ganzo-ji Temple (Maeda-cho) and the current location of Jinbashira Shrine was a field filled with many small pines, so it was called Komatsubara or Matsubara, and there was a villa of the Shimazu family of Miyakonojo called Matsubara Ochaya. Upon the relocation, the area around Matsubara Ochaya was developed into {{ill|10=ja|11=神柱公園|Kanbashira Park|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} . The shrine's former location was in {{ill|10=ja|11=梅北町|Masutsuki, Umekita-cho|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, within the city, and {{ill|10=ja|11=黒尾神社|Kuroo Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} has been relocated to its site. After the war, it joined the Association of Shinto Shrines and was designated a [[Beppyo Shrine]] on July 1, 1976 [[Shōwa period|.]] == Gallery == The area between the current Ganzo-ji Temple (Maeda-cho) and the current location of Jinbashira Shrine was a field filled with many small pines, so it was called Komatsubara or Matsubara, and there was a villa of the Shimazu family of Miyakonojo called Matsubara Ochaya. Upon the relocation, the area around Matsubara Ochaya was developed into {{ill|10=ja|11=神柱公園|Kanbashira Park|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} . The shrine's former location was in {{ill|10=ja|11=梅北町|Masutsuki, Umekita-cho|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, within the city, and {{ill|10=ja|11=黒尾神社|Kuroo Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} has been relocated to its site. After the war, it joined the Association of Shinto Shrines and was designated a [[Beppyo Shrine]] on July 1, 1976 [[Shōwa period|.]] == Gallery ==
