revision.comment: Bot: remove invalid enwiki interwiki links
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:26, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Kanbashira-gū]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Kanbashira-gū]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox shinto shrine| map_type = Japan Miyazaki Prefecture#Japan|name=神柱宮|image=Kanbashira guu Miyakonojo Shaden.jpg|location=[[宮崎県]][[都城市]]前田町1417-1|coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|31|43|56.1|N|131|4|19.3|E|display=inline,title}}|deity=[[Amaterasu]]
[[Toyouke-hime]]|Rank=旧[[県社]]・[[別表神社]]|established=1026 {{ill|September 9 (Lunisolar)|ja|9月9日 (旧暦)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 9 (Lunisolar)|qq=}}|main hall=[[流造]]|alternative name=神柱神社|札所等=|festival={{ill|June lanterns|ja|六月灯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June lanterns|qq=}}（{{ill|Summer festival|ja|夏祭り|1=Natsu Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Summer festival|13=User:Immanuelle/Natsu Matsuri|qq=}}）|map={{location map|Japan Miyazaki Prefecture|lat_deg=31|lat_min=43|lat_sec=56.1|lon_deg=131|lon_min=4|lon_sec=19.3|label=神柱宮|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|31|43|56.1|N|131|4|19.3|E}}}}|infobox shinto shrine|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|caption=Shrine}} {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮|fr|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kanbashira-gū|en:draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanbashira-gū|qq=}} '''Shrine''' is [[Shinto shrine|a shrine]] located in [[:en:Miyakonojō|Miyakonojō]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]] . [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Its former Rank]] was that of [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], but it is currently [[Beppyo Shrine|a special shrine]] under [[Association of Shinto Shrines|the Association of Shinto Shrines]] {{Cite web|title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー|url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/|access-date=2023-04-02|website=jinja-gosyuin.com}}. It is also called '''Kanbashira Shrine''' . Commonly known as '''the sacred pillar''' . There are two [[:en:Setsumatsusha|sub-shrines]] within the grounds : {{nihongo|Mikado Shrine|神門神社|Mikado Jinja}} and {{nihongo|Motobashira Shrine|基柱神社|Motobashira Jinja}}. [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ごdeity｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 == deity == The main deities enshrined are [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Omikami]] and [[Toyouke-hime|Toyoukehime Okami]], and five other deities are enshrined in the side shrine: [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]] and [[Ame-no-Tajikarao]] no Mikoto on the left (right side when facing the shrine) and [[:en:Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane]], [[:en:Takuhadachiji-hime|Takuhadachiji-hime]], and [[Futodama|Ame-no-Tatamikoto]] on the right (left side when facing the shrine ). Additionally, the {{nihongo|Mikado Shrine|神門神社|Mikado Jinja}}, a sub-shrine within the grounds, enshrines {{ill|10=ja|11=天石門別神|Ama-no-Iwato-wake no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, while {{nihongo|Motobashira Shrine|基柱神社|Motobashira Jinja}} enshrines [[Sugawara no Michizane]] and {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} . [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ごdeity｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 == history == It is said that [[Oracle|the shrine]] was founded [[1026|in 1026]] when {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, who had moved from [[:en:Dazaifu (government)|Dazaifu]] to develop [[:en:Shimazu Estate|Shimazu Estate]], received a divine revelation from Amaterasu Omikami that said, "A shrine should be quickly built in this place and the name of the shrine should be called Kanbashira," and so he [[:en:Bunrei|enshrined]] a shrine in Umekitamura Masutsuki from [[Ise Grand Shrine]] . Since then, it has been revered as the guardian deity of Shimazu Manor. [http://www.kanbashira.net/yuisho/index.html ご由緒｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 In [[Meiji period|May]] [[1873]], it was designated [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], and on October 28 of the same year, it was moved to its current location. The area between the current Ganzo-ji Temple (Maeda-cho) and the current location of Jinbashira Shrine was a field filled with many small pines, so it was called Komatsubara or Matsubara, and there was a villa of the Shimazu family of Miyakonojo called Matsubara Ochaya. Upon the relocation, the area around Matsubara Ochaya was developed into {{ill|10=ja|11=神柱公園|Kanbashira Park|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} . The shrine's former location was in {{ill|10=ja|11=梅北町|Masutsuki, Umekita-cho|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, within the city, and {{ill|10=ja|11=黒尾神社|Kuroo Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} has been relocated to its site. After the war, it joined the Association of Shinto Shrines and was designated a [[Beppyo Shrine]] on July 1, 1976 [[Shōwa period|.]] == Gallery == File:Chozuya_and_Sanshuden_Hall_of_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=手水舎と参集殿|Chozuya and Sanshuden Hall File:Ginkgo_biloba_in_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=銀杏|Ginkgo biloba File:Kanbashira_guu_Miyakonojo_Shaden.jpg|alt=宮之城社殿|Shaden in Miyakonojo File:Motobashira_Shrine_in_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=元柱神社|Motobashira Shrine File:Okagebashi_Bridge_on_pond_in_Kambashira_Park.jpg|alt=恩賀橋|Okagebashi Bridge File:Sando_of_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=参道|Sando File:Sanshuden_of_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=参集殿|Sanshuden File:Stela_in_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=石碑|Stela File:Stele_in_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=碑文|Stele File:TiranoSuemoto's_Stone_monument.jpg|alt=津軽末元の石碑|TiranoSuemoto's Stone Monument File:Torii_and_stairs_of_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=鳥居と階段|Torii and Stairs File:Torii_of_Kambashira_Shrine_from_inner_side.jpg|alt=内側からの鳥居|Torii from Inner Side File:Torii_of_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=鳥居|Torii File:Torpedo_in_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=魚雷|Torpedo File:基柱神社.jpg|alt=基柱神社|Kambashira Shrine File:基柱神社の扁額.jpg|alt=扁額|Shrine's name Plaque == revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:26, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Kanbashira-gū]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Kanbashira-gū]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox shinto shrine| map_type = Japan Miyazaki Prefecture#Japan|name=神柱宮|image=Kanbashira guu Miyakonojo Shaden.jpg|location=[[宮崎県]][[都城市]]前田町1417-1|coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|31|43|56.1|N|131|4|19.3|E|display=inline,title}}|deity=[[Amaterasu]]
[[Toyouke-hime]]|Rank=旧[[県社]]・[[別表神社]]|established=1026 {{ill|September 9 (Lunisolar)|ja|9月9日 (旧暦)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 9 (Lunisolar)|qq=}}|main hall=[[流造]]|alternative name=神柱神社|札所等=|festival={{ill|June lanterns|ja|六月灯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June lanterns|qq=}}（{{ill|Summer festival|ja|夏祭り|1=Natsu Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Summer festival|13=User:Immanuelle/Natsu Matsuri|qq=}}）|map={{location map|Japan Miyazaki Prefecture|lat_deg=31|lat_min=43|lat_sec=56.1|lon_deg=131|lon_min=4|lon_sec=19.3|label=神柱宮|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|31|43|56.1|N|131|4|19.3|E}}}}|infobox shinto shrine|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|caption=Shrine}} {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮|fr|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kanbashira-gū|en:draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanbashira-gū|qq=}} '''Shrine''' is [[Shinto shrine|a shrine]] located in [[:en:Miyakonojō|Miyakonojō]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]] . [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Its former Rank]] was that of [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], but it is currently [[Beppyo Shrine|a special shrine]] under [[Association of Shinto Shrines|the Association of Shinto Shrines]] {{Cite web|title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー|url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/|access-date=2023-04-02|website=jinja-gosyuin.com}}. It is also called '''Kanbashira Shrine''' . Commonly known as '''the sacred pillar''' . There are two [[:en:Setsumatsusha|sub-shrines]] within the grounds : {{nihongo|Mikado Shrine|神門神社|Mikado Jinja}} and {{nihongo|Motobashira Shrine|基柱神社|Motobashira Jinja}}. [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ごdeity｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 == deity == The main deities enshrined are [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Omikami]] and [[Toyouke-hime|Toyoukehime Okami]], and five other deities are enshrined in the side shrine: [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]] and [[Ame-no-Tajikarao]] no Mikoto on the left (right side when facing the shrine) and [[:en:Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane]], [[:en:Takuhadachiji-hime|Takuhadachiji-hime]], and [[Futodama|Ame-no-Tatamikoto]] on the right (left side when facing the shrine ). Additionally, the {{nihongo|Mikado Shrine|神門神社|Mikado Jinja}}, a sub-shrine within the grounds, enshrines {{ill|10=ja|11=天石門別神|Ama-no-Iwato-wake no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, while {{nihongo|Motobashira Shrine|基柱神社|Motobashira Jinja}} enshrines [[Sugawara no Michizane]] and {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} . [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ごdeity｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 == history == It is said that [[Oracle|the shrine]] was founded [[:en:1026|in 1026]] when {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, who had moved from [[:en:Dazaifu (government)|Dazaifu]] to develop [[:en:Shimazu Estate|Shimazu Estate]], received a divine revelation from Amaterasu Omikami that said, "A shrine should be quickly built in this place and the name of the shrine should be called Kanbashira," and so he [[:en:Bunrei|enshrined]] a shrine in Umekitamura Masutsuki from [[Ise Grand Shrine]] . Since then, it has been revered as the guardian deity of Shimazu Manor. [http://www.kanbashira.net/yuisho/index.html ご由緒｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 In [[Meiji period|May]] [[1873]], it was designated [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], and on October 28 of the same year, it was moved to its current location. The area between the current Ganzo-ji Temple (Maeda-cho) and the current location of Jinbashira Shrine was a field filled with many small pines, so it was called Komatsubara or Matsubara, and there was a villa of the Shimazu family of Miyakonojo called Matsubara Ochaya. Upon the relocation, the area around Matsubara Ochaya was developed into {{ill|10=ja|11=神柱公園|Kanbashira Park|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} . The shrine's former location was in {{ill|10=ja|11=梅北町|Masutsuki, Umekita-cho|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, within the city, and {{ill|10=ja|11=黒尾神社|Kuroo Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} has been relocated to its site. After the war, it joined the Association of Shinto Shrines and was designated a [[Beppyo Shrine]] on July 1, 1976 [[Shōwa period|.]] == Gallery == File:Chozuya_and_Sanshuden_Hall_of_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=手水舎と参集殿|Chozuya and Sanshuden Hall File:Ginkgo_biloba_in_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=銀杏|Ginkgo biloba File:Grand_torii_of_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=大鳥居|Grand Torii File:Haiden_of_Kambashira_Shrine_closeup.jpg|alt=拝殿クローズアップ|Haiden Closeup File:Haiden_of_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=拝殿|Haiden File:Kanbashira_guu_Miyakonojo_Shaden.jpg|alt=宮之城社殿|Shaden in Miyakonojo File:Motobashira_Shrine_in_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=元柱神社|Motobashira Shrine File:Okagebashi_Bridge_on_pond_in_Kambashira_Park.jpg|alt=恩賀橋|Okagebashi Bridge File:Sando_of_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=参道|Sando File:Sanshuden_of_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=参集殿|Sanshuden File:Stela_in_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=石碑|Stela File:Stele_in_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=碑文|Stele File:TiranoSuemoto's_Stone_monument.jpg|alt=津軽末元の石碑|TiranoSuemoto's Stone Monument File:Torii_and_stairs_of_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=鳥居と階段|Torii and Stairs File:Torii_of_Kambashira_Shrine_from_inner_side.jpg|alt=内側からの鳥居|Torii from Inner Side File:Torii_of_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=鳥居|Torii File:Torpedo_in_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=魚雷|Torpedo File:基柱神社.jpg|alt=基柱神社|Kambashira Shrine File:基柱神社の扁額.jpg|alt=扁額|Shrine's name Plaque == [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox shinto shrine| map_type = Japan Miyazaki Prefecture#Japan|name=神柱宮|image=Kanbashira guu Miyakonojo Shaden.jpg|location=[[宮崎県]][[都城市]]前田町1417-1|coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|31|43|56.1|N|131|4|19.3|E|display=inline,title}}|deity=[[Amaterasu]]
[[Toyouke-hime]]|Rank=旧[[県社]]・[[別表神社]]|established=1026 {{ill|September 9 (Lunisolar)|ja|9月9日 (旧暦)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 9 (Lunisolar)|qq=}}|main hall=[[流造]]|alternative name=神柱神社|札所等=|festival={{ill|June lanterns|ja|六月灯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June lanterns|qq=}}（{{ill|Summer festival|ja|夏祭り|1=Natsu Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Summer festival|13=User:Immanuelle/Natsu Matsuri|qq=}}）|map={{location map|Japan Miyazaki Prefecture|lat_deg=31|lat_min=43|lat_sec=56.1|lon_deg=131|lon_min=4|lon_sec=19.3|label=神柱宮|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|31|43|56.1|N|131|4|19.3|E}}}}|infobox shinto shrine|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|caption=Shrine}} {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮|fr|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kanbashira-gū|en:draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanbashira-gū|qq=}} '''Shrine''' is [[Shinto shrine|a shrine]] located in [[:en:Miyakonojō|Miyakonojō]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]] . [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Its former Rank]] was that of [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], but it is currently [[Beppyo Shrine|a special shrine]] under [[Association of Shinto Shrines|the Association of Shinto Shrines]] {{Cite web|title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー|url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/|access-date=2023-04-02|website=jinja-gosyuin.com}}. It is also called '''Kanbashira Shrine''' . Commonly known as '''the sacred pillar''' . There are two [[:en:Setsumatsusha|sub-shrines]] within the grounds : {{nihongo|Mikado Shrine|神門神社|Mikado Jinja}} and {{nihongo|Motobashira Shrine|基柱神社|Motobashira Jinja}}. [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ごdeity｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 == deity == The main deities enshrined are [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Omikami]] and [[Toyouke-hime|Toyoukehime Okami]], and five other deities are enshrined in the side shrine: [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]] and [[:en:Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao]] no Mikoto on the left (right side when facing the shrine) and [[:en:Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane]], [[:en:Takuhadachiji-hime|Takuhadachiji-hime]], and [[Futodama|Ame-no-Tatamikoto]] on the right (left side when facing the shrine ). Additionally, the {{nihongo|Mikado Shrine|神門神社|Mikado Jinja}}, a sub-shrine within the grounds, enshrines {{ill|10=ja|11=天石門別神|Ama-no-Iwato-wake no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, while {{nihongo|Motobashira Shrine|基柱神社|Motobashira Jinja}} enshrines [[Sugawara no Michizane]] and {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} . [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ごdeity｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 == history == It is said that [[Oracle|the shrine]] was founded [[:en:1026|in 1026]] when {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, who had moved from [[:en:Dazaifu (government)|Dazaifu]] to develop [[:en:Shimazu Estate|Shimazu Estate]], received a divine revelation from Amaterasu Omikami that said, "A shrine should be quickly built in this place and the name of the shrine should be called Kanbashira," and so he [[:en:Bunrei|enshrined]] a shrine in Umekitamura Masutsuki from [[Ise Grand Shrine]] . Since then, it has been revered as the guardian deity of Shimazu Manor. [http://www.kanbashira.net/yuisho/index.html ご由緒｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 In [[Meiji period|May]] [[:en:1873|1873]], it was designated [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], and on October 28 of the same year, it was moved to its current location. The area between the current Ganzo-ji Temple (Maeda-cho) and the current location of Jinbashira Shrine was a field filled with many small pines, so it was called Komatsubara or Matsubara, and there was a villa of the Shimazu family of Miyakonojo called Matsubara Ochaya. Upon the relocation, the area around Matsubara Ochaya was developed into {{ill|10=ja|11=神柱公園|Kanbashira Park|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} . The shrine's former location was in {{ill|10=ja|11=梅北町|Masutsuki, Umekita-cho|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, within the city, and {{ill|10=ja|11=黒尾神社|Kuroo Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} has been relocated to its site. After the war, it joined the Association of Shinto Shrines and was designated a [[Beppyo Shrine]] on July 1, 1976 [[Shōwa period|.]] == Gallery == [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Its former Rank]] was that of [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], but it is currently [[Beppyo Shrine|a special shrine]] under [[Association of Shinto Shrines|the Association of Shinto Shrines]] {{Cite web|title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー|url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/|access-date=2023-04-02|website=jinja-gosyuin.com}}. It is also called '''Kanbashira Shrine''' . Commonly known as '''the sacred pillar''' . There are two [[:en:Setsumatsusha|sub-shrines]] within the grounds : {{nihongo|Mikado Shrine|神門神社|Mikado Jinja}} and {{nihongo|Motobashira Shrine|基柱神社|Motobashira Jinja}}. [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ごdeity｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 == deity == The main deities enshrined are [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Omikami]] and [[Toyouke-hime|Toyoukehime Okami]], and five other deities are enshrined in the side shrine: [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]] and [[:en:Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao]] no Mikoto on the left (right side when facing the shrine) and [[:en:Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane]], [[:en:Takuhadachiji-hime|Takuhadachiji-hime]], and [[Futodama|Ame-no-Tatamikoto]] on the right (left side when facing the shrine ). Additionally, the {{nihongo|Mikado Shrine|神門神社|Mikado Jinja}}, a sub-shrine within the grounds, enshrines {{ill|10=ja|11=天石門別神|Ama-no-Iwato-wake no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, while {{nihongo|Motobashira Shrine|基柱神社|Motobashira Jinja}} enshrines [[Sugawara no Michizane]] and {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} . [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ごdeity｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 == history == It is said that [[Oracle|the shrine]] was founded [[:en:1026|in 1026]] when {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, who had moved from [[:en:Dazaifu (government)|Dazaifu]] to develop [[:en:Shimazu Estate|Shimazu Estate]], received a divine revelation from Amaterasu Omikami that said, "A shrine should be quickly built in this place and the name of the shrine should be called Kanbashira," and so he [[:en:Bunrei|enshrined]] a shrine in Umekitamura Masutsuki from [[Ise Grand Shrine]] . Since then, it has been revered as the guardian deity of Shimazu Manor. [http://www.kanbashira.net/yuisho/index.html ご由緒｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 In [[Meiji period|May]] [[:en:1873|1873]], it was designated [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], and on October 28 of the same year, it was moved to its current location. The area between the current Ganzo-ji Temple (Maeda-cho) and the current location of Jinbashira Shrine was a field filled with many small pines, so it was called Komatsubara or Matsubara, and there was a villa of the Shimazu family of Miyakonojo called Matsubara Ochaya. Upon the relocation, the area around Matsubara Ochaya was developed into {{ill|10=ja|11=神柱公園|Kanbashira Park|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} . The shrine's former location was in {{ill|10=ja|11=梅北町|Masutsuki, Umekita-cho|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, within the city, and {{ill|10=ja|11=黒尾神社|Kuroo Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} has been relocated to its site. After the war, it joined the Association of Shinto Shrines and was designated a [[Beppyo Shrine]] on July 1, 1976 [[Shōwa period|.]] == Gallery == [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ごdeity｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 == history == It is said that [[Oracle|the shrine]] was founded [[:en:1026|in 1026]] when {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, who had moved from [[:en:Dazaifu (government)|Dazaifu]] to develop [[:en:Shimazu Estate|Shimazu Estate]], received a divine revelation from Amaterasu Omikami that said, "A shrine should be quickly built in this place and the name of the shrine should be called Kanbashira," and so he [[:en:Bunrei|enshrined]] a shrine in Umekitamura Masutsuki from [[Ise Grand Shrine]] . Since then, it has been revered as the guardian deity of Shimazu Manor. [http://www.kanbashira.net/yuisho/index.html ご由緒｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 In [[Meiji period|May]] [[:en:1873|1873]], it was designated [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], and on October 28 of the same year, it was moved to its current location. The area between the current Ganzo-ji Temple (Maeda-cho) and the current location of Jinbashira Shrine was a field filled with many small pines, so it was called Komatsubara or Matsubara, and there was a villa of the Shimazu family of Miyakonojo called Matsubara Ochaya. Upon the relocation, the area around Matsubara Ochaya was developed into {{ill|10=ja|11=神柱公園|Kanbashira Park|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} . The shrine's former location was in {{ill|10=ja|11=梅北町|Masutsuki, Umekita-cho|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, within the city, and {{ill|10=ja|11=黒尾神社|Kuroo Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} has been relocated to its site. After the war, it joined the Association of Shinto Shrines and was designated a [[Beppyo Shrine]] on July 1, 1976 [[Shōwa period|.]] == Gallery == revision.comment: Text replacement - "市の歴史" to "の Municipal History"
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:26, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Kanbashira-gū]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Kanbashira-gū]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox shinto shrine| map_type = Japan Miyazaki Prefecture#Japan|name=神柱宮|image=Kanbashira guu Miyakonojo Shaden.jpg|location=[[:en:宮崎県|宮崎県]][[:en:都城市|都城市]]前田町1417-1|coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|31|43|56.1|N|131|4|19.3|E|display=inline,title}}|deity=[[Amaterasu]]
[[Toyouke-hime]]|Rank=旧[[県社]]・[[別表神社]]|established=1026 {{ill|September 9 (Lunisolar)|ja|9月9日 (旧暦)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 9 (Lunisolar)|qq=}}|main hall=[[流造]]|alternative name=神柱神社|札所等=|festival={{ill|June lanterns|ja|六月灯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June lanterns|qq=}}（{{ill|Summer festival|ja|夏祭り|1=Natsu Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Summer festival|13=User:Immanuelle/Natsu Matsuri|qq=}}）|map={{location map|Japan Miyazaki Prefecture|lat_deg=31|lat_min=43|lat_sec=56.1|lon_deg=131|lon_min=4|lon_sec=19.3|label=神柱宮|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|31|43|56.1|N|131|4|19.3|E}}}}|infobox shinto shrine|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|caption=Shrine}} {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮|fr|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kanbashira-gū|en:draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanbashira-gū|qq=}} '''Shrine''' is [[Shinto shrine|a shrine]] located in [[:en:Miyakonojō|Miyakonojō]], [[:en:Miyazaki Prefecture|Miyazaki Prefecture]] . [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Its former Rank]] was that of [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], but it is currently [[Beppyo Shrine|a special shrine]] under [[Association of Shinto Shrines|the Association of Shinto Shrines]] {{Cite web|title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー|url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/|access-date=2023-04-02|website=jinja-gosyuin.com}}. It is also called '''Kanbashira Shrine''' . Commonly known as '''the sacred pillar''' . There are two [[:en:Setsumatsusha|sub-shrines]] within the grounds : {{nihongo|Mikado Shrine|神門神社|Mikado Jinja}} and {{nihongo|Motobashira Shrine|基柱神社|Motobashira Jinja}}. [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ごdeity｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 == deity == The main deities enshrined are [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Omikami]] and [[Toyouke-hime|Toyoukehime Okami]], and five other deities are enshrined in the side shrine: [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]] and [[:en:Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao]] no Mikoto on the left (right side when facing the shrine) and [[:en:Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane]], [[:en:Takuhadachiji-hime|Takuhadachiji-hime]], and [[Futodama|Ame-no-Tatamikoto]] on the right (left side when facing the shrine ). Additionally, the {{nihongo|Mikado Shrine|神門神社|Mikado Jinja}}, a sub-shrine within the grounds, enshrines {{ill|10=ja|11=天石門別神|Ama-no-Iwato-wake no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, while {{nihongo|Motobashira Shrine|基柱神社|Motobashira Jinja}} enshrines [[Sugawara no Michizane]] and {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} . [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ごdeity｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 == history == It is said that [[Oracle|the shrine]] was founded [[:en:1026|in 1026]] when {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, who had moved from [[:en:Dazaifu (government)|Dazaifu]] to develop [[:en:Shimazu Estate|Shimazu Estate]], received a divine revelation from Amaterasu Omikami that said, "A shrine should be quickly built in this place and the name of the shrine should be called Kanbashira," and so he [[:en:Bunrei|enshrined]] a shrine in Umekitamura Masutsuki from [[Ise Grand Shrine]] . Since then, it has been revered as the guardian deity of Shimazu Manor. [http://www.kanbashira.net/yuisho/index.html ご由緒｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 In [[Meiji period|May]] [[:en:1873|1873]], it was designated [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], and on October 28 of the same year, it was moved to its current location. The area between the current Ganzo-ji Temple (Maeda-cho) and the current location of Jinbashira Shrine was a field filled with many small pines, so it was called Komatsubara or Matsubara, and there was a villa of the Shimazu family of Miyakonojo called Matsubara Ochaya. Upon the relocation, the area around Matsubara Ochaya was developed into {{ill|10=ja|11=神柱公園|Kanbashira Park|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} . The shrine's former location was in {{ill|10=ja|11=梅北町|Masutsuki, Umekita-cho|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, within the city, and {{ill|10=ja|11=黒尾神社|Kuroo Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} has been relocated to its site. After the war, it joined the Association of Shinto Shrines and was designated a [[Beppyo Shrine]] on July 1, 1976 [[Shōwa period|.]] == Gallery == revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:26, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Kanbashira-gū]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Kanbashira-gū]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox shinto shrine| map_type = Japan Miyazaki Prefecture#Japan|name=神柱宮|image=Kanbashira guu Miyakonojo Shaden.jpg|location=[[:en:宮崎県|宮崎県]][[:en:都城市|都城市]]前田町1417-1|coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|31|43|56.1|N|131|4|19.3|E|display=inline,title}}|deity=[[Amaterasu]]
[[Toyouke-hime]]|Rank=旧[[県社]]・[[別表神社]]|established=1026 {{ill|September 9 (Lunisolar)|ja|9月9日 (旧暦)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 9 (Lunisolar)|qq=}}|main hall=[[流造]]|alternative name=神柱神社|札所等=|festival={{ill|June lanterns|ja|六月灯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June lanterns|qq=}}（{{ill|Summer festival|ja|夏祭り|1=Natsu Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Summer festival|13=User:Immanuelle/Natsu Matsuri|qq=}}）|map={{location map|Japan Miyazaki Prefecture|lat_deg=31|lat_min=43|lat_sec=56.1|lon_deg=131|lon_min=4|lon_sec=19.3|label=神柱宮|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|31|43|56.1|N|131|4|19.3|E}}}}|infobox shinto shrine|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|caption=Shrine}} {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮|fr|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kanbashira-gū|en:draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanbashira-gū|qq=}} '''Shrine''' is [[Shinto shrine|a shrine]] located in [[:en:Miyakonojō|Miyakonojō]], [[:en:Miyazaki Prefecture|Miyazaki Prefecture]] . [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Its former Rank]] was that of [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], but it is currently [[Beppyo Shrine|a special shrine]] under [[Association of Shinto Shrines|the Association of Shinto Shrines]] {{Cite web|title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー|url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/|access-date=2023-04-02|website=jinja-gosyuin.com}}. It is also called '''Kanbashira Shrine''' . Commonly known as '''the sacred pillar''' . There are two [[:en:Setsumatsusha|sub-shrines]] within the grounds : {{nihongo|Mikado Shrine|神門神社|Mikado Jinja}} and {{nihongo|Motobashira Shrine|基柱神社|Motobashira Jinja}}. [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ごdeity｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 == deity == The main deities enshrined are [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Omikami]] and [[Toyouke-hime|Toyoukehime Okami]], and five other deities are enshrined in the side shrine: [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]] and [[:en:Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao]] no Mikoto on the left (right side when facing the shrine) and [[:en:Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane]], [[:en:Takuhadachiji-hime|Takuhadachiji-hime]], and [[Futodama|Ame-no-Tatamikoto]] on the right (left side when facing the shrine ). Additionally, the {{nihongo|Mikado Shrine|神門神社|Mikado Jinja}}, a sub-shrine within the grounds, enshrines {{ill|10=ja|11=天石門別神|Ama-no-Iwato-wake no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, while {{nihongo|Motobashira Shrine|基柱神社|Motobashira Jinja}} enshrines [[Sugawara no Michizane]] and {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} . [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ごdeity｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 == history == It is said that [[Oracle|the shrine]] was founded [[:en:1026|in 1026]] when {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, who had moved from [[:en:Dazaifu (government)|Dazaifu]] to develop [[:en:Shimazu Estate|Shimazu Estate]], received a divine revelation from Amaterasu Omikami that said, "A shrine should be quickly built in this place and the name of the shrine should be called Kanbashira," and so he [[:en:Bunrei|enshrined]] a shrine in Umekitamura Masutsuki from [[Ise Grand Shrine]] . Since then, it has been revered as the guardian deity of Shimazu Manor. [http://www.kanbashira.net/yuisho/index.html ご由緒｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 In [[:en:Meiji era|May]] [[:en:1873|1873]], it was designated [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], and on October 28 of the same year, it was moved to its current location. The area between the current Ganzo-ji Temple (Maeda-cho) and the current location of Jinbashira Shrine was a field filled with many small pines, so it was called Komatsubara or Matsubara, and there was a villa of the Shimazu family of Miyakonojo called Matsubara Ochaya. Upon the relocation, the area around Matsubara Ochaya was developed into {{ill|10=ja|11=神柱公園|Kanbashira Park|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} . The shrine's former location was in {{ill|10=ja|11=梅北町|Masutsuki, Umekita-cho|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, within the city, and {{ill|10=ja|11=黒尾神社|Kuroo Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} has been relocated to its site. After the war, it joined the Association of Shinto Shrines and was designated a [[Beppyo Shrine]] on July 1, 1976 [[Shōwa period|.]] == Gallery == File:Okagebashi_Bridge_on_pond_in_Kambashira_Park.jpg|alt=恩賀橋|Okagebashi Bridge File:Sando_of_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=参道|Sando File:Sanshuden_of_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=参集殿|Sanshuden File:Stela_in_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=石碑|Stela File:Stele_in_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=碑文|Stele File:TiranoSuemoto's_Stone_monument.jpg|alt=津軽末元の石碑|TiranoSuemoto's Stone Monument File:Torii_of_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=鳥居|Torii File:Torpedo_in_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=魚雷|Torpedo File:基柱神社.jpg|alt=基柱神社|Kambashira Shrine File:基柱神社の扁額.jpg|alt=扁額|Shrine's name Plaque == revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:26, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Kanbashira-gū]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Kanbashira-gū]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox shinto shrine| map_type = Japan Miyazaki Prefecture#Japan|name=神柱宮|image=Kanbashira guu Miyakonojo Shaden.jpg|location=[[:en:宮崎県|宮崎県]][[:en:都城市|都城市]]前田町1417-1|coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|31|43|56.1|N|131|4|19.3|E|display=inline,title}}|deity=[[Amaterasu]]
[[Toyouke-hime]]|Rank=旧[[県社]]・[[別表神社]]|established=1026 {{ill|September 9 (Lunisolar)|ja|9月9日 (旧暦)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 9 (Lunisolar)|qq=}}|main hall=[[流造]]|alternative name=神柱神社|札所等=|festival={{ill|June lanterns|ja|六月灯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June lanterns|qq=}}（{{ill|Summer festival|ja|夏祭り|1=Natsu Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Summer festival|13=User:Immanuelle/Natsu Matsuri|qq=}}）|map={{location map|Japan Miyazaki Prefecture|lat_deg=31|lat_min=43|lat_sec=56.1|lon_deg=131|lon_min=4|lon_sec=19.3|label=神柱宮|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|31|43|56.1|N|131|4|19.3|E}}}}|infobox shinto shrine|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|caption=Shrine}} {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮|fr|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kanbashira-gū|en:draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanbashira-gū|qq=}} '''Shrine''' is [[Shinto shrine|a shrine]] located in [[:en:Miyakonojō|Miyakonojō]], [[:en:Miyazaki Prefecture|Miyazaki Prefecture]] . [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Its former Rank]] was that of [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], but it is currently [[Beppyo Shrine|a special shrine]] under [[Association of Shinto Shrines|the Association of Shinto Shrines]] {{Cite web|title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー|url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/|access-date=2023-04-02|website=jinja-gosyuin.com}}. It is also called '''Kanbashira Shrine''' . Commonly known as '''the sacred pillar''' . There are two [[:en:Setsumatsusha|sub-shrines]] within the grounds : {{nihongo|Mikado Shrine|神門神社|Mikado Jinja}} and {{nihongo|Motobashira Shrine|基柱神社|Motobashira Jinja}}. [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ごdeity｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 == deity == The main deities enshrined are [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Omikami]] and [[Toyouke-hime|Toyoukehime Okami]], and five other deities are enshrined in the side shrine: [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]] and [[:en:Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao]] no Mikoto on the left (right side when facing the shrine) and [[:en:Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane]], [[:en:Takuhadachiji-hime|Takuhadachiji-hime]], and [[Futodama|Ame-no-Tatamikoto]] on the right (left side when facing the shrine ). Additionally, the {{nihongo|Mikado Shrine|神門神社|Mikado Jinja}}, a sub-shrine within the grounds, enshrines {{ill|10=ja|11=天石門別神|Ama-no-Iwato-wake no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, while {{nihongo|Motobashira Shrine|基柱神社|Motobashira Jinja}} enshrines [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]] and {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} . [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ごdeity｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 == history == It is said that [[:en:Oracle|the shrine]] was founded [[:en:1026|in 1026]] when {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, who had moved from [[:en:Dazaifu (government)|Dazaifu]] to develop [[:en:Shimazu Estate|Shimazu Estate]], received a divine revelation from Amaterasu Omikami that said, "A shrine should be quickly built in this place and the name of the shrine should be called Kanbashira," and so he [[:en:Bunrei|enshrined]] a shrine in Umekitamura Masutsuki from [[Ise Grand Shrine]] . Since then, it has been revered as the guardian deity of Shimazu Manor. [http://www.kanbashira.net/yuisho/index.html ご由緒｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 In [[:en:Meiji era|May]] [[:en:1873|1873]], it was designated [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], and on October 28 of the same year, it was moved to its current location. The area between the current Ganzo-ji Temple (Maeda-cho) and the current location of Jinbashira Shrine was a field filled with many small pines, so it was called Komatsubara or Matsubara, and there was a villa of the Shimazu family of Miyakonojo called Matsubara Ochaya. Upon the relocation, the area around Matsubara Ochaya was developed into {{ill|10=ja|11=神柱公園|Kanbashira Park|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} . The shrine's former location was in {{ill|10=ja|11=梅北町|Masutsuki, Umekita-cho|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, within the city, and {{ill|10=ja|11=黒尾神社|Kuroo Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} has been relocated to its site. After the war, it joined the Association of Shinto Shrines and was designated a [[Beppyo Shrine]] on July 1, 1976 [[Shōwa period|.]] == Gallery == File:Chozuya_and_Sanshuden_Hall_of_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=手水舎と参集殿|Chozuya and Sanshuden Hall File:Ginkgo_biloba_in_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=銀杏|Ginkgo biloba File:Kanbashira_guu_Miyakonojo_Shaden.jpg|alt=宮之城社殿|Shaden in Miyakonojo File:Motobashira_Shrine_in_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=元柱神社|Motobashira Shrine File:Okagebashi_Bridge_on_pond_in_Kambashira_Park.jpg|alt=恩賀橋|Okagebashi Bridge File:Sando_of_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=参道|Sando File:Sanshuden_of_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=参集殿|Sanshuden File:Stela_in_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=石碑|Stela File:Stele_in_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=碑文|Stele File:TiranoSuemoto's_Stone_monument.jpg|alt=津軽末元の石碑|TiranoSuemoto's Stone Monument File:Torii_and_stairs_of_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=鳥居と階段|Torii and Stairs File:Torii_of_Kambashira_Shrine_from_inner_side.jpg|alt=内側からの鳥居|Torii from Inner Side File:Torii_of_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=鳥居|Torii File:Torpedo_in_Kambashira_Shrine.jpg|alt=魚雷|Torpedo File:基柱神社.jpg|alt=基柱神社|Kambashira Shrine File:基柱神社の扁額.jpg|alt=扁額|Shrine's name Plaque == [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyouke-hime]]|Rank=旧[[県社]]・[[別表神社]]|established=1026 {{ill|September 9 (Lunisolar)|ja|9月9日 (旧暦)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 9 (Lunisolar)|qq=}}|main hall=[[流造]]|alternative name=神柱神社|札所等=|festival={{ill|June lanterns|ja|六月灯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June lanterns|qq=}}（{{ill|Summer festival|ja|夏祭り|1=Natsu Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Summer festival|13=User:Immanuelle/Natsu Matsuri|qq=}}）|map={{location map|Japan Miyazaki Prefecture|lat_deg=31|lat_min=43|lat_sec=56.1|lon_deg=131|lon_min=4|lon_sec=19.3|label=神柱宮|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|31|43|56.1|N|131|4|19.3|E}}}}|infobox shinto shrine|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|caption=Shrine}} {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮|fr|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kanbashira-gū|en:draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanbashira-gū|qq=}} '''Shrine''' is [[:en:Shinto shrine|a shrine]] located in [[:en:Miyakonojō|Miyakonojō]], [[:en:Miyazaki Prefecture|Miyazaki Prefecture]] . [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Its former Rank]] was that of [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], but it is currently [[Beppyo Shrine|a special shrine]] under [[Association of Shinto Shrines|the Association of Shinto Shrines]] {{Cite web|title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー|url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/|access-date=2023-04-02|website=jinja-gosyuin.com}}. It is also called '''Kanbashira Shrine''' . Commonly known as '''the sacred pillar''' . There are two [[:en:Setsumatsusha|sub-shrines]] within the grounds : {{nihongo|Mikado Shrine|神門神社|Mikado Jinja}} and {{nihongo|Motobashira Shrine|基柱神社|Motobashira Jinja}}. [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ごdeity｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 == deity == The main deities enshrined are [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Omikami]] and [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyoukehime Okami]], and five other deities are enshrined in the side shrine: [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]] and [[:en:Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao]] no Mikoto on the left (right side when facing the shrine) and [[:en:Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane]], [[:en:Takuhadachiji-hime|Takuhadachiji-hime]], and [[Futodama|Ame-no-Tatamikoto]] on the right (left side when facing the shrine ). Additionally, the {{nihongo|Mikado Shrine|神門神社|Mikado Jinja}}, a sub-shrine within the grounds, enshrines {{ill|10=ja|11=天石門別神|Ama-no-Iwato-wake no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, while {{nihongo|Motobashira Shrine|基柱神社|Motobashira Jinja}} enshrines [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]] and {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} . [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ごdeity｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 == history == It is said that [[:en:Oracle|the shrine]] was founded [[:en:1026|in 1026]] when {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, who had moved from [[:en:Dazaifu (government)|Dazaifu]] to develop [[:en:Shimazu Estate|Shimazu Estate]], received a divine revelation from Amaterasu Omikami that said, "A shrine should be quickly built in this place and the name of the shrine should be called Kanbashira," and so he [[:en:Bunrei|enshrined]] a shrine in Umekitamura Masutsuki from [[Ise Grand Shrine]] . Since then, it has been revered as the guardian deity of Shimazu Manor. [http://www.kanbashira.net/yuisho/index.html ご由緒｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 In [[:en:Meiji era|May]] [[:en:1873|1873]], it was designated [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], and on October 28 of the same year, it was moved to its current location. The area between the current Ganzo-ji Temple (Maeda-cho) and the current location of Jinbashira Shrine was a field filled with many small pines, so it was called Komatsubara or Matsubara, and there was a villa of the Shimazu family of Miyakonojo called Matsubara Ochaya. Upon the relocation, the area around Matsubara Ochaya was developed into {{ill|10=ja|11=神柱公園|Kanbashira Park|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} . The shrine's former location was in {{ill|10=ja|11=梅北町|Masutsuki, Umekita-cho|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, within the city, and {{ill|10=ja|11=黒尾神社|Kuroo Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} has been relocated to its site. After the war, it joined the Association of Shinto Shrines and was designated a [[Beppyo Shrine]] on July 1, 1976 [[Shōwa period|.]] == Gallery == [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyouke-hime]]|Rank=旧[[県社]]・[[別表神社]]|established=1026 {{ill|September 9 (Lunisolar)|ja|9月9日 (旧暦)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 9 (Lunisolar)|qq=}}|main hall=[[流造]]|alternative name=神柱神社|札所等=|festival={{ill|June lanterns|ja|六月灯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June lanterns|qq=}}（{{ill|Summer festival|ja|夏祭り|1=Natsu Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Summer festival|13=User:Immanuelle/Natsu Matsuri|qq=}}）|map={{location map|Japan Miyazaki Prefecture|lat_deg=31|lat_min=43|lat_sec=56.1|lon_deg=131|lon_min=4|lon_sec=19.3|label=神柱宮|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|31|43|56.1|N|131|4|19.3|E}}}}|infobox shinto shrine|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|caption=Shrine}} {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮|fr|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kanbashira-gū|en:draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanbashira-gū|qq=}} '''Shrine''' is [[:en:Shinto shrine|a shrine]] located in [[:en:Miyakonojō|Miyakonojō]], [[:en:Miyazaki Prefecture|Miyazaki Prefecture]] . [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Its former Rank]] was that of [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], but it is currently [[Beppyo Shrine|a special shrine]] under [[Association of Shinto Shrines|the Association of Shinto Shrines]] {{Cite web|title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー|url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/|access-date=2023-04-02|website=jinja-gosyuin.com}}. It is also called '''Kanbashira Shrine''' . Commonly known as '''the sacred pillar''' . There are two [[:en:Setsumatsusha|sub-shrines]] within the grounds : {{nihongo|Mikado Shrine|神門神社|Mikado Jinja}} and {{nihongo|Motobashira Shrine|基柱神社|Motobashira Jinja}}. [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ごdeity｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 == deity == The main deities enshrined are [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Omikami]] and [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyoukehime Okami]], and five other deities are enshrined in the side shrine: [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]] and [[:en:Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao]] no Mikoto on the left (right side when facing the shrine) and [[:en:Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane]], [[:en:Takuhadachiji-hime|Takuhadachiji-hime]], and [[Futodama|Ame-no-Tatamikoto]] on the right (left side when facing the shrine ). Additionally, the {{nihongo|Mikado Shrine|神門神社|Mikado Jinja}}, a sub-shrine within the grounds, enshrines {{ill|10=ja|11=天石門別神|Ama-no-Iwato-wake no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, while {{nihongo|Motobashira Shrine|基柱神社|Motobashira Jinja}} enshrines [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]] and {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} . [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ごdeity｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 == history == It is said that [[:en:Oracle|the shrine]] was founded [[:en:1026|in 1026]] when {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, who had moved from [[:en:Dazaifu (government)|Dazaifu]] to develop [[:en:Shimazu Estate|Shimazu Estate]], received a divine revelation from Amaterasu Omikami that said, "A shrine should be quickly built in this place and the name of the shrine should be called Kanbashira," and so he [[:en:Bunrei|enshrined]] a shrine in Umekitamura Masutsuki from [[Ise Grand Shrine]] . Since then, it has been revered as the guardian deity of Shimazu Manor. [http://www.kanbashira.net/yuisho/index.html ご由緒｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 In [[:en:Meiji era|May]] [[:en:1873|1873]], it was designated [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], and on October 28 of the same year, it was moved to its current location. The area between the current Ganzo-ji Temple (Maeda-cho) and the current location of Jinbashira Shrine was a field filled with many small pines, so it was called Komatsubara or Matsubara, and there was a villa of the Shimazu family of Miyakonojo called Matsubara Ochaya. Upon the relocation, the area around Matsubara Ochaya was developed into {{ill|10=ja|11=神柱公園|Kanbashira Park|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} . The shrine's former location was in {{ill|10=ja|11=梅北町|Masutsuki, Umekita-cho|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, within the city, and {{ill|10=ja|11=黒尾神社|Kuroo Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} has been relocated to its site. After the war, it joined the Association of Shinto Shrines and was designated a [[Beppyo Shrine]] on July 1, 1976 [[:en:Shōwa era|.]] == Gallery == [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyouke-hime]]|Rank=旧[[県社]]・[[別表神社]]|established=1026 {{ill|September 9 (Lunisolar)|ja|9月9日 (旧暦)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 9 (Lunisolar)|qq=}}|main hall=[[流造]]|alternative name=神柱神社|札所等=|festival={{ill|June lanterns|ja|六月灯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June lanterns|qq=|1=June lanterns|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16144215}}（{{ill|Summer festival|ja|夏祭り|1=Natsu Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Summer festival|13=User:Immanuelle/Natsu Matsuri|qq=}}）|map={{location map|Japan Miyazaki Prefecture|lat_deg=31|lat_min=43|lat_sec=56.1|lon_deg=131|lon_min=4|lon_sec=19.3|label=神柱宮|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|31|43|56.1|N|131|4|19.3|E}}}}|infobox shinto shrine|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|caption=Shrine}} {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮|fr|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kanbashira-gū|en:draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanbashira-gū|qq=}} '''Shrine''' is [[:en:Shinto shrine|a shrine]] located in [[:en:Miyakonojō|Miyakonojō]], [[:en:Miyazaki Prefecture|Miyazaki Prefecture]] . [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Its former Rank]] was that of [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], but it is currently [[Beppyo Shrine|a special shrine]] under [[Association of Shinto Shrines|the Association of Shinto Shrines]] {{Cite web|title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー|url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/|access-date=2023-04-02|website=jinja-gosyuin.com}}. It is also called '''Kanbashira Shrine''' . Commonly known as '''the sacred pillar''' . There are two [[:en:Setsumatsusha|sub-shrines]] within the grounds : {{nihongo|Mikado Shrine|神門神社|Mikado Jinja}} and {{nihongo|Motobashira Shrine|基柱神社|Motobashira Jinja}}. [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ごdeity｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 == deity == The main deities enshrined are [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Omikami]] and [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyoukehime Okami]], and five other deities are enshrined in the side shrine: [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]] and [[:en:Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao]] no Mikoto on the left (right side when facing the shrine) and [[:en:Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane]], [[:en:Takuhadachiji-hime|Takuhadachiji-hime]], and [[Futodama|Ame-no-Tatamikoto]] on the right (left side when facing the shrine ). Additionally, the {{nihongo|Mikado Shrine|神門神社|Mikado Jinja}}, a sub-shrine within the grounds, enshrines {{ill|10=ja|11=天石門別神|Ama-no-Iwato-wake no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, while {{nihongo|Motobashira Shrine|基柱神社|Motobashira Jinja}} enshrines [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]] and {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} . [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ごdeity｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 == history == It is said that [[:en:Oracle|the shrine]] was founded [[:en:1026|in 1026]] when {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, who had moved from [[:en:Dazaifu (government)|Dazaifu]] to develop [[:en:Shimazu Estate|Shimazu Estate]], received a divine revelation from Amaterasu Omikami that said, "A shrine should be quickly built in this place and the name of the shrine should be called Kanbashira," and so he [[:en:Bunrei|enshrined]] a shrine in Umekitamura Masutsuki from [[Ise Grand Shrine]] . Since then, it has been revered as the guardian deity of Shimazu Manor. [http://www.kanbashira.net/yuisho/index.html ご由緒｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 In [[:en:Meiji era|May]] [[:en:1873|1873]], it was designated [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], and on October 28 of the same year, it was moved to its current location. The area between the current Ganzo-ji Temple (Maeda-cho) and the current location of Jinbashira Shrine was a field filled with many small pines, so it was called Komatsubara or Matsubara, and there was a villa of the Shimazu family of Miyakonojo called Matsubara Ochaya. Upon the relocation, the area around Matsubara Ochaya was developed into {{ill|10=ja|11=神柱公園|Kanbashira Park|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|1=Kanbashira Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590365}} . The shrine's former location was in {{ill|10=ja|11=梅北町|Masutsuki, Umekita-cho|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, within the city, and {{ill|10=ja|11=黒尾神社|Kuroo Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|1=Kuroo-jinja|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q107410082}} has been relocated to its site. After the war, it joined the Association of Shinto Shrines and was designated a [[Beppyo Shrine]] on July 1, 1976 [[:en:Shōwa era|.]] == Gallery == [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyouke-hime]]|Rank=旧[[県社]]・[[別表神社]]|established=1026 {{ill|September 9 (Lunisolar)|ja|9月9日 (旧暦)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 9 (Lunisolar)|qq=}}|main hall=[[流造]]|alternative name=神柱神社|札所等=|festival={{ill|June lanterns|ja|六月灯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June lanterns|qq=|1=June lanterns|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16144215}}（{{ill|Summer festival|ja|夏祭り|1=Summer Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Summer festival|13=User:Immanuelle/Summer Matsuri|qq=}}）|map={{location map|Japan Miyazaki Prefecture|lat_deg=31|lat_min=43|lat_sec=56.1|lon_deg=131|lon_min=4|lon_sec=19.3|label=神柱宮|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|31|43|56.1|N|131|4|19.3|E}}}}|infobox shinto shrine|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|caption=Shrine}} {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮|fr|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kanbashira-gū|en:draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanbashira-gū|qq=}} '''Shrine''' is [[:en:Shinto shrine|a shrine]] located in [[:en:Miyakonojō|Miyakonojō]], [[:en:Miyazaki Prefecture|Miyazaki Prefecture]] . [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Its former Rank]] was that of [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], but it is currently [[Beppyo Shrine|a special shrine]] under [[Association of Shinto Shrines|the Association of Shinto Shrines]] {{Cite web|title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー|url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/|access-date=2023-04-02|website=jinja-gosyuin.com}}. It is also called '''Kanbashira Shrine''' . Commonly known as '''the sacred pillar''' . There are two [[:en:Setsumatsusha|sub-shrines]] within the grounds : {{nihongo|Mikado Shrine|神門神社|Mikado Jinja}} and {{nihongo|Motobashira Shrine|基柱神社|Motobashira Jinja}}. [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ごdeity｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 == deity == The main deities enshrined are [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Omikami]] and [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyoukehime Okami]], and five other deities are enshrined in the side shrine: [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]] and [[:en:Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao]] no Mikoto on the left (right side when facing the shrine) and [[:en:Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane]], [[:en:Takuhadachiji-hime|Takuhadachiji-hime]], and [[Futodama|Ame-no-Tatamikoto]] on the right (left side when facing the shrine ). Additionally, the {{nihongo|Mikado Shrine|神門神社|Mikado Jinja}}, a sub-shrine within the grounds, enshrines {{ill|10=ja|11=天石門別神|Ama-no-Iwato-wake no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, while {{nihongo|Motobashira Shrine|基柱神社|Motobashira Jinja}} enshrines [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]] and {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} . [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ごdeity｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 == history == It is said that [[:en:Oracle|the shrine]] was founded [[:en:1026|in 1026]] when {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, who had moved from [[:en:Dazaifu (government)|Dazaifu]] to develop [[:en:Shimazu Estate|Shimazu Estate]], received a divine revelation from Amaterasu Omikami that said, "A shrine should be quickly built in this place and the name of the shrine should be called Kanbashira," and so he [[:en:Bunrei|enshrined]] a shrine in Umekitamura Masutsuki from [[Ise Grand Shrine]] . Since then, it has been revered as the guardian deity of Shimazu Manor. [http://www.kanbashira.net/yuisho/index.html ご由緒｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 In [[:en:Meiji era|May]] [[:en:1873|1873]], it was designated [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], and on October 28 of the same year, it was moved to its current location. The area between the current Ganzo-ji Temple (Maeda-cho) and the current location of Jinbashira Shrine was a field filled with many small pines, so it was called Komatsubara or Matsubara, and there was a villa of the Shimazu family of Miyakonojo called Matsubara Ochaya. Upon the relocation, the area around Matsubara Ochaya was developed into {{ill|10=ja|11=神柱公園|Kanbashira Park|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|1=Kanbashira Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590365}} . The shrine's former location was in {{ill|10=ja|11=梅北町|Masutsuki, Umekita-cho|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, within the city, and {{ill|10=ja|11=黒尾神社|Kuroo Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|1=Kuroo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q107410082}} has been relocated to its site. After the war, it joined the Association of Shinto Shrines and was designated a [[Beppyo Shrine]] on July 1, 1976 [[:en:Shōwa era|.]] == Gallery == [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyouke-hime]]|Rank=旧[[県社]]・[[別表神社]]|established=1026 {{ill|September 9 (Lunisolar)|ja|9月9日 (旧暦)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 9 (Lunisolar)|qq=}}|main hall=[[流造]]|alternative name=神柱神社|札所等=|festival={{ill|June lanterns|ja|六月灯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June lanterns|qq=|1=June lanterns|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16144215}}（{{ill|Summer festival|ja|夏祭り|1=Summer Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Summer festival|13=User:Immanuelle/Summer Matsuri|qq=}}）|map={{location map|Japan Miyazaki Prefecture|lat_deg=31|lat_min=43|lat_sec=56.1|lon_deg=131|lon_min=4|lon_sec=19.3|label=神柱宮|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|31|43|56.1|N|131|4|19.3|E}}}}|infobox shinto shrine|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|caption=Shrine}} {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮|fr|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kanbashira-gū|en:draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanbashira-gū|qq=}} '''Shrine''' is [[:en:Shinto shrine|a shrine]] located in [[:en:Miyakonojō|Miyakonojō]], [[:en:Miyazaki Prefecture|Miyazaki Prefecture]] . [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Its former Rank]] was that of [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], but it is currently [[Beppyo Shrine|a special shrine]] under [[Association of Shinto Shrines|the Association of Shinto Shrines]] {{Cite web|title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー|url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/|access-date=2023-04-02|website=jinja-gosyuin.com}}. It is also called '''Kanbashira Shrine''' . Commonly known as '''the sacred pillar''' . There are two [[:en:Setsumatsusha|sub-shrines]] within the grounds : {{nihongo|Mikado Shrine|神門神社|Mikado Jinja}} and {{nihongo|Motobashira Shrine|基柱神社|Motobashira Jinja}}. [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ごdeity｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 == deity == The main deities enshrined are [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Omikami]] and [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyoukehime Okami]], and five other deities are enshrined in the side shrine: [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]] and [[:en:Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao]] no Mikoto on the left (right side when facing the shrine) and [[:en:Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane]], [[:en:Takuhadachiji-hime|Takuhadachiji-hime]], and [[Futodama|Ame-no-Tatamikoto]] on the right (left side when facing the shrine ). Additionally, the {{nihongo|Mikado Shrine|神門神社|Mikado Jinja}}, a sub-shrine within the grounds, enshrines {{ill|10=ja|11=天石門別神|Ama-no-Iwato-wake no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, while {{nihongo|Motobashira Shrine|基柱神社|Motobashira Jinja}} enshrines [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]] and {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} . [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ごdeity｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 == history == It is said that [[:en:Oracle|the shrine]] was founded [[:en:1026|in 1026]] when {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, who had moved from [[:en:Dazaifu (government)|Dazaifu]] to develop [[:en:Shimazu Estate|Shimazu Estate]], received a divine revelation from Amaterasu Omikami that said, "A shrine should be quickly built in this place and the name of the shrine should be called Kanbashira," and so he [[:en:Bunrei|enshrined]] a shrine in Umekitamura Masutsuki from [[Ise Grand Shrine]] . Since then, it has been revered as the guardian deity of Shimazu Manor. [http://www.kanbashira.net/yuisho/index.html ご由緒｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 In [[:en:Meiji era|May]] [[:en:1873|1873]], it was designated [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], and on October 28 of the same year, it was moved to its current location. The area between the current Ganzo-ji Temple (Maeda-cho) and the current location of Jinbashira Shrine was a field filled with many small pines, so it was called Komatsubara or Matsubara, and there was a villa of the Shimazu family of Miyakonojo called Matsubara Ochaya. Upon the relocation, the area around Matsubara Ochaya was developed into {{ill|10=ja|11=神柱公園|Kanbashira Park|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|1=Kanbashira Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590365}} . The shrine's former location was in {{ill|10=ja|11=梅北町|Masutsuki, Umekita-cho|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, within the city, and {{ill|10=ja|11=黒尾神社|Kuroo Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|1=Kuroo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q107410082}} has been relocated to its site. After the war, it joined the Association of Shinto Shrines and was designated a [[Beppyo Shrine]] on July 1, 1976 [[:en:Shōwa era|.]] == Gallery == [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyouke-hime]]|Rank=旧[[県社]]・[[別表神社]]|established=1026 {{ill|September 9 (Lunisolar)|ja|9月9日 (旧暦)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 9 (Lunisolar)|qq=}}|main hall=[[流造]]|alternative name=神柱神社|札所等=|festival={{ill|June lanterns|ja|六月灯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June lanterns|qq=|1=June lanterns|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16144215}}（{{ill|Summer festival|ja|夏祭り|1=Summer Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Summer festival|13=User:Immanuelle/Summer Matsuri|qq=}}）|map={{location map|Japan Miyazaki Prefecture|lat_deg=31|lat_min=43|lat_sec=56.1|lon_deg=131|lon_min=4|lon_sec=19.3|label=神柱宮|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|31|43|56.1|N|131|4|19.3|E}}}}|infobox shinto shrine|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|caption=Shrine}} {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮|fr|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kanbashira-gū|en:draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanbashira-gū|qq=}} '''Shrine''' is [[:en:Shinto shrine|a shrine]] located in [[:en:Miyakonojō|Miyakonojō]], [[:en:Miyazaki Prefecture|Miyazaki Prefecture]] . [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Its former Rank]] was that of [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], but it is currently [[Beppyo Shrine|a special shrine]] under [[Association of Shinto Shrines|the Association of Shinto Shrines]] {{Cite web|title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー|url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/|access-date=2023-04-02|website=jinja-gosyuin.com}}. It is also called '''Kanbashira Shrine''' . Commonly known as '''the sacred pillar''' . There are two [[:en:Setsumatsusha|sub-shrines]] within the grounds : {{nihongo|Mikado Shrine|神門神社|Mikado Jinja}} and {{nihongo|Motobashira Shrine|基柱神社|Motobashira Jinja}}. [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ごdeity｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 == deity == The main deities enshrined are [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Omikami]] and [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyoukehime Okami]], and five other deities are enshrined in the side shrine: [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]] and [[:en:Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao]] no Mikoto on the left (right side when facing the shrine) and [[:en:Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane]], [[:en:Takuhadachiji-hime|Takuhadachiji-hime]], and [[Futodama|Ame-no-Tatamikoto]] on the right (left side when facing the shrine ). Additionally, the {{nihongo|Mikado Shrine|神門神社|Mikado Jinja}}, a sub-shrine within the grounds, enshrines {{ill|10=ja|11=天石門別神|Ama-no-Iwato-wake no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, while {{nihongo|Motobashira Shrine|基柱神社|Motobashira Jinja}} enshrines [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]] and {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} . [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ごdeity｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 == history == It is said that [[:en:Oracle|the shrine]] was founded [[:en:1026|in 1026]] when {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, who had moved from [[:en:Dazaifu (government)|Dazaifu]] to develop [[:en:Shimazu Estate|Shimazu Estate]], received a divine revelation from Amaterasu Omikami that said, "A shrine should be quickly built in this place and the name of the shrine should be called Kanbashira," and so he [[:en:Bunrei|enshrined]] a shrine in Umekitamura Masutsuki from [[Ise Grand Shrine]] . Since then, it has been revered as the guardian deity of Shimazu Manor. [http://www.kanbashira.net/yuisho/index.html ご由緒｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 In [[:en:Meiji era|May]] [[:en:1873|1873]], it was designated [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], and on October 28 of the same year, it was moved to its current location. The area between the current Ganzo-ji Temple (Maeda-cho) and the current location of Jinbashira Shrine was a field filled with many small pines, so it was called Komatsubara or Matsubara, and there was a villa of the Shimazu family of Miyakonojo called Matsubara Ochaya. Upon the relocation, the area around Matsubara Ochaya was developed into {{ill|10=ja|11=神柱公園|Kanbashira Park|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|1=Kanbashira Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590365}} . The shrine's former location was in {{ill|10=ja|11=梅北町|Masutsuki, Umekita-cho|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, within the city, and {{ill|10=ja|11=黒尾神社|Kuroo Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|1=Kuroo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q107410082}} has been relocated to its site. After the war, it joined the Association of Shinto Shrines and was designated a [[Beppyo Shrine]] on July 1, 1976 [[:en:Shōwa era|.]] == Gallery ==
