[[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyouke-hime]]|Rank=旧[[県社]]・[[別表神社]]|established=1026 {{ill|September 9 (Lunisolar)|ja|9月9日 (旧暦)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 9 (Lunisolar)|qq=}}|main hall=[[流造]]|alternative name=神柱神社|札所等=|festival={{ill|June lanterns|ja|六月灯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June lanterns|qq=|1=June lanterns|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16144215}}（{{ill|Summer festival|ja|夏祭り|1=Summer Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Summer festival|13=User:Immanuelle/Summer Matsuri|qq=}}）|map={{location map|Japan Miyazaki Prefecture|lat_deg=31|lat_min=43|lat_sec=56.1|lon_deg=131|lon_min=4|lon_sec=19.3|label=神柱宮|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|31|43|56.1|N|131|4|19.3|E}}}}|infobox shinto shrine|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|caption=Shrine}} {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮|fr|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kanbashira-gū|en:draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanbashira-gū|qq=}} '''Shrine''' is [[:en:Shinto shrine|a shrine]] located in [[:en:Miyakonojō|Miyakonojō]], [[:en:Miyazaki Prefecture|Miyazaki Prefecture]] . [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Its former Rank]] was that of [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], but it is currently [[Beppyo Shrine|a special shrine]] under [[Association of Shinto Shrines|the Association of Shinto Shrines]] {{Cite web|title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー|url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/|access-date=2023-04-02|website=jinja-gosyuin.com}}. It is also called '''Kanbashira Shrine''' . Commonly known as '''the sacred pillar''' . There are two [[:en:Setsumatsusha|sub-shrines]] within the grounds : {{nihongo|Mikado Shrine|神門神社|Mikado Jinja}} and {{nihongo|Motobashira Shrine|基柱神社|Motobashira Jinja}}. [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ごdeity｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 == deity == The main deities enshrined are [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Omikami]] and [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyoukehime Okami]], and five other deities are enshrined in the side shrine: [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]] and [[:en:Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao]] no Mikoto on the left (right side when facing the shrine) and [[:en:Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane]], [[:en:Takuhadachiji-hime|Takuhadachiji-hime]], and [[Futodama|Ame-no-Tatamikoto]] on the right (left side when facing the shrine ). Additionally, the {{nihongo|Mikado Shrine|神門神社|Mikado Jinja}}, a sub-shrine within the grounds, enshrines {{ill|10=ja|11=天石門別神|Ama-no-Iwato-wake no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, while {{nihongo|Motobashira Shrine|基柱神社|Motobashira Jinja}} enshrines [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]] and {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} . [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ごdeity｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 == history == It is said that [[:en:Oracle|the shrine]] was founded [[:en:1026|in 1026]] when {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, who had moved from [[:en:Dazaifu (government)|Dazaifu]] to develop [[:en:Shimazu Estate|Shimazu Estate]], received a divine revelation from Amaterasu Omikami that said, "A shrine should be quickly built in this place and the name of the shrine should be called Kanbashira," and so he [[:en:Bunrei|enshrined]] a shrine in Umekitamura Masutsuki from [[Ise Grand Shrine]] . Since then, it has been revered as the guardian deity of Shimazu Manor. [http://www.kanbashira.net/yuisho/index.html ご由緒｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 In [[:en:Meiji era|May]] [[:en:1873|1873]], it was designated [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], and on October 28 of the same year, it was moved to its current location. The area between the current Ganzo-ji Temple (Maeda-cho) and the current location of Jinbashira Shrine was a field filled with many small pines, so it was called Komatsubara or Matsubara, and there was a villa of the Shimazu family of Miyakonojo called Matsubara Ochaya. Upon the relocation, the area around Matsubara Ochaya was developed into {{ill|10=ja|11=神柱公園|Kanbashira Park|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|1=Kanbashira Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590365}} . The shrine's former location was in {{ill|10=ja|11=梅北町|Masutsuki, Umekita-cho|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, within the city, and {{ill|10=ja|11=黒尾神社|Kuroo Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|1=Kuroo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q107410082}} has been relocated to its site. After the war, it joined the Association of Shinto Shrines and was designated a [[Beppyo Shrine]] on July 1, 1976 [[:en:Shōwa era|.]] == Gallery == [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyouke-hime]]|Rank=旧[[県社]]・[[別表神社]]|established=1026 {{ill|September 9 (Lunisolar)|ja|9月9日 (旧暦)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 9 (Lunisolar)|qq=}}|main hall=[[流造]]|alternative name=神柱神社|札所等=|festival={{ill|June lanterns|ja|六月灯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June lanterns|qq=|1=June lanterns|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16144215}}（{{ill|Summer festival|ja|夏祭り|1=Summer Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Summer festival|13=User:Immanuelle/Summer Matsuri|qq=}}）|map={{location map|Japan Miyazaki Prefecture|lat_deg=31|lat_min=43|lat_sec=56.1|lon_deg=131|lon_min=4|lon_sec=19.3|label=神柱宮|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|31|43|56.1|N|131|4|19.3|E}}}}|infobox shinto shrine|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|caption=Shrine}} {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮|fr|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kanbashira-gū|en:draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanbashira-gū|qq=}} '''Shrine''' is [[:en:Shinto shrine|a shrine]] located in [[:en:Miyakonojō|Miyakonojō]], [[:en:Miyazaki Prefecture|Miyazaki Prefecture]] . [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Its former Rank]] was that of [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], but it is currently [[Beppyo Shrine|a special shrine]] under [[Association of Shinto Shrines|the Association of Shinto Shrines]] {{Cite web|title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー|url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/|access-date=2023-04-02|website=jinja-gosyuin.com}}. It is also called '''Kanbashira Shrine''' . Commonly known as '''the sacred pillar''' . There are two [[:en:Setsumatsusha|sub-shrines]] within the grounds : {{nihongo|Mikado Shrine|神門神社|Mikado Jinja}} and {{nihongo|Motobashira Shrine|基柱神社|Motobashira Jinja}}. [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ごdeity｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 == deity == The main deities enshrined are [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Omikami]] and [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyoukehime Okami]], and five other deities are enshrined in the side shrine: [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]] and [[:en:Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao]] no Mikoto on the left (right side when facing the shrine) and [[:en:Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane]], [[:en:Takuhadachiji-hime|Takuhadachiji-hime]], and [[Futodama|Ame-no-Tatamikoto]] on the right (left side when facing the shrine ). Additionally, the {{nihongo|Mikado Shrine|神門神社|Mikado Jinja}}, a sub-shrine within the grounds, enshrines {{ill|10=ja|11=天石門別神|Ama-no-Iwato-wake no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, while {{nihongo|Motobashira Shrine|基柱神社|Motobashira Jinja}} enshrines [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]] and {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} . [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ごdeity｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 == history == It is said that [[:en:Oracle|the shrine]] was founded [[:en:1026|in 1026]] when {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, who had moved from [[:en:Dazaifu (government)|Dazaifu]] to develop [[:en:Shimazu Estate|Shimazu Estate]], received a divine revelation from Amaterasu Omikami that said, "A shrine should be quickly built in this place and the name of the shrine should be called Kanbashira," and so he [[:en:Bunrei|enshrined]] a shrine in Umekitamura Masutsuki from [[Ise Grand Shrine]] . Since then, it has been revered as the guardian deity of Shimazu Manor. [http://www.kanbashira.net/yuisho/index.html ご由緒｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 In [[:en:Meiji era|May]] [[:en:1873|1873]], it was designated [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], and on October 28 of the same year, it was moved to its current location. The area between the current Ganzo-ji Temple (Maeda-cho) and the current location of Jinbashira Shrine was a field filled with many small pines, so it was called Komatsubara or Matsubara, and there was a villa of the Shimazu family of Miyakonojo called Matsubara Ochaya. Upon the relocation, the area around Matsubara Ochaya was developed into {{ill|10=ja|11=神柱公園|Kanbashira Park|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|1=Kanbashira Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590365}} . The shrine's former location was in {{ill|10=ja|11=梅北町|Masutsuki, Umekita-cho|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, within the city, and {{ill|10=ja|11=黒尾神社|Kuroo Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|1=Kuroo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q107410082}} has been relocated to its site. After the war, it joined the Association of Shinto Shrines and was designated a [[Beppyo Shrine]] on July 1, 1976 [[:en:Shōwa era|.]] == Gallery == [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ごdeity｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 == history == It is said that [[:en:Oracle|the shrine]] was founded [[:en:1026|in 1026]] when {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, who had moved from [[:en:Dazaifu (government)|Dazaifu]] to develop [[:en:Shimazu Estate|Shimazu Estate]], received a divine revelation from Amaterasu Omikami that said, "A shrine should be quickly built in this place and the name of the shrine should be called Kanbashira," and so he [[:en:Bunrei|enshrined]] a shrine in Umekitamura Masutsuki from [[Ise Grand Shrine]] . [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyouke-hime]]|Rank=旧[[県社]]・[[別表神社]]|established=1026 {{ill|September 9 (Lunisolar)|ja|9月9日 (旧暦)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 9 (Lunisolar)|qq=}}|main hall=[[流造]]|alternative name=神柱神社|札所等=|festival={{ill|June lanterns|ja|六月灯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June lanterns|qq=|1=June lanterns|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16144215}}（{{ill|Summer festival|ja|夏祭り|1=Summer Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Summer festival|13=User:Immanuelle/Summer Matsuri|qq=}}）|map={{location map|Japan Miyazaki Prefecture|lat_deg=31|lat_min=43|lat_sec=56.1|lon_deg=131|lon_min=4|lon_sec=19.3|label=神柱宮|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|31|43|56.1|N|131|4|19.3|E}}}}|infobox shinto shrine|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|caption=Shrine}} {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮|fr|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kanbashira-gū|en:draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanbashira-gū|qq=}} '''Shrine''' is [[:en:Shinto shrine|a shrine]] located in [[:en:Miyakonojō|Miyakonojō]], [[:en:Miyazaki Prefecture|Miyazaki Prefecture]] . [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Its former Rank]] was that of [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], but it is currently [[Beppyo Shrine|a special shrine]] under [[Association of Shinto Shrines|the Association of Shinto Shrines]] {{Cite web|title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー|url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/|access-date=2023-04-02|website=jinja-gosyuin.com}}. It is also called '''Kanbashira Shrine''' . Commonly known as '''the sacred pillar''' . There are two [[:en:Setsumatsusha|sub-shrines]] within the grounds : {{nihongo|Mikado Shrine|神門神社|Mikado Jinja}} and {{nihongo|Motobashira Shrine|基柱神社|Motobashira Jinja}}. [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ごdeity｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 == deity == The main deities enshrined are [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Omikami]] and [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyoukehime Okami]], and five other deities are enshrined in the side shrine: [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]] and [[:en:Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao]] no Mikoto on the left (right side when facing the shrine) and [[:en:Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane]], [[:en:Takuhadachiji-hime|Takuhadachiji-hime]], and [[Futodama|Ame-no-Tatamikoto]] on the right (left side when facing the shrine ). Additionally, the {{nihongo|Mikado Shrine|神門神社|Mikado Jinja}}, a sub-shrine within the grounds, enshrines {{ill|10=ja|11=天石門別神|Ama-no-Iwato-wake no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, while {{nihongo|Motobashira Shrine|基柱神社|Motobashira Jinja}} enshrines [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]] and {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} . [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ごdeity｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 == history == It is said that [[:en:Oracle|the shrine]] was founded [[:en:1026|in 1026]] when {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, who had moved from [[:en:Dazaifu (government)|Dazaifu]] to develop [[:en:Shimazu Estate|Shimazu Estate]], received a divine revelation from Amaterasu Omikami that said, "A shrine should be quickly built in this place and the name of the shrine should be called Kanbashira," and so he [[:en:Bunrei|enshrined]] a shrine in Umekitamura Masutsuki from [[Ise Grand Shrine]] . Since then, it has been revered as the guardian deity of Shimazu Manor. [http://www.kanbashira.net/yuisho/index.html ご由緒｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 In [[:en:Meiji era|May]] [[:en:1873|1873]], it was designated [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], and on October 28 of the same year, it was moved to its current location. The area between the current Ganzo-ji Temple (Maeda-cho) and the current location of Jinbashira Shrine was a field filled with many small pines, so it was called Komatsubara or Matsubara, and there was a villa of the Shimazu family of Miyakonojo called Matsubara Ochaya. Upon the relocation, the area around Matsubara Ochaya was developed into {{ill|10=ja|11=神柱公園|Kanbashira Park|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|1=Kanbashira Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590365}} . The shrine's former location was in {{ill|10=ja|11=梅北町|Masutsuki, Umekita-cho|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, within the city, and {{ill|10=ja|11=黒尾神社|Kuroo Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|1=Kuroo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q107410082}} has been relocated to its site. After the war, it joined the Association of Shinto Shrines and was designated a [[Beppyo Shrine]] on July 1, 1976 [[:en:Shōwa era|.]] == Gallery == [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyouke-hime]]|Rank=旧[[県社]]・[[別表神社]]|established=1026 {{ill|September 9 (Lunisolar)|ja|9月9日 (旧暦)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 9 (Lunisolar)|qq=}}|main hall=[[流造]]|alternative name=神柱神社|札所等=|festival={{ill|June lanterns|ja|六月灯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June lanterns|qq=|1=June lanterns|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16144215}}（{{ill|Summer festival|ja|夏祭り|1=Summer Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Summer festival|13=User:Immanuelle/Summer Matsuri|qq=}}）|map={{location map|Japan Miyazaki Prefecture|lat_deg=31|lat_min=43|lat_sec=56.1|lon_deg=131|lon_min=4|lon_sec=19.3|label=神柱宮|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|31|43|56.1|N|131|4|19.3|E}}}}|infobox shinto shrine|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|caption=Shrine}} {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮|fr|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kanbashira-gū|en:draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanbashira-gū|qq=}} '''Shrine''' is [[:en:Shinto shrine|a shrine]] located in [[:en:Miyakonojō|Miyakonojō]], [[:en:Miyazaki Prefecture|Miyazaki Prefecture]] . [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Its former Rank]] was that of [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], but it is currently [[Beppyo Shrine|a special shrine]] under [[Association of Shinto Shrines|the Association of Shinto Shrines]] {{Cite web|title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー|url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/|access-date=2023-04-02|website=jinja-gosyuin.com}}. It is also called '''Kanbashira Shrine''' . Commonly known as '''the sacred pillar''' . There are two [[:en:Setsumatsusha|sub-shrines]] within the grounds : {{nihongo|Mikado Shrine|神門神社|Mikado Jinja}} and {{nihongo|Motobashira Shrine|基柱神社|Motobashira Jinja}}. [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ごdeity｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 == deity == The main deities enshrined are [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Omikami]] and [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyoukehime Okami]], and five other deities are enshrined in the side shrine: [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]] and [[:en:Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao]] no Mikoto on the left (right side when facing the shrine) and [[:en:Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane]], [[:en:Takuhadachiji-hime|Takuhadachiji-hime]], and [[Futodama|Ame-no-Tatamikoto]] on the right (left side when facing the shrine ). Additionally, the {{nihongo|Mikado Shrine|神門神社|Mikado Jinja}}, a sub-shrine within the grounds, enshrines {{ill|10=ja|11=天石門別神|Ama-no-Iwato-wake no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, while {{nihongo|Motobashira Shrine|基柱神社|Motobashira Jinja}} enshrines [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]] and {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} . [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ごdeity｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 == history == It is said that [[:en:Oracle|the shrine]] was founded [[:en:1026|in 1026]] when {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, who had moved from [[:en:Dazaifu (government)|Dazaifu]] to develop [[:en:Shimazu Estate|Shimazu Estate]], received a divine revelation from Amaterasu Omikami that said, "A shrine should be quickly built in this place and the name of the shrine should be called Kanbashira," and so he [[:en:Bunrei|enshrined]] a shrine in Umekitamura Masutsuki from [[Ise Grand Shrine]] . Since then, it has been revered as the guardian deity of Shimazu Manor. [http://www.kanbashira.net/yuisho/index.html ご由緒｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 In [[:en:Meiji era|May]] [[:en:1873|1873]], it was designated [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], and on October 28 of the same year, it was moved to its current location. The area between the current Ganzo-ji Temple (Maeda-cho) and the current location of Jinbashira Shrine was a field filled with many small pines, so it was called Komatsubara or Matsubara, and there was a villa of the Shimazu family of Miyakonojo called Matsubara Ochaya. Upon the relocation, the area around Matsubara Ochaya was developed into {{ill|10=ja|11=神柱公園|Kanbashira Park|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|1=Kanbashira Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590365}} . The shrine's former location was in {{ill|10=ja|11=梅北町|Masutsuki, Umekita-cho|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, within the city, and {{ill|10=ja|11=黒尾神社|Kuroo Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|1=Kuroo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q107410082}} has been relocated to its site. After the war, it joined the Association of Shinto Shrines and was designated a [[Beppyo Shrine]] on July 1, 1976 [[:en:Shōwa era|.]] == Gallery == [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyouke-hime]]|Rank=旧[[県社]]・[[別表神社]]|established=1026 {{ill|September 9 (Lunisolar)|ja|9月9日 (旧暦)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 9 (Lunisolar)|qq=}}|main hall=[[流造]]|alternative name=神柱神社|札所等=|festival={{ill|June lanterns|ja|六月灯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June lanterns|qq=|1=June lanterns|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16144215}}（{{ill|Summer festival|ja|夏祭り|1=Summer Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Summer festival|13=User:Immanuelle/Summer Matsuri|qq=}}）|map={{location map|Japan Miyazaki Prefecture|lat_deg=31|lat_min=43|lat_sec=56.1|lon_deg=131|lon_min=4|lon_sec=19.3|label=神柱宮|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|31|43|56.1|N|131|4|19.3|E}}}}|infobox shinto shrine|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|caption=Shrine}} {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮|fr|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kanbashira-gū|en:draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanbashira-gū|qq=}} '''Shrine''' is [[:en:Shinto shrine|a shrine]] located in [[:en:Miyakonojō|Miyakonojō]], [[:en:Miyazaki Prefecture|Miyazaki Prefecture]] . [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Its former Rank]] was that of [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], but it is currently [[Beppyo Shrine|a special shrine]] under [[Association of Shinto Shrines|the Association of Shinto Shrines]] {{Cite web|title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー|url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/|access-date=2023-04-02|website=jinja-gosyuin.com}}. It is also called '''Kanbashira Shrine''' . Commonly known as '''the sacred pillar''' . There are two [[:en:Setsumatsusha|sub-shrines]] within the grounds : {{nihongo|Mikado Shrine|神門神社|Mikado Jinja}} and {{nihongo|Motobashira Shrine|基柱神社|Motobashira Jinja}}. [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ごdeity｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 == deity == The main deities enshrined are [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Omikami]] and [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyoukehime Okami]], and five other deities are enshrined in the side shrine: [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]] and [[:en:Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao]] no Mikoto on the left (right side when facing the shrine) and [[:en:Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane]], [[:en:Takuhadachiji-hime|Takuhadachiji-hime]], and [[Futodama|Ame-no-Tatamikoto]] on the right (left side when facing the shrine ). Additionally, the {{nihongo|Mikado Shrine|神門神社|Mikado Jinja}}, a sub-shrine within the grounds, enshrines {{ill|10=ja|11=天石門別神|Ama-no-Iwato-wake no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, while {{nihongo|Motobashira Shrine|基柱神社|Motobashira Jinja}} enshrines [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]] and {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} . [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ごdeity｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 == history == It is said that [[:en:Oracle|the shrine]] was founded [[:en:1026|in 1026]] when {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, who had moved from [[:en:Dazaifu (government)|Dazaifu]] to develop [[:en:Shimazu Estate|Shimazu Estate]], received a divine revelation from Amaterasu Omikami that said, "A shrine should be quickly built in this place and the name of the shrine should be called Kanbashira," and so he [[:en:Bunrei|enshrined]] a shrine in Umekitamura Masutsuki from [[Ise Grand Shrine]] . Since then, it has been revered as the guardian deity of Shimazu Manor. [http://www.kanbashira.net/yuisho/index.html ご由緒｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 In [[:en:Meiji era|May]] [[:en:1873|1873]], it was designated [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], and on October 28 of the same year, it was moved to its current location. The area between the current Ganzo-ji Temple (Maeda-cho) and the current location of Jinbashira Shrine was a field filled with many small pines, so it was called Komatsubara or Matsubara, and there was a villa of the Shimazu family of Miyakonojo called Matsubara Ochaya. Upon the relocation, the area around Matsubara Ochaya was developed into {{ill|10=ja|11=神柱公園|Kanbashira Park|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|1=Kanbashira Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590365}} . The shrine's former location was in {{ill|10=ja|11=梅北町|Masutsuki, Umekita-cho|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, within the city, and {{ill|10=ja|11=黒尾神社|Kuroo Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|1=Kuroo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q107410082}} has been relocated to its site. After the war, it joined the Association of Shinto Shrines and was designated a [[Beppyo Shrine]] on July 1, 1976 [[:en:Shōwa era|.]] == Gallery == [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyouke-hime]]|Rank=旧[[県社]]・[[別表神社]]|established=1026 {{ill|September 9 (Lunisolar)|ja|9月9日 (旧暦)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 9 (Lunisolar)|qq=}}|main hall=[[流造]]|alternative name=神柱神社|札所等=|festival={{ill|June lanterns|ja|六月灯|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June lanterns|1=June lanterns|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16144215}}（{{ill|Summer festival|ja|夏祭り|1=Summer Matsuri|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Summer festival|13=User:Immanuelle/Summer Matsuri|qq=}}）|map={{location map|Japan Miyazaki Prefecture|lat_deg=31|lat_min=43|lat_sec=56.1|lon_deg=131|lon_min=4|lon_sec=19.3|label=神柱宮|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|31|43|56.1|N|131|4|19.3|E}}}}|infobox shinto shrine|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|caption=Shrine}} {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮|fr|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kanbashira-gū|en:draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanbashira-gū|qq=}} '''Shrine''' is [[:en:Shinto shrine|a shrine]] located in [[:en:Miyakonojō|Miyakonojō]], [[:en:Miyazaki Prefecture|Miyazaki Prefecture]] . [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Its former Rank]] was that of [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], but it is currently [[Beppyo Shrine|a special shrine]] under [[Association of Shinto Shrines|the Association of Shinto Shrines]] {{Cite web|title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー|url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/|access-date=2023-04-02|website=jinja-gosyuin.com}}. It is also called '''Kanbashira Shrine''' . Commonly known as '''the sacred pillar''' . There are two [[:en:Setsumatsusha|sub-shrines]] within the grounds : {{nihongo|Mikado Shrine|神門神社|Mikado Jinja}} and {{nihongo|Motobashira Shrine|基柱神社|Motobashira Jinja}}. [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ごdeity｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 == deity == The main deities enshrined are [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Omikami]] and [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyoukehime Okami]], and five other deities are enshrined in the side shrine: [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]] and [[:en:Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao]] no Mikoto on the left (right side when facing the shrine) and [[:en:Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane]], [[:en:Takuhadachiji-hime|Takuhadachiji-hime]], and [[Futodama|Ame-no-Tatamikoto]] on the right (left side when facing the shrine ). Additionally, the {{nihongo|Mikado Shrine|神門神社|Mikado Jinja}}, a sub-shrine within the grounds, enshrines {{ill|10=ja|11=天石門別神|Ama-no-Iwato-wake no Kami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, while {{nihongo|Motobashira Shrine|基柱神社|Motobashira Jinja}} enshrines [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]] and {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} . [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ごdeity｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 == history == It is said that [[:en:Oracle|the shrine]] was founded [[:en:1026|in 1026]] when {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, who had moved from [[:en:Dazaifu (government)|Dazaifu]] to develop [[:en:Shimazu Estate|Shimazu Estate]], received a divine revelation from Amaterasu Omikami that said, "A shrine should be quickly built in this place and the name of the shrine should be called Kanbashira," and so he [[:en:Bunrei|enshrined]] a shrine in Umekitamura Masutsuki from [[Ise Grand Shrine]] . Since then, it has been revered as the guardian deity of Shimazu Manor. [http://www.kanbashira.net/yuisho/index.html ご由緒｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 In [[:en:Meiji era|May]] [[:en:1873|1873]], it was designated [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], and on October 28 of the same year, it was moved to its current location. The area between the current Ganzo-ji Temple (Maeda-cho) and the current location of Jinbashira Shrine was a field filled with many small pines, so it was called Komatsubara or Matsubara, and there was a villa of the Shimazu family of Miyakonojo called Matsubara Ochaya. Upon the relocation, the area around Matsubara Ochaya was developed into {{ill|10=ja|11=神柱公園|Kanbashira Park|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|1=Kanbashira Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590365}} . The shrine's former location was in {{ill|10=ja|11=梅北町|Masutsuki, Umekita-cho|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, within the city, and {{ill|10=ja|11=黒尾神社|Kuroo Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|1=Kuroo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q107410082}} has been relocated to its site. After the war, it joined the Association of Shinto Shrines and was designated a [[Beppyo Shrine]] on July 1, 1976 [[:en:Shōwa era|.]] == Gallery == [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyouke-hime]]|Rank=旧[[県社]]・[[別表神社]]|established=1026 {{ill|September 9 (Lunisolar)|ja|9月9日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 9 (Lunisolar)|qq=}}|main hall=[[流造]]|alternative name=神柱神社|札所等=|festival={{ill|June lanterns|ja|六月灯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June lanterns|1=June lanterns|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16144215}}（{{ill|Summer festival|ja|夏祭り|1=Summer Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Summer festival|13=User:Immanuelle/Summer Matsuri|qq=}}）|map={{location map|Japan Miyazaki Prefecture|lat_deg=31|lat_min=43|lat_sec=56.1|lon_deg=131|lon_min=4|lon_sec=19.3|label=神柱宮|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|31|43|56.1|N|131|4|19.3|E}}}}|infobox shinto shrine|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|caption=Shrine}} {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮|fr|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kanbashira-gū|en:draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanbashira-gū|qq=}} '''Shrine''' is [[:en:Shinto shrine|a shrine]] located in [[:en:Miyakonojō|Miyakonojō]], [[:en:Miyazaki Prefecture|Miyazaki Prefecture]] . [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Its former Rank]] was that of [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], but it is currently [[Beppyo Shrine|a special shrine]] under [[Association of Shinto Shrines|the Association of Shinto Shrines]] {{Cite web|title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー|url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/|access-date=2023-04-02|website=jinja-gosyuin.com}}. It is also called '''Kanbashira Shrine''' . Commonly known as '''the sacred pillar''' . There are two [[:en:Setsumatsusha|sub-shrines]] within the grounds : {{nihongo|Mikado Shrine|神門神社|Mikado Jinja}} and {{nihongo|Motobashira Shrine|基柱神社|Motobashira Jinja}}. [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ごdeity｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 == deity == The main deities enshrined are [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Omikami]] and [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyoukehime Okami]], and five other deities are enshrined in the side shrine: [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]] and [[:en:Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao]] no Mikoto on the left (right side when facing the shrine) and [[:en:Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane]], [[:en:Takuhadachiji-hime|Takuhadachiji-hime]], and [[Futodama|Ame-no-Tatamikoto]] on the right (left side when facing the shrine ). Additionally, the {{nihongo|Mikado Shrine|神門神社|Mikado Jinja}}, a sub-shrine within the grounds, enshrines {{ill|10=ja|11=天石門別神|Ama-no-Iwato-wake no Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, while {{nihongo|Motobashira Shrine|基柱神社|Motobashira Jinja}} enshrines [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]] and {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} . [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ごdeity｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 == history == It is said that [[:en:Oracle|the shrine]] was founded [[:en:1026|in 1026]] when {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, who had moved from [[:en:Dazaifu (government)|Dazaifu]] to develop [[:en:Shimazu Estate|Shimazu Estate]], received a divine revelation from Amaterasu Omikami that said, "A shrine should be quickly built in this place and the name of the shrine should be called Kanbashira," and so he [[:en:Bunrei|enshrined]] a shrine in Umekitamura Masutsuki from [[Ise Grand Shrine]] . Since then, it has been revered as the guardian deity of Shimazu Manor. [http://www.kanbashira.net/yuisho/index.html ご由緒｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 In [[:en:Meiji era|May]] [[:en:1873|1873]], it was designated [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], and on October 28 of the same year, it was moved to its current location. The area between the current Ganzo-ji Temple (Maeda-cho) and the current location of Jinbashira Shrine was a field filled with many small pines, so it was called Komatsubara or Matsubara, and there was a villa of the Shimazu family of Miyakonojo called Matsubara Ochaya. Upon the relocation, the area around Matsubara Ochaya was developed into {{ill|10=ja|11=神柱公園|Kanbashira Park|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|1=Kanbashira Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590365}} . The shrine's former location was in {{ill|10=ja|11=梅北町|Masutsuki, Umekita-cho|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, within the city, and {{ill|10=ja|11=黒尾神社|Kuroo Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|1=Kuroo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q107410082}} has been relocated to its site. After the war, it joined the Association of Shinto Shrines and was designated a [[Beppyo Shrine]] on July 1, 1976 [[:en:Shōwa era|.]] == Gallery == [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyouke-hime]]|Rank=旧[[県社]]・[[別表神社]]|established=1026 {{ill|September 9 (Lunisolar)|ja|9月9日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 9 (Lunisolar)|qq=}}|main hall=[[流造]]|alternative name=神柱神社|札所等=|festival={{ill|June lanterns|ja|六月灯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June lanterns|1=June lanterns|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16144215}}（{{ill|Summer festival|ja|夏祭り|1=Summer Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Summer festival|13=User:Immanuelle/Summer Matsuri|qq=}}）|map={{location map|Japan Miyazaki Prefecture|lat_deg=31|lat_min=43|lat_sec=56.1|lon_deg=131|lon_min=4|lon_sec=19.3|label=神柱宮|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|31|43|56.1|N|131|4|19.3|E}}}}|infobox shinto shrine|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|caption=Shrine}} {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮|fr|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kanbashira-gū|en:draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanbashira-gū|qq=}} '''Shrine''' is [[:en:Shinto shrine|a shrine]] located in [[:en:Miyakonojō|Miyakonojō]], [[:en:Miyazaki Prefecture|Miyazaki Prefecture]] . [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Its former Rank]] was that of [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], but it is currently [[Beppyo Shrine|a special shrine]] under [[Association of Shinto Shrines|the Association of Shinto Shrines]] {{Cite web|title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー|url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/|access-date=2023-04-02|website=jinja-gosyuin.com}}. It is also called '''Kanbashira Shrine''' . Commonly known as '''the sacred pillar''' . There are two [[:en:Setsumatsusha|sub-shrines]] within the grounds : {{nihongo|Mikado Shrine|神門神社|Mikado Jinja}} and {{nihongo|Motobashira Shrine|基柱神社|Motobashira Jinja}}. [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ごdeity｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 == deity == The main deities enshrined are [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Omikami]] and [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyoukehime Okami]], and five other deities are enshrined in the side shrine: [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]] and [[:en:Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao]] no Mikoto on the left (right side when facing the shrine) and [[:en:Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane]], [[:en:Takuhadachiji-hime|Takuhadachiji-hime]], and [[Futodama|Ame-no-Tatamikoto]] on the right (left side when facing the shrine ). Additionally, the {{nihongo|Mikado Shrine|神門神社|Mikado Jinja}}, a sub-shrine within the grounds, enshrines {{ill|10=ja|11=天石門別神|Ama-no-Iwato-wake no Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, while {{nihongo|Motobashira Shrine|基柱神社|Motobashira Jinja}} enshrines [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]] and {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} . [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ごdeity｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 == history == It is said that [[:en:Oracle|the shrine]] was founded [[:en:1026|in 1026]] when {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, who had moved from [[:en:Dazaifu (government)|Dazaifu]] to develop [[:en:Shimazu Estate|Shimazu Estate]], received a divine revelation from Amaterasu Omikami that said, "A shrine should be quickly built in this place and the name of the shrine should be called Kanbashira," and so he [[:en:Bunrei|enshrined]] a shrine in Umekitamura Masutsuki from [[Ise Grand Shrine]] . Since then, it has been revered as the guardian deity of Shimazu Manor. [http://www.kanbashira.net/yuisho/index.html ご由緒｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 In [[:en:Meiji era|May]] [[:en:1873|1873]], it was designated [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], and on October 28 of the same year, it was moved to its current location. The area between the current Ganzo-ji Temple (Maeda-cho) and the current location of Jinbashira Shrine was a field filled with many small pines, so it was called Komatsubara or Matsubara, and there was a villa of the Shimazu family of Miyakonojo called Matsubara Ochaya. Upon the relocation, the area around Matsubara Ochaya was developed into {{ill|10=ja|11=神柱公園|Kanbashira Park|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|1=Kanbashira Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590365}} . The shrine's former location was in {{ill|10=ja|11=梅北町|Masutsuki, Umekita-cho|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, within the city, and {{ill|10=ja|11=黒尾神社|Kuroo Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|1=Kuroo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q107410082}} has been relocated to its site. After the war, it joined the Association of Shinto Shrines and was designated a [[Beppyo Shrine]] on July 1, 1976 [[:en:Shōwa era|.]] == Gallery == [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyouke-hime]]|Rank=旧[[県社]]・[[別表神社]]|established=1026 {{ill|September 9 (Lunisolar)|ja|9月9日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 9 (Lunisolar)|qq=}}|main hall=[[流造]]|alternative name=神柱神社|札所等=|festival={{ill|June lanterns|ja|六月灯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June lanterns|1=June lanterns|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16144215}}（{{ill|Summer festival|ja|夏祭り|1=Summer Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Summer festival|13=User:Immanuelle/Summer Matsuri|qq=}}）|map={{location map|Japan Miyazaki Prefecture|lat_deg=31|lat_min=43|lat_sec=56.1|lon_deg=131|lon_min=4|lon_sec=19.3|label=神柱宮|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|31|43|56.1|N|131|4|19.3|E}}}}|infobox shinto shrine|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|caption=Shrine}} {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮|fr|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kanbashira-gū|en:draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanbashira-gū|qq=}} '''Shrine''' is [[:en:Shinto shrine|a shrine]] located in [[:en:Miyakonojō|Miyakonojō]], [[:en:Miyazaki Prefecture|Miyazaki Prefecture]] . [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Its former Rank]] was that of [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], but it is currently [[Beppyo Shrine|a special shrine]] under [[Association of Shinto Shrines|the Association of Shinto Shrines]] {{Cite web|title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー|url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/|access-date=2023-04-02|website=jinja-gosyuin.com}}. It is also called '''Kanbashira Shrine''' . Commonly known as '''the sacred pillar''' . There are two [[:en:Setsumatsusha|sub-shrines]] within the grounds : {{nihongo|Mikado Shrine|神門神社|Mikado Jinja}} and {{nihongo|Motobashira Shrine|基柱神社|Motobashira Jinja}}. [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ごdeity｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 == deity == The main deities enshrined are [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Omikami]] and [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyoukehime Okami]], and five other deities are enshrined in the side shrine: [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]] and [[:en:Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao]] no Mikoto on the left (right side when facing the shrine) and [[:en:Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane]], [[:en:Takuhadachiji-hime|Takuhadachiji-hime]], and [[Futodama|Ame-no-Tatamikoto]] on the right (left side when facing the shrine ). Additionally, the {{nihongo|Mikado Shrine|神門神社|Mikado Jinja}}, a sub-shrine within the grounds, enshrines {{ill|10=ja|11=天石門別神|Ama-no-Iwato-wake no Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, while {{nihongo|Motobashira Shrine|基柱神社|Motobashira Jinja}} enshrines [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]] and {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} . [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ごdeity｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 == history == It is said that [[:en:Oracle|the shrine]] was founded [[:en:1026|in 1026]] when {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, who had moved from [[:en:Dazaifu (government)|Dazaifu]] to develop [[:en:Shimazu Estate|Shimazu Estate]], received a divine revelation from Amaterasu Omikami that said, "A shrine should be quickly built in this place and the name of the shrine should be called Kanbashira," and so he [[:en:Bunrei|enshrined]] a shrine in Umekitamura Masutsuki from [[Ise Grand Shrine]] . Since then, it has been revered as the guardian deity of Shimazu Manor. [http://www.kanbashira.net/yuisho/index.html ご由緒｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 In [[:en:Meiji era|May]] [[:en:1873|1873]], it was designated [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], and on October 28 of the same year, it was moved to its current location. The area between the current Ganzo-ji Temple (Maeda-cho) and the current location of Jinbashira Shrine was a field filled with many small pines, so it was called Komatsubara or Matsubara, and there was a villa of the Shimazu family of Miyakonojo called Matsubara Ochaya. Upon the relocation, the area around Matsubara Ochaya was developed into {{ill|10=ja|11=神柱公園|Kanbashira Park|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|1=Kanbashira Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590365}} . The shrine's former location was in {{ill|10=ja|11=梅北町|Masutsuki, Umekita-cho|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, within the city, and {{ill|10=ja|11=黒尾神社|Kuroo Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|1=Kuroo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q107410082}} has been relocated to its site. After the war, it joined the Association of Shinto Shrines and was designated a [[Beppyo Shrine]] on July 1, 1976 [[:en:Shōwa era|.]] == Gallery == [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyouke-hime]]|Rank=旧[[県社]]・[[別表神社]]|established=1026 {{ill|September 9 (Lunisolar)|ja|9月9日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 9 (Lunisolar)|qq=}}|main hall=[[流造]]|alternative name=神柱神社|札所等=|festival={{ill|June lanterns|ja|六月灯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June lanterns|1=June lanterns|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16144215}}（{{ill|Summer festival|ja|夏祭り|1=Summer Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Summer festival|13=User:Immanuelle/Summer Matsuri|qq=}}）|map={{location map|Japan Miyazaki Prefecture|lat_deg=31|lat_min=43|lat_sec=56.1|lon_deg=131|lon_min=4|lon_sec=19.3|label=神柱宮|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|31|43|56.1|N|131|4|19.3|E}}}}|infobox shinto shrine|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|caption=Shrine}} {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮|fr|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kanbashira-gū|en:draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanbashira-gū|qq=}} '''Shrine''' is [[:en:Shinto shrine|a shrine]] located in [[:en:Miyakonojō|Miyakonojō]], [[:en:Miyazaki Prefecture|Miyazaki Prefecture]] . [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Its former Rank]] was that of [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], but it is currently [[Beppyo Shrine|a special shrine]] under [[Association of Shinto Shrines|the Association of Shinto Shrines]] {{Cite web|title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー|url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/|access-date=2023-04-02|website=jinja-gosyuin.com}}. It is also called '''Kanbashira Shrine''' . Commonly known as '''the sacred pillar''' . There are two [[:en:Setsumatsusha|sub-shrines]] within the grounds : {{nihongo|Mikado Shrine|神門神社|Mikado Jinja}} and {{nihongo|Motobashira Shrine|基柱神社|Motobashira Jinja}}. [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ごdeity｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 == deity == The main deities enshrined are [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Omikami]] and [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyoukehime Okami]], and five other deities are enshrined in the side shrine: [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]] and [[:en:Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao]] no Mikoto on the left (right side when facing the shrine) and [[:en:Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane]], [[:en:Takuhadachiji-hime|Takuhadachiji-hime]], and [[Futodama|Ame-no-Tatamikoto]] on the right (left side when facing the shrine ). Additionally, the {{nihongo|Mikado Shrine|神門神社|Mikado Jinja}}, a sub-shrine within the grounds, enshrines {{ill|10=ja|11=天石門別神|Ama-no-Iwato-wake no Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, while {{nihongo|Motobashira Shrine|基柱神社|Motobashira Jinja}} enshrines [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]] and {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} . [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ごdeity｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 == history == It is said that [[:en:Oracle|the shrine]] was founded [[:en:1026|in 1026]] when {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, who had moved from [[:en:Dazaifu (government)|Dazaifu]] to develop [[:en:Shimazu Estate|Shimazu Estate]], received a divine revelation from Amaterasu Omikami that said, "A shrine should be quickly built in this place and the name of the shrine should be called Kanbashira," and so he [[:en:Bunrei|enshrined]] a shrine in Umekitamura Masutsuki from [[Ise Grand Shrine]] . Since then, it has been revered as the guardian deity of Shimazu Manor. [http://www.kanbashira.net/yuisho/index.html ご由緒｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 In [[:en:Meiji era|May]] [[:en:1873|1873]], it was designated [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], and on October 28 of the same year, it was moved to its current location. The area between the current Ganzo-ji Temple (Maeda-cho) and the current location of Jinbashira Shrine was a field filled with many small pines, so it was called Komatsubara or Matsubara, and there was a villa of the Shimazu family of Miyakonojo called Matsubara Ochaya. Upon the relocation, the area around Matsubara Ochaya was developed into {{ill|10=ja|11=神柱公園|Kanbashira Park|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|1=Kanbashira Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590365}} . The shrine's former location was in {{ill|10=ja|11=梅北町|Masutsuki, Umekita-cho|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, within the city, and {{ill|10=ja|11=黒尾神社|Kuroo Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|1=Kuroo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q107410082}} has been relocated to its site. After the war, it joined the Association of Shinto Shrines and was designated a [[Beppyo Shrine]] on July 1, 1976 [[:en:Shōwa era|.]] == Gallery == [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyouke-hime]]|Rank=旧[[県社]]・[[別表神社]]|established=1026 {{ill|September 9 (Lunisolar)|ja|9月9日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 9 (Lunisolar)|qq=}}|main hall=[[流造]]|alternative name=神柱神社|札所等=|festival={{ill|June lanterns|ja|六月灯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June lanterns|1=June lanterns|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16144215}}（{{ill|Summer festival|ja|夏祭り|1=Summer Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Summer festival|13=User:Immanuelle/Summer Matsuri|qq=}}）|map={{location map|Japan Miyazaki Prefecture|lat_deg=31|lat_min=43|lat_sec=56.1|lon_deg=131|lon_min=4|lon_sec=19.3|label=神柱宮|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|31|43|56.1|N|131|4|19.3|E}}}}|infobox shinto shrine|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|caption=Shrine}} {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮|fr|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kanbashira-gū|en:draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanbashira-gū|qq=}} '''Shrine''' is [[:en:Shinto shrine|a shrine]] located in [[:en:Miyakonojō|Miyakonojō]], [[:en:Miyazaki Prefecture|Miyazaki Prefecture]] . [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Its former Rank]] was that of [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], but it is currently [[Beppyo Shrine|a special shrine]] under [[Association of Shinto Shrines|the Association of Shinto Shrines]] {{Cite web|title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー|url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/|access-date=2023-04-02|website=jinja-gosyuin.com}}. It is also called '''Kanbashira Shrine''' . Commonly known as '''the sacred pillar''' . There are two [[:en:Setsumatsusha|sub-shrines]] within the grounds : {{nihongo|Mikado Shrine|神門神社|Mikado Jinja}} and {{nihongo|Motobashira Shrine|基柱神社|Motobashira Jinja}}. [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ごdeity｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 == deity == The main deities enshrined are [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Omikami]] and [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyoukehime Okami]], and five other deities are enshrined in the side shrine: [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]] and [[:en:Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao]] no Mikoto on the left (right side when facing the shrine) and [[:en:Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane]], [[:en:Takuhadachiji-hime|Takuhadachiji-hime]], and [[Futodama|Ame-no-Tatamikoto]] on the right (left side when facing the shrine ). Additionally, the {{nihongo|Mikado Shrine|神門神社|Mikado Jinja}}, a sub-shrine within the grounds, enshrines {{ill|10=ja|11=天石門別神|Ama-no-Iwato-wake no Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, while {{nihongo|Motobashira Shrine|基柱神社|Motobashira Jinja}} enshrines [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]] and {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} . [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ごdeity｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 == history == It is said that [[:en:Oracle|the shrine]] was founded [[:en:1026|in 1026]] when {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, who had moved from [[:en:Dazaifu (government)|Dazaifu]] to develop [[:en:Shimazu Estate|Shimazu Estate]], received a divine revelation from Amaterasu Omikami that said, "A shrine should be quickly built in this place and the name of the shrine should be called Kanbashira," and so he [[:en:Bunrei|enshrined]] a shrine in Umekitamura Masutsuki from [[Ise Grand Shrine]] . Since then, it has been revered as the guardian deity of Shimazu Manor. [http://www.kanbashira.net/yuisho/index.html ご由緒｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 In [[:en:Meiji era|May]] [[:en:1873|1873]], it was designated [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], and on October 28 of the same year, it was moved to its current location. The area between the current Ganzo-ji Temple (Maeda-cho) and the current location of Jinbashira Shrine was a field filled with many small pines, so it was called Komatsubara or Matsubara, and there was a villa of the Shimazu family of Miyakonojo called Matsubara Ochaya. Upon the relocation, the area around Matsubara Ochaya was developed into {{ill|10=ja|11=神柱公園|Kanbashira Park|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|1=Kanbashira Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590365}} . The shrine's former location was in {{ill|10=ja|11=梅北町|Masutsuki, Umekita-cho|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, within the city, and {{ill|10=ja|11=黒尾神社|Kuroo Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|1=Kuroo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q107410082}} has been relocated to its site. After the war, it joined the Association of Shinto Shrines and was designated a [[Beppyo Shrine]] on July 1, 1976 [[:en:Shōwa era|.]] == Gallery == [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyouke-hime]]|Rank=旧[[県社]]・[[別表神社]]|established=1026 {{ill|September 9 (Lunisolar)|ja|9月9日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 9 (Lunisolar)|qq=}}|main hall=[[流造]]|alternative name=神柱神社|札所等=|festival={{ill|June lanterns|ja|六月灯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June lanterns|1=June lanterns|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16144215}}（{{ill|Summer festival|ja|夏祭り|1=Summer Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Summer festival|13=User:Immanuelle/Summer Matsuri|qq=}}）|map={{location map|Japan Miyazaki Prefecture|lat_deg=31|lat_min=43|lat_sec=56.1|lon_deg=131|lon_min=4|lon_sec=19.3|label=神柱宮|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|31|43|56.1|N|131|4|19.3|E}}}}|infobox shinto shrine|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|caption=Shrine}} {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮|fr|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kanbashira-gū|en:draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanbashira-gū|qq=}} '''Shrine''' is [[:en:Shinto shrine|a shrine]] located in [[:en:Miyakonojō|Miyakonojō]], [[:en:Miyazaki Prefecture|Miyazaki Prefecture]] . [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Its former Rank]] was that of [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], but it is currently [[Beppyo Shrine|a special shrine]] under [[Association of Shinto Shrines|the Association of Shinto Shrines]] {{Cite web|title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー|url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/|access-date=2023-04-02|website=jinja-gosyuin.com}}. It is also called '''Kanbashira Shrine''' . Commonly known as '''the sacred pillar''' . There are two [[:en:Setsumatsusha|sub-shrines]] within the grounds : {{nihongo|Mikado Shrine|神門神社|Mikado Jinja}} and {{nihongo|Motobashira Shrine|基柱神社|Motobashira Jinja}}. [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ごdeity｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 == deity == The main deities enshrined are [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Omikami]] and [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyoukehime Okami]], and five other deities are enshrined in the side shrine: [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]] and [[:en:Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao]] no Mikoto on the left (right side when facing the shrine) and [[:en:Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane]], [[:en:Takuhadachiji-hime|Takuhadachiji-hime]], and [[Futodama|Ame-no-Tatamikoto]] on the right (left side when facing the shrine ). Additionally, the {{nihongo|Mikado Shrine|神門神社|Mikado Jinja}}, a sub-shrine within the grounds, enshrines {{ill|10=ja|11=天石門別神|Ama-no-Iwato-wake no Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, while {{nihongo|Motobashira Shrine|基柱神社|Motobashira Jinja}} enshrines [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]] and {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} . [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ごdeity｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 == history == It is said that [[:en:Oracle|the shrine]] was founded [[:en:1026|in 1026]] when {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, who had moved from [[:en:Dazaifu (government)|Dazaifu]] to develop [[:en:Shimazu Estate|Shimazu Estate]], received a divine revelation from Amaterasu Omikami that said, "A shrine should be quickly built in this place and the name of the shrine should be called Kanbashira," and so he [[:en:Bunrei|enshrined]] a shrine in Umekitamura Masutsuki from [[Ise Grand Shrine]] . Since then, it has been revered as the guardian deity of Shimazu Manor. [http://www.kanbashira.net/yuisho/index.html ご由緒｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 In [[:en:Meiji era|May]] [[:en:1873|1873]], it was designated [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], and on October 28 of the same year, it was moved to its current location. The area between the current Ganzo-ji Temple (Maeda-cho) and the current location of Jinbashira Shrine was a field filled with many small pines, so it was called Komatsubara or Matsubara, and there was a villa of the Shimazu family of Miyakonojo called Matsubara Ochaya. Upon the relocation, the area around Matsubara Ochaya was developed into {{ill|10=ja|11=神柱公園|Kanbashira Park|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|1=Kanbashira Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590365}} . The shrine's former location was in {{ill|10=ja|11=梅北町|Masutsuki, Umekita-cho|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, within the city, and {{ill|10=ja|11=黒尾神社|Kuroo Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|1=Kuroo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q107410082}} has been relocated to its site. After the war, it joined the Association of Shinto Shrines and was designated a [[Beppyo Shrine]] on July 1, 1976 [[:en:Shōwa era|.]] == Gallery == [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyouke-hime]]|Rank=旧[[県社]]・[[別表神社]]|established=1026 {{ill|September 9 (Lunisolar)|ja|9月9日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 9 (Lunisolar)|qq=}}|main hall=[[流造]]|alternative name=神柱神社|札所等=|festival={{ill|June lanterns|ja|六月灯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June lanterns|1=June lanterns|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16144215}}（{{ill|Summer festival|ja|夏祭り|1=Summer Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Summer festival|13=User:Immanuelle/Summer Matsuri|qq=}}）|map={{location map|Japan Miyazaki Prefecture|lat_deg=31|lat_min=43|lat_sec=56.1|lon_deg=131|lon_min=4|lon_sec=19.3|label=神柱宮|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|31|43|56.1|N|131|4|19.3|E}}}}|infobox shinto shrine|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|caption=Shrine}} {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮|fr|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kanbashira-gū|en:draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanbashira-gū|qq=}} '''Shrine''' is [[:en:Shinto shrine|a shrine]] located in [[:en:Miyakonojō|Miyakonojō]], [[:en:Miyazaki Prefecture|Miyazaki Prefecture]] . [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Its former Rank]] was that of [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], but it is currently [[Beppyo Shrine|a special shrine]] under [[Association of Shinto Shrines|the Association of Shinto Shrines]] {{Cite web|title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー|url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/|access-date=2023-04-02|website=jinja-gosyuin.com}}. It is also called '''Kanbashira Shrine''' . Commonly known as '''the sacred pillar''' . There are two [[:en:Setsumatsusha|sub-shrines]] within the grounds : {{nihongo|Mikado Shrine|神門神社|Mikado Jinja}} and {{nihongo|Motobashira Shrine|基柱神社|Motobashira Jinja}}. [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ごdeity｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 == deity == The main deities enshrined are [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Omikami]] and [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyoukehime Okami]], and five other deities are enshrined in the side shrine: [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]] and [[:en:Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao]] no Mikoto on the left (right side when facing the shrine) and [[:en:Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane]], [[:en:Takuhadachiji-hime|Takuhadachiji-hime]], and [[Futodama|Ame-no-Tatamikoto]] on the right (left side when facing the shrine ). Additionally, the {{nihongo|Mikado Shrine|神門神社|Mikado Jinja}}, a sub-shrine within the grounds, enshrines {{ill|10=ja|11=天石門別神|Ama-no-Iwato-wake no Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, while {{nihongo|Motobashira Shrine|基柱神社|Motobashira Jinja}} enshrines [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]] and {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} . [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ごdeity｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 == history == It is said that [[:en:Oracle|the shrine]] was founded [[:en:1026|in 1026]] when {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, who had moved from [[:en:Dazaifu (government)|Dazaifu]] to develop [[:en:Shimazu Estate|Shimazu Estate]], received a divine revelation from Amaterasu Omikami that said, "A shrine should be quickly built in this place and the name of the shrine should be called Kanbashira," and so he [[:en:Bunrei|enshrined]] a shrine in Umekitamura Masutsuki from [[Ise Grand Shrine]] . Since then, it has been revered as the guardian deity of Shimazu Manor. [http://www.kanbashira.net/yuisho/index.html ご由緒｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 In [[:en:Meiji era|May]] [[:en:1873|1873]], it was designated [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], and on October 28 of the same year, it was moved to its current location. The area between the current Ganzo-ji Temple (Maeda-cho) and the current location of Jinbashira Shrine was a field filled with many small pines, so it was called Komatsubara or Matsubara, and there was a villa of the Shimazu family of Miyakonojo called Matsubara Ochaya. Upon the relocation, the area around Matsubara Ochaya was developed into {{ill|10=ja|11=神柱公園|Kanbashira Park|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|1=Kanbashira Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590365}} . The shrine's former location was in {{ill|10=ja|11=梅北町|Masutsuki, Umekita-cho|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, within the city, and {{ill|10=ja|11=黒尾神社|Kuroo Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|1=Kuroo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q107410082}} has been relocated to its site. After the war, it joined the Association of Shinto Shrines and was designated a [[Beppyo Shrine]] on July 1, 1976 [[:en:Shōwa era|.]] == Gallery == [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyouke-hime]]|Rank=旧[[県社]]・[[別表神社]]|established=1026 {{ill|September 9 (Lunisolar)|ja|9月9日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 9 (Lunisolar)|qq=}}|main hall=[[流造]]|alternative name=神柱神社|札所等=|festival={{ill|June lanterns|ja|六月灯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June lanterns|1=June lanterns|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16144215}}（{{ill|Summer festival|ja|夏祭り|1=Summer Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Summer festival|13=User:Immanuelle/Summer Matsuri|qq=}}）|map={{location map|Japan Miyazaki Prefecture|lat_deg=31|lat_min=43|lat_sec=56.1|lon_deg=131|lon_min=4|lon_sec=19.3|label=神柱宮|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|31|43|56.1|N|131|4|19.3|E}}}}|infobox shinto shrine|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|caption=Shrine}} {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮|fr|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kanbashira-gū|en:draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanbashira-gū|qq=}} '''Shrine''' is [[:en:Shinto shrine|a shrine]] located in [[:en:Miyakonojō|Miyakonojō]], [[:en:Miyazaki Prefecture|Miyazaki Prefecture]] . [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Its former Rank]] was that of [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], but it is currently [[Beppyo Shrine|a special shrine]] under [[Association of Shinto Shrines|the Association of Shinto Shrines]] {{Cite web|title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー|url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/|access-date=2023-04-02|website=jinja-gosyuin.com}}. It is also called '''Kanbashira Shrine''' . Commonly known as '''the sacred pillar''' . There are two [[:en:Setsumatsusha|sub-shrines]] within the grounds : {{nihongo|Mikado Shrine|神門神社|Mikado Jinja}} and {{nihongo|Motobashira Shrine|基柱神社|Motobashira Jinja}}. [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ごdeity｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 == deity == The main deities enshrined are [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Omikami]] and [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyoukehime Okami]], and five other deities are enshrined in the side shrine: [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]] and [[:en:Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao]] no Mikoto on the left (right side when facing the shrine) and [[:en:Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane]], [[:en:Takuhadachiji-hime|Takuhadachiji-hime]], and [[Futodama|Ame-no-Tatamikoto]] on the right (left side when facing the shrine ). Additionally, the {{nihongo|Mikado Shrine|神門神社|Mikado Jinja}}, a sub-shrine within the grounds, enshrines {{ill|10=ja|11=天石門別神|Ama-no-Iwato-wake no Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, while {{nihongo|Motobashira Shrine|基柱神社|Motobashira Jinja}} enshrines [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]] and {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} . [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ごdeity｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 == history == It is said that [[:en:Oracle|the shrine]] was founded [[:en:1026|in 1026]] when {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, who had moved from [[:en:Dazaifu (government)|Dazaifu]] to develop [[:en:Shimazu Estate|Shimazu Estate]], received a divine revelation from Amaterasu Omikami that said, "A shrine should be quickly built in this place and the name of the shrine should be called Kanbashira," and so he [[:en:Bunrei|enshrined]] a shrine in Umekitamura Masutsuki from [[Ise Grand Shrine]] . Since then, it has been revered as the guardian deity of Shimazu Manor. [http://www.kanbashira.net/yuisho/index.html ご由緒｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 In [[:en:Meiji era|May]] [[:en:1873|1873]], it was designated [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], and on October 28 of the same year, it was moved to its current location. The area between the current Ganzo-ji Temple (Maeda-cho) and the current location of Jinbashira Shrine was a field filled with many small pines, so it was called Komatsubara or Matsubara, and there was a villa of the Shimazu family of Miyakonojo called Matsubara Ochaya. Upon the relocation, the area around Matsubara Ochaya was developed into {{ill|10=ja|11=神柱公園|Kanbashira Park|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|1=Kanbashira Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590365}} . The shrine's former location was in {{ill|10=ja|11=梅北町|Masutsuki, Umekita-cho|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, within the city, and {{ill|10=ja|11=黒尾神社|Kuroo Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|1=Kuroo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q107410082}} has been relocated to its site. After the war, it joined the Association of Shinto Shrines and was designated a [[Beppyo Shrine]] on July 1, 1976 [[:en:Shōwa era|.]] == Gallery == [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Its former Rank]] was that of [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], but it is currently [[Beppyo Shrine|a special shrine]] under [[Association of Shinto Shrines|the Association of Shinto Shrines]] {{Cite web|title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー|url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/|access-date=2023-04-02|website=jinja-gosyuin.com}}. It is also called '''Kanbashira Shrine''' . Commonly known as '''the sacred pillar''' . There are two {{ill|Setsumatsusha|en|Setsumatsusha|WD=Q1071160|lt=sub-shrines}} within the grounds : {{nihongo|Mikado Shrine|神門神社|Mikado Jinja}} and {{nihongo|Motobashira Shrine|基柱神社|Motobashira Jinja}}. [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ごdeity｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 == deity == The main deities enshrined are [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Omikami]] and {{ill|Toyouke-hime|en|Toyouke-hime|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyoukehime Okami}}, and five other deities are enshrined in the side shrine: [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]] and {{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|en|Ame-no-Tajikarao|WD=Q849412|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao}} no Mikoto on the left (right side when facing the shrine) and {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|en|Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane}}, {{ill|Takuhadachiji-hime|en|Takuhadachiji-hime|WD=Q11110848|lt=Takuhadachiji-hime}}, and [[Futodama|Ame-no-Tatamikoto]] on the right (left side when facing the shrine ). Additionally, the {{nihongo|Mikado Shrine|神門神社|Mikado Jinja}}, a sub-shrine within the grounds, enshrines {{ill|10=ja|11=天石門別神|Ama-no-Iwato-wake no Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, while {{nihongo|Motobashira Shrine|基柱神社|Motobashira Jinja}} enshrines {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane}} and {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} . [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ごdeity｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 == history == It is said that {{ill|Oracle|en|Oracle|WD=Q217123|lt=the shrine}} was founded {{ill|1026|en|1026|WD=Q19269|lt=in 1026}} when {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, who had moved from {{ill|Dazaifu (government)|en|Dazaifu (government)|WD=Q144845|lt=Dazaifu}} to develop {{ill|Shimazu Estate|en|Shimazu Estate|WD=Q11476819|lt=Shimazu Estate}}, received a divine revelation from Amaterasu Omikami that said, "A shrine should be quickly built in this place and the name of the shrine should be called Kanbashira," and so he {{ill|Bunrei|en|Bunrei|WD=Q195793|lt=enshrined}} a shrine in Umekitamura Masutsuki from [[Ise Grand Shrine]] . Since then, it has been revered as the guardian deity of Shimazu Manor. [http://www.kanbashira.net/yuisho/index.html ご由緒｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 In {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=May}} {{ill|1873|en|1873|WD=Q7757|lt=1873}}, it was designated [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], and on October 28 of the same year, it was moved to its current location. The area between the current Ganzo-ji Temple (Maeda-cho) and the current location of Jinbashira Shrine was a field filled with many small pines, so it was called Komatsubara or Matsubara, and there was a villa of the Shimazu family of Miyakonojo called Matsubara Ochaya. Upon the relocation, the area around Matsubara Ochaya was developed into {{ill|10=ja|11=神柱公園|Kanbashira Park|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|1=Kanbashira Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590365}} . The shrine's former location was in {{ill|10=ja|11=梅北町|Masutsuki, Umekita-cho|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, within the city, and {{ill|10=ja|11=黒尾神社|Kuroo Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|1=Kuroo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q107410082}} has been relocated to its site. After the war, it joined the Association of Shinto Shrines and was designated a [[Beppyo Shrine]] on July 1, 1976 {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=.}} == Gallery == The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Kanbashira-gū]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox shinto shrine| map_type = Japan Miyazaki Prefecture#Japan|name=神柱宮|image=Kanbashira guu Miyakonojo Shaden.jpg|location=[[:en:宮崎県|宮崎県]][[:en:都城市|都城市]]前田町1417-1|coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|31|43|56.1|N|131|4|19.3|E|display=inline,title}}|deity=[[Amaterasu]]
{{ill|Toyouke-hime|en|Toyouke-hime|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-hime}}|Rank=旧[[県社]]・[[別表神社]]|established=1026 {{ill|September 9 (Lunisolar)|ja|9月9日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 9 (Lunisolar)|qq=}}|main hall=[[流造]]|alternative name=神柱神社|札所等=|festival={{ill|June lanterns|ja|六月灯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June lanterns|1=June lanterns|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16144215}}（{{ill|Summer festival|ja|夏祭り|1=Summer Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Summer festival|13=User:Immanuelle/Summer Matsuri|qq=}}）|map={{location map|Japan Miyazaki Prefecture|lat_deg=31|lat_min=43|lat_sec=56.1|lon_deg=131|lon_min=4|lon_sec=19.3|label=神柱宮|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|31|43|56.1|N|131|4|19.3|E}}}}|infobox shinto shrine|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|caption=Shrine}} {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮|fr|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kanbashira-gū|en:draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanbashira-gū|qq=}} '''Shrine''' is {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=a shrine}} located in {{ill|Miyakonojō|en|Miyakonojō|WD=Q638301|lt=Miyakonojō}}, {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|en|Miyazaki Prefecture|WD=Q130300|lt=Miyazaki Prefecture}} . [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Its former Rank]] was that of [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], but it is currently [[Beppyo Shrine|a special shrine]] under [[Association of Shinto Shrines|the Association of Shinto Shrines]] {{Cite web|title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー|url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/|access-date=2023-04-02|website=jinja-gosyuin.com}}. It is also called '''Kanbashira Shrine''' . Commonly known as '''the sacred pillar''' . There are two {{ill|Setsumatsusha|en|Setsumatsusha|WD=Q1071160|lt=sub-shrines}} within the grounds : {{nihongo|Mikado Shrine|神門神社|Mikado Jinja}} and {{nihongo|Motobashira Shrine|基柱神社|Motobashira Jinja}}. [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ごdeity｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 == deity == The main deities enshrined are [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Omikami]] and {{ill|Toyouke-hime|en|Toyouke-hime|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyoukehime Okami}}, and five other deities are enshrined in the side shrine: [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]] and {{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|en|Ame-no-Tajikarao|WD=Q849412|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao}} no Mikoto on the left (right side when facing the shrine) and {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|en|Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane}}, {{ill|Takuhadachiji-hime|en|Takuhadachiji-hime|WD=Q11110848|lt=Takuhadachiji-hime}}, and [[Futodama|Ame-no-Tatamikoto]] on the right (left side when facing the shrine ). Additionally, the {{nihongo|Mikado Shrine|神門神社|Mikado Jinja}}, a sub-shrine within the grounds, enshrines {{ill|10=ja|11=天石門別神|Ama-no-Iwato-wake no Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, while {{nihongo|Motobashira Shrine|基柱神社|Motobashira Jinja}} enshrines {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane}} and {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} . [http://www.kanbashira.net/gosaishin/index.html ごdeity｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 == history == It is said that {{ill|Oracle|en|Oracle|WD=Q217123|lt=the shrine}} was founded {{ill|1026|en|1026|WD=Q19269|lt=in 1026}} when {{ill|10=ja|11=平季基|Taira no Suemoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, who had moved from {{ill|Dazaifu (government)|en|Dazaifu (government)|WD=Q144845|lt=Dazaifu}} to develop {{ill|Shimazu Estate|en|Shimazu Estate|WD=Q11476819|lt=Shimazu Estate}}, received a divine revelation from Amaterasu Omikami that said, "A shrine should be quickly built in this place and the name of the shrine should be called Kanbashira," and so he {{ill|Bunrei|en|Bunrei|WD=Q195793|lt=enshrined}} a shrine in Umekitamura Masutsuki from [[Ise Grand Shrine]] . Since then, it has been revered as the guardian deity of Shimazu Manor. [http://www.kanbashira.net/yuisho/index.html ご由緒｜神柱宮　宮崎県都城市の神社]2013rd year 4月15日閲覧。 In {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=May}} {{ill|1873|en|1873|WD=Q7757|lt=1873}}, it was designated [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|a prefectural shrine]], and on October 28 of the same year, it was moved to its current location. The area between the current Ganzo-ji Temple (Maeda-cho) and the current location of Jinbashira Shrine was a field filled with many small pines, so it was called Komatsubara or Matsubara, and there was a villa of the Shimazu family of Miyakonojo called Matsubara Ochaya. Upon the relocation, the area around Matsubara Ochaya was developed into {{ill|10=ja|11=神柱公園|Kanbashira Park|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|1=Kanbashira Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590365}} . The shrine's former location was in {{ill|10=ja|11=梅北町|Masutsuki, Umekita-cho|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, within the city, and {{ill|10=ja|11=黒尾神社|Kuroo Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|1=Kuroo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q107410082}} has been relocated to its site. After the war, it joined the Association of Shinto Shrines and was designated a [[Beppyo Shrine]] on July 1, 1976 {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=.}} == Gallery ==
